Description: On her wedding night, a noblewoman's husband is summoned away by the palace. Three years later, he returns with a concubine and child, forcing her to make a life-changing decision.

INT. WEDDING CHAMBER - NIGHT

A festive wedding chamber with red candles flickering.

PEI XUAN and YUN QING wear bright red wedding attire, sitting across from each other. Exquisite wine vessels are placed on the table.

PEI XUAN pours wine for YUN QING, his hands trembling slightly.

PEI XUAN
The Yongning Marquis Manor could have allied with any noble house. Yet you chose... us.

YUN QING watches him, her smile gentle but measured.

YUN QING
I chose you.

PEI XUAN meets her eyes, his voice low and sincere.

PEI XUAN
Then I will spend my life proving you right.

They raise their cups. The moment stretches—

Pounding erupts at the door.

MAID (O.S.)
Young Master! Urgent summons!

A MAID bursts in, breathless.

MAID
The Northern Border dispatch—General Pei is ordered to Ye City immediately!

PEI XUAN freezes. YUN QING's cup pauses mid-air.

PEI XUAN
(surprised)
Tonight?

MAID
The carriage waits, Young Master.

PEI XUAN turns to YUN QING. He cups her face, trying to memorize it.

PEI XUAN
Wait for me to return.

He kisses her forehead, then hurries out. YUN QING stands alone, the red silk veil slipping from her hair. She looks at the two full cups on the table, perfectly aligned.
continuation:
INT. A ROOM - NIGHT

A SERVANT kneels before a desk where a MAN writes. A small "Pei" embroidered pouch lies beside his brush.

SERVANT
Master, as per your instructions, the Young Master of the Duke of Qingguo's Manor has departed for Ye City.

The MAN's lips curl into a cold smile, his hand tightly clutching the embroidered pouch.

MAN
Three years. Let him remember what he left behind—or forget why he should have.

TITLE CARD: THREE YEARS LATER

INT. ROOM - DAY

YUN QING, in muted grey robes, reviews manor accounts by the window. Her white veil is practical, not decorative. The MAID runs in excitedly.

MAID
Madam! Madam, the Young Master has returned!

YUN QING's brush stills. Ink bleeds onto the page. She carefully sets it down.

YUN QING
Let's go. Return to the manor.

EXT. DUKE OF QINGGUO'S MANOR ENTRANCE - DAY

A dust-covered carriage lurches to a stop. SHEN MIAO YUN, holding a baby, descends. PEI XUAN follows, exhaustion etched on his face.

Two MAIDS whisper to each other in the distance.

MAID A
Did you see? The Young Master actually brought back a woman.

MAID B
I also heard a child crying. Could they have bypassed Madam and directly produced an illegitimate eldest son?

MAID A
If that's true, Madam is truly pitiful.

MAID B
She is the legitimate daughter of a Marquis Manor, yet now she's being treated and humiliated like this. How can she maintain her position in the manor from now on?

YUN QING and her MAID happen to arrive at the manor entrance and overhear their conversation.

MAID
(anxiously)
Miss, this servant doesn't believe the Young Master would cast aside his wife and remarry. Don't believe these slanderous words.

YUN QING looks at the happy trio in the distance, her eyes dimming.

YUN QING
Whether it's slander or not, we'll know once we return.

YUN QING's jaw tightens. She turns sharply—her foot catches on a loose stone.

A hand catches her elbow. XIAO LANG, in weather-stained travel robes, steadies her.

XIAO LANG
Be careful, Miss.

YUN QING steadies herself and nods slightly.

YUN QING
Thank you, sir.

MAID
Miss, we should return to the manor.

YUN QING takes one last look in PEI XUAN's direction, then resolutely follows her MAID away.

XIAO LANG stands in place, watching YUN QING's departing figure. Then he slowly turns his head, his eyes beneath the conical hat becoming cold and sharp, gazing toward PEI XUAN.

XIAO LANG (V.O.)
Pei Xuan, I gave you a chance.

ON SCREEN: TO BE CONTINUED

title: Strength Works Miracles (Episode 57)
description: A young martial artist with incredible power accidentally destroys her master's prized fish pond and must go down the mountain to earn money for compensation, only to save a CEO who tore up their arranged marriage contract.
context:
Title: Strength Works Miracles (Episode 57)
Description: A young martial artist with incredible power accidentally destroys her master's prized fish pond and must go down the mountain to earn money for compensation, only to save a CEO who tore up their arranged marriage contract.

EXT. LAKESIDE - DAY

ZHUGE DALI, a young girl in simple practice clothes, squats by the lake, jabbing her bamboo fishing pole at the water like it's a training dummy.

ZHUGE DALI
Master said one fish. That's all.

Her stomach GROWLS. She clutches it, then her eyes brighten with recognition.

ZHUGE DALI
Oh. Right.

She leaps up, assumes a stance, and executes a kata among the fallen leaves. Energy concentrates in her palms. Air currents whip around her.

EXT. TRAINING GROUND - DAY

ZHUGE IMMORTAL, elderly with white hair and beard, dozes in a hand-carved rocking chair surrounded by DISCIPLES flowing through forms.

A low RUMBLE builds. The disciples freeze. The rocking chair flips, dumping Zhuge Immortal onto the flagstones.

ZHUGE IMMORTAL
(roaring)
DALIII!

EXT. LAKESIDE - DAY

Fish flop across the grass, gasping. ZHUGE DALI scoops them into her basket, proud.

ZHUGE DALI
Efficiency. That's what Master preaches.

She looks up as ZHUGE IMMORTAL storms over with eldest disciple ZHUGE QING.

ZHUGE DALI
(excitedly)
Master! Senior Brother! Dinner's—

She trails off, seeing their faces.

ZHUGE IMMORTAL
(staring at the empty pond, voice cracking)
My spirit-gathering fish. Twenty years of alchemical feed. Gone.

ZHUGE QING
Junior Sister, this pond cost Master everything.

ZHUGE IMMORTAL
(pointing a trembling finger)
Eighty-eight million, eight hundred eighty-eight thousand, eight hundred eighty-eight yuan and eighty-eight cents. Every fen.

ZHUGE DALI
(nervous now)
That's... very exact.

ZHUGE IMMORTAL
Off the mountain. Don't return without it.

ZHUGE DALI
But Master, my cousin Pei Weiwei's wedding—

She fumbles out a red invitation.

ZHUGE DALI
She's marrying into the Fu family. Cloud City's richest. I could—

ZHUGE IMMORTAL
Then go! But you pay every fen yourself.

ZHUGE DALI
(quiet, serious)
Fine. I'll earn it.

She shoulders her bundle and leaves without looking back.
continuation:
EXT. ZHUGE VILLAGE ENTRANCE - DAY

ASSISTANT SHEN in a suit argues with a VILLAGER.

VILLAGER
Zhuge Village doesn't welcome outsiders.

FU YUCHENG, handsome and cold, steps from a black luxury car.

ASSISTANT SHEN
Young Master, Zhuge Immortal refuses to see you. Must you personally reject this marriage contract?

FU YUCHENG
Give it to me.

Assistant Shen hands over a red marriage scroll. The paper shimmers faintly. Fu Yucheng's fingers hesitate a fraction—then he tears it. Thunder CRACKS. The air DISTORTS.

ASSISTANT SHEN
(terrified)
The contract was sealed by blood oath!

FU YUCHENG
Superstition.

He reaches for the car door. His hand sticks. The engine ROARS to life.

FU YUCHENG
What—

The car lurches forward, dragging him. He fights to keep his feet.

ASSISTANT SHEN
(chasing)
Young Master! The cliff!

FU YUCHENG
(shouting)
I can't let go! The handle—

The car speeds toward the edge. Fu Yucheng hangs on, desperate.

ZHUGE DALI, leaving the village, sees this. She launches into the air like a projectile.

She slams into Fu Yucheng, the impact ripping him free. They tumble across the dirt as the car sails over the cliff behind them.

They roll to a stop. Dali lands hard on her back, winded. Fu Yucheng stares up at the sky, stunned.

ASSISTANT SHEN
(catching up)
Young Master!

FU YUCHENG
(sitting up, looking at his freed hand, then at Dali)
Who—

ZHUGE DALI
(interrupting, sitting up painfully)
You're the Fu family heir, right?

She pulls out the torn invitation.

ZHUGE DALI
Your fiancée's cousin. I'm here to collect a debt.

title: The Torn Notice
description: An elder brother sacrifices everything to support his siblings, but jealousy leads to betrayal when university acceptance letters arrive.
context:
Title: The Torn Notice
Description: An elder brother sacrifices everything to support his siblings, but jealousy leads to betrayal when university acceptance letters arrive.

INT. CLINIC - DAY

A DOCTOR examines LI YANG's swollen, callused hand. X-ray films glow on the desk.

DOCTOR
The inflammation is severe. Those tingling sensations you've been ignoring? That's nerve damage. You need proper treatment at the county hospital, or this could become permanent.

LI YANG
(quietly)
How much?

DOCTOR
At least three thousand for the procedure. More for medication.

LI YANG
(staring at his hand)
A box of patches is twenty yuan. I'll take four.

DOCTOR
You can't just—

LI YANG
I'm fine, Doctor. Really.

The DOCTOR reluctantly hands over the patches, watching LI YANG tuck them into his worn jacket. As LI YANG exits, the DOCTOR turns to the NURSE.

DOCTOR
(quietly)
That's the boy who supports his whole family. Works three jobs, they say. Barely twenty.

NURSE
And still found time to take the exams?

The DOCTOR nods grimly.

EXT. STREET - DAY

LI YANG walks, flexing his fingers experimentally. He stops at a notice board, where a local university's scholarship poster catches his eye. He touches it briefly, then keeps walking.
continuation:
EXT. CONSTRUCTION SITE - DAY

LI YANG and LI SIYU haul cement bags. LI YUE sits on a crate, pretending to massage his ankle.

LI SIYU
(gasping)
Big brother, your face is pale.

LI YANG
Just dehydrated. Pass me that thermos.

He drains it, his hands trembling slightly. LI YUE watches, a peculiar expression on his face.

LI YUE
(casual)
Hey, I need to check on that job application. Be back in an hour.

LI YANG
You mean the one you already got rejected from?

LI YUE
(defensive)
They said they'd reconsider.

LI YANG says nothing, just nods. LI YUE slinks away.

EXT. ALLEY BEHIND SITE - DAY

LI YUE leans against a wall, scrolling through his phone. A slip of paper is visible in his pocket—an exam results preview. He stares at it, knuckles white.

LI YUE
(quietly)
Eight points. Eight lousy points below him.

A VILLAGE WOMAN hurries past, spots him.

VILLAGE WOMAN
Li Yue! Registered mail at the office. Your whole family's names are on it. I saw Li Yang's too—big university seal!

LI YUE
(frozen)
Both? Together?

VILLAGE WOMAN
Better hurry. They're closing in ten minutes.

She leaves. LI YUE stares at nothing, then begins walking—not toward the office, but away.

EXT. UNDER THE BRIDGE - DAY

LI YUE sits beneath the concrete, both acceptance letters unopened on his lap. His fingers trace LI YANG's name on the thick envelope. He opens his own first—a modest provincial college. He opens Li Yang's—a top-tier university with a full scholarship note visible.

LI YUE
(whispering)
Full ride. Of course. And mine...

His hands shake. He pulls out a worn photograph—three smiling kids in threadbare clothes. His thumb grazes LI YANG's youthful face.

INT. KITCHEN - FLASHBACK - NIGHT

LI YANG stirs congee, tired. LI SIYU (younger) and LI YUE (youngest) sit at the table.

LI SIYU
But you scored highest in your practice tests. You should be the one to—

LI YANG
(cutting her off)
Yue's English is weak. He needs more prep time. I'll work.

LI YUE
(from doorway)
I don't need your charity.

LI YANG
It's not charity. It's family.

BACK TO:

EXT. UNDER THE BRIDGE - DAY

LI YUE stares at the letters, tears streaming down his face. He begins ripping LI YANG's letter—slowly at first, then violently. The scholarship notice flutters down: 50,000 yuan stipend.

LI YUE
(choking)
You wouldn't even need this. You'd find a way. You always do.

LI YANG and LI SIYU's voices echo in the distance.

LI YANG (O.S.)
Ah Yue! The foreman's paying out early!

LI YUE scrambles, stuffing the pieces into his pocket. He stands, wiping his face, forcing a smile.

CLOSE-UP on his pocket. The torn edge of LI YANG's name is visible, stained with tears.

ON SCREEN: TO BE CONTINUED

title: Wedding Night
description: On her mother's remarriage night, a woman discovers her new stepfather dead, uncovering dark secrets about her mother's past.
context:
Title: Wedding Night
Description: On her mother's remarriage night, a woman discovers her new stepfather dead, uncovering dark secrets about her mother's past.

INT. BEDROOM - NIGHT

JIANG YA lies asleep in bed, her brow slightly furrowed.

JIANG YA (V.O.)
My mother wore red tonight. She reserves that color for special occasions. The last time she wore it, my father died.

SFX: Distant firecrackers. Thunder.

WEN FUYAO (O.S.)
Chaoyang!

JIANG YA's eyes snap open.

WEN FUYAO (O.S.)
(panicked)
Chaoyang, please—

JIANG YA sits up, looking confused and tense.

JIANG YA
Mom?

WEN FUYAO (O.S.)
(urgent)
Chaoyang, look at me! Chaoyang!

JIANG YA immediately gets out of bed, hurriedly puts on slippers, and runs out.
continuation:
INT. JIANG CHAOYANG'S BEDROOM - NIGHT

The room is crowded with wedding guests, the atmosphere heavy. WEN FUYAO wears a bright red qipao, standing to the side with a grief-stricken expression.

JIANG YA is pushed through the crowd to the bedside.

On the bed, her stepfather JIANG CHAOYANG lies with eyes closed, lifeless.

JIANG YA
(crying)
Dad! Dad! Dad, what's wrong? It's Xiao Ya! Dad!

CROWD
The doctor is here! Make way, make way!

An elderly man in a long robe, carrying a medicine box, pushes through the crowd.

The OLD DOCTOR approaches the bed and leans in, sniffing the air.

OLD DOCTOR
(to himself)
This smell...

JIANG YA
(urgently)
Doctor, please check on him! What's happened?

The OLD DOCTOR sets down his medicine box, sits on the edge of the bed, and takes Chaoyang's pulse. His expression is serious as he carefully examines him.

After a moment, the OLD DOCTOR stands and shakes his head.

OLD DOCTOR
Medicine cannot help him now. The family should prepare.

JIANG YA
(shocked)
No! That's impossible!

JIANG YA falls to her knees before the OLD DOCTOR.

JIANG YA
(pleading)
Doctor, I'm begging you, please check him again! He was just dancing at the wedding!

The OLD DOCTOR turns to look at WEN FUYAO.

OLD DOCTOR
Fuyao. The wedding wine. Did he drink more than usual?

WEN FUYAO
(eyes evasive)
We were celebrating. Then he said he needed to rest...

JIANG YA stands up, looking at her mother in disbelief.

JIANG YA
You're lying. I saw his cup. You kept filling it.

SFX: Flashback of wine pouring into a cup—overspilling.

WEN FUYAO's face changes, a flash of panic crossing her eyes.

title: The Great Magician
description: A legendary Peking Opera martial role actor refuses to perform for Japanese invaders and pays the ultimate price, leaving his son to carry on his legacy.
context:
Title: The Great Magician
Description: A legendary Peking Opera martial role actor refuses to perform for Japanese invaders and pays the ultimate price, leaving his son to carry on his legacy.

INT. GUAN FAMILY TROUPE THEATER - NIGHT

A plaque reads "Guan Family Troupe."

The sound of Peking Opera drums and gongs is intense.

GUAN HAITIAN, dressed in full martial role costume, fights fiercely against multiple attackers inside the theater. He is skilled, but outnumbered, his hands already stained with blood.

After a brutal battle, GUAN HAITIAN defeats all his enemies and stands alone in the center of the devastated theater, breathing heavily.

INT. GUAN FAMILY TROUPE BACKSTAGE - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)

GUAN HAITIAN speaks to a large wooden crate. His young son GUAN YILONG is hidden inside.

GUAN HAITIAN
Your grandfather taught me the martial role carries heroes' weight. Tonight I bear it alone. Tomorrow, you must.
(quietly)
When they offer safety for your art... remember this crate. Promise me.

GUAN YILONG
Father, I promise!

GUAN HAITIAN closes the lid. GUAN YILONG peers out through the cracks in terror.

INT. GUAN FAMILY TROUPE THEATER - NIGHT

YU KUI enters with a group of men carrying a plaque that reads "Master of Martial Roles."

GUAN HAITIAN
You're returning it to the wrong theater, Yu Kui.

YU KUI
The art belongs to whoever keeps it alive, Haitian. Not to a building.

A man in Japanese clothing, YAMAMOTO TAKESHI, steps forward.

YAMAMOTO TAKESHI
Your "Zhao Yun at Changban" was definitive. I would consider it a personal honor.

GUAN HAITIAN
I would rather die backstage than sing for you.

YU KUI
The art dies with you!

GUAN HAITIAN
The art died when you started translating libretti for them.
continuation:
GUAN HAITIAN shouts and launches an attack. Both sides engage in fierce combat again.

Though brave, GUAN HAITIAN is ultimately outnumbered. He is stabbed multiple times and falls to the ground. GUAN YILONG witnesses everything through the cracks in the crate, covering his mouth in terror, tears streaming down his face.

YU KUI
(quietly)
Just say yes.

GUAN HAITIAN
(grinning through blood)
Listen close...

GUAN HAITIAN uses his last strength to stab YAMAMOTO TAKESHI in the face with the pheasant feather from his headdress.

YAMAMOTO TAKESHI
(covering his face, screaming)

YAMAMOTO TAKESHI, enraged and humiliated, draws his sword and kills GUAN HAITIAN.

YAMAMOTO TAKESHI
(to YU KUI)
The plaque is yours. But you will always be the man who let this happen.

YU KUI clutches the plaque, his expression hollow.

EXT. STREET CORNER - NIGHT

GUAN YILONG has become a young beggar. He finds some leftover food and tries to feed it to a stray dog named XIAO HEI.

GUAN YILONG
Xiao Hei, Xiao Hei.

GUAN YILONG is starving. He grabs the leftover food from the ground and stuffs it into his mouth, his hands covered in dirt and blood.

A cleanly dressed young girl, YE MULAN, approaches and hands him a steamed bun.

YE MULAN
My grandfather says you have a performer's hands.

GUAN YILONG looks at her warily.

YE MULAN
Eat. The body forgets, but the hands remember.

GUAN YILONG takes the bun and devours it hungrily.

MASTER YE approaches and looks at GUAN YILONG.

MASTER YE
Would you come with us to the Ye Family Troupe? We have a crate in our storeroom.

GUAN YILONG looks up at them, eyes brimming with tears.

title: All Scumbags Are Equally Dark (Episode 42)
description: A pregnant woman braves a stormy night to beg for money so her husband can study abroad, selling everything her family owns and desperately seeking help from neighbors and the village chief.
context:
Title: All Scumbags Are Equally Dark (Episode 42)
Description: A pregnant woman braves a stormy night to beg for money so her husband can study abroad, selling everything her family owns and desperately seeking help from neighbors and the village chief.

EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - NIGHT

A stormy night with thunder and lightning. On a muddy path, pregnant LING HUA (late 20s, determined eyes, using a yellow fertilizer bag as shield) struggles forward with a flashlight. She stops, steadies herself against a tree, one hand on her belly, then pushes on with renewed purpose.

INT. CLINIC - NIGHT

LING HUA, soaking wet, stands before DR. LI (60s, stern but tired). She places her worn work permit on the counter.

LING HUA
Doctor, please. Just a week's advance. I'll work double shifts after—

DR. LI
(interrupting, firm)
You're not on staff. And in your condition?

DR. LI pushes the permit back.

DR. LI
Go home. Think of your child.

Ling Hua's jaw tightens. She pockets the permit, nods once, and turns to leave.
continuation:
EXT. FARMHOUSE YARD - NIGHT

LING HUA stumbles out of the clinic porch, leans against the wall. She dials a number—no answer. Stares at her phone: a photo of HONG YU in graduation robes. She whispers.

LING HUA
(barely audible)
You promised.

She wipes her face, straightens, and moves down the road to a wooden fence.

LING HUA
Auntie Wang. I'll work your fields—three months, no pay. Just the advance.

AUNTIE WANG (O.S.)
(cold, through window)
My crops failed. You've got nothing to offer.

The window slams. Ling Hua grips the fence, then releases it deliberately. She doesn't crumble—she calculates.

EXT. VILLAGE CHIEF'S HOUSE - NIGHT

LING HUA kneels on the steps, but her posture has changed—exhausted yet unbroken. She speaks clearly.

LING HUA
Village Chief. The scholarship deposit is due tomorrow. My father pawned his wheelchair. I've sold every hen. I need five thousand more. I'll sign any contract.

The VILLAGE CHIEF (50s, weathered) opens the door. He studies her, then pulls out an envelope.

VILLAGE CHIEF
Your husband called yesterday. Asked for the same amount. Said it was for your hospital fees.

Beat. Ling Hua's face goes still. The rain pounds.

VILLAGE CHIEF
(offering envelope)
This is what he didn't take. Three thousand. It's yours.

Ling Hua takes it slowly, her expression unreadable.

VILLAGE CHIEF
(quiet warning)
Whatever he's chasing... make sure he brings something back worth keeping.

LING HUA
(folds money into her bag, voice firm)
Thank you, Chief.

She rises on her own, turns, and walks back into the storm—no longer hunched, but walking tall, one hand on her belly, the other gripping the bag. The flashlight beam ahead of her is steady.

The Village Chief watches, then pulls out his phone, dialing.

VILLAGE CHIEF
(into phone)
Hong Yu? She just left. She knows.

He hangs up, looking grim.

FADE OUT.

title: The Little Immortal Chef
description: A mischievous immortal girl who inherited the God of Cooking's divine position descends to the mortal world to find her father and fulfill her destiny.
context:
Title: The Little Immortal Chef
Description: A mischievous immortal girl who inherited the God of Cooking's divine position descends to the mortal world to find her father and fulfill her destiny.

INT. LAKESIDE - DAY

SUPER: IMMORTAL REALM

A little girl in flowing silk robes, YUANYUAN (8), stirs from sleep atop a jade-colored boulder. She stretches, her nose already twitching.

YUANYUAN
(to herself)
Nothing but ambrosia for three hundred years... I'm starving for something real.

She springs up, eyes sparkling with inspiration.

YUANYUAN
Time for something with actual flavor!

Above the misty lake, two divine beasts circle—GOLDEN DRAGON and FIRE PHOENIX—playfully chasing each other through clouds.

MALE VOICE A (V.O.)
The little ancestor's awake! Hide the divine beasts!

MALE VOICE B (V.O.)
Too late—she's already spotted them!

Yuanyuan stands on a floating wooden pier, hands on hips, her laughter like silver bells.

YUANYUAN
Dragon and Phoenix Hot Pot! Perfect!

She whips out a tiny slingshot carved from peach wood. As she aims, the ammunition glows—essence orbs, not stones. She fires once, twice. The divine beasts shimmer and TRANSFORM mid-air into their base spiritual forms: a chicken and a fish, which plop into Yuanyuan's waiting basket.

Yuanyuan catches them effortlessly, already setting up her cooking pot.

INT. COURTYARD - DAY

XUANTIAN LAOZU, an ancient immortal whose white beard trails to his knees, shuffles through the courtyard in panic.

XUANTIAN LAOZU
(calling out)
Long'er! Feng'er! Where have those rascals gotten to now?

His nose catches an irresistible aroma. He follows it like a man possessed.

He finds Yuanyuan tending her pot, the broth simmering with heavenly fragrance.

XUANTIAN LAOZU
(leaning close to the pot)
Ah, Yuanyuan... that aroma could wake the dead. Mortal fire needs mortal fuel, yes? Let this old one stoke it for you.

XUANTIAN LAOZU casts a spell, and the flames leap up, singeing his sleeve. He doesn't notice.

YUANYUAN
(without looking up)
You just want first taste.

XUANTIAN LAOZU
(rubbing his hands together)
A mere sample. For quality assurance.

YUANYUAN
(pointing her spoon)
One spoonful. That's it.

XUANTIAN LAOZU
(beaming)
You're a jewel among immortals!

XUANTIAN LAOZU plunges his hand into the pot—time and pain mean nothing to him—and extracts a morsel.

YUANYUAN
(disgusted)
At least use a spoon. We're civilized beings.

XUANTIAN LAOZU
(mouth full, eyes wide)
Divine! What is this? I've tasted every beast in the nine heavens, but this...

YUANYUAN
(casually)
Dragon and phoenix, of course.

XUANTIAN LAOZU
(shocked)
MY Dragon? MY Phoenix? The ones I've raised since they were eggs?

YUANYUAN
They looked mature enough to harvest.

XUANTIAN LAOZU
(grief-stricken)
My beautiful mounts! My companions of a thousand years!

XUANTIAN LAOZU collapses on the ground, beating his chest.

XUANTIAN LAOZU
Little ancestor, please show mercy! The divine beasts on this Spirit Mountain are almost all eaten by you.

NINE HEAVENS SAINT, a fairy in white robes, walks over.

NINE HEAVENS SAINT
Yuanyuan. You've consumed half the spiritual guardians of this mountain.

YUANYUAN
continuation:
(pouting)
It's not my fault. The God of Cooking's divine position requires actual cooking. You can't make an omelet without breaking a few eggs. Or roasting a few dragons.

XUANTIAN LAOZU
(standing up)
She has a point. It's her nature.

NINE HEAVENS SAINT
Since Yuanyuan loves cooking so much, why don't you go to the mortal world to find your father?

YUANYUAN
(delighted)
Father? I have a father?

NINE HEAVENS SAINT
Every immortal has an origin.

NINE HEAVENS SAINT casts a spell, and an image shimmers in the air—a MAN in a modern chef's coat, his face kind, his hands skilled.

YUANYUAN
(whispering)
He looks... kind. And his hands know work.

NINE HEAVENS SAINT
It was a winter's night when your mortal mother saved his life.

FLASHBACK: A young woman tends to an injured man in a mountain inn.

MAN
I thought I'd die alone on that cliff.

WOMAN
Then it's fortunate I was hunting for rare herbs.

END FLASHBACK.

NINE HEAVENS SAINT
(noticing YUANYUAN has disappeared)
Hmph, this little imp—

XUANTIAN LAOZU watches as Yuanyuan's life statue in the courtyard—the measure of her divine power—glows, then hardens into ordinary stone.

XUANTIAN LAOZU
(sadly)
She's sealed her immortal power. In the mortal world, she'll be just a gifted child. Until she earns her place.

NINE HEAVENS SAINT
Let us hope gifted is enough.

INT. HOTEL KITCHEN - DAY

The kitchen is bustling. Head Chef ZHANG (40s) towers over LIN YANG (28), a talented sous chef.

CHEF ZHANG
Lin Yang! That prep work should've been done ten minutes ago! This is a kitchen, not a meditation hall!

LIN YANG
Chef Zhang, I'm doing the work of three people here. I can't—

A golden light flashes, and YUANYUAN appears, her ancient robes ludicrously out of place.

She spots Lin Yang's hands—the way they move, the precision in his knife work. Her eyes widen. It's the same hands from the vision.

YUANYUAN
(voice trembling)
Daddy?

Yuanyuan runs to Lin Yang and hugs his leg fiercely.

LIN YANG
(surprised)
Whoa! Hey, little girl. I'm not—

CHEF ZHANG
(exploding)
You bring a KID into my kitchen? Lin Yang, if you don't want to work, get out!

LIN YANG
No, she's not my child.

CHEF ZHANG
If she's not your child, is she mine?

YUANYUAN
(shouting at Chef Zhang)
Don't you bully my daddy!

CHEF ZHANG
So what if I bully him?

YUANYUAN
(pointing at Chef Zhang)
You just wait!

CHEF ZHANG
I'm waiting! Go away!

YUANYUAN walks to the cutting board, picks up a piece of carrot and a cleaver. In a BLUR OF MOTION, she reduces it to perfect julienne. The kitchen falls silent.

LIN YANG and CHEF ZHANG stare, mouths agape.

Yuanyuan looks up, her face fierce with pride and vulnerability.

YUANYUAN
(to Lin Yang)
I can help. Just... don't send me away.

title: The Genius Painter is Not to Be Trifled With
description: A talented young painter is reborn after being betrayed by her junior sister who stole her masterpiece and claimed it as her own, leading to her downfall and death.
context:
Title: The Genius Painter is Not to Be Trifled With
Description: A talented young painter is reborn after being betrayed by her junior sister who stole her masterpiece and claimed it as her own, leading to her downfall and death.

EXT. ROOFTOP - NIGHT

AN YIRUO, a young woman in a white dress, sits with her back to the camera in front of a blank easel. In the distance, a brightly lit cross-sea bridge spans the horizon.

ON SCREEN: AN YIRUO - A genius young woman with exceptional painting skills

AN YIRUO suddenly wakes with a start, her expression filled with terror.

AN YIRUO (V.O.)
I've been reborn. But the canvas is still empty.

AN YIRUO looks at her hands as memories flood back.

AN YIRUO (V.O.)
Seven days and nights. Every brushstroke, a piece of my soul. She took it while I blinked.

FLASHBACK:

EXT. STREET - DAY

AN YIRUO, wearing period costume, runs through the rain and gets into a taxi.

A large truck collides with the taxi.

AN YIRUO, blood covering her face, loses consciousness inside the car.

AN YIRUO (V.O.)
The rain was so loud. I never got to say goodbye.
continuation:
INT. AWARD CEREMONY - DAY

SU WANXIAO, a young woman with exquisite makeup, smiles on stage holding up a first-place certificate and trophy. The audience applauds below.

AN YIRUO (V.O.)
While she held my trophy, they applauded my downfall.

END FLASHBACK.

INT. AWARD CEREMONY - DAY

The HOST stands next to SU WANXIAO, holding a microphone to interview her.

HOST
Miss Su, as Master An's final disciple, you've won the championship at such a young age. Truly remarkable. By the way, why didn't your senior sister An Yiruo come to compete?

SU WANXIAO
(smiling)
My senior sister... the genius painter...

SU WANXIAO suddenly leans closer to the microphone, her expression becoming subtle.

SU WANXIAO
(in a whisper)
I hope she finds her own inspiration someday.

In the audience seats below, a MAN and WOMAN are discussing.

MALE AUDIENCE MEMBER
That's the third time she's hinted at it. Poor Master An's daughter...

FEMALE AUDIENCE MEMBER
Where there's smoke...

EXT. ROOFTOP - NIGHT

AN YIRUO stands before the easel, her expression filled with pain and unwillingness.

AN YIRUO (V.O.)
The contempt, the disdain... that honor was mine to claim.

AN YIRUO turns around, her eyes becoming determined.

AN YIRUO (V.O.)
This time, I'll paint a truth she can't steal.

INT. STUDIO - DAY

AN YIRUO is painting in front of an easel in her studio. Her phone rings, the caller ID shows "AN HONGSHAN."

She picks up the phone to answer.

AN HONGSHAN (V.O.)
Your mother understands if you withdraw. She's making your favorite soup.

A beat.

AN HONGSHAN (V.O.)
(coldly)
Xiaoxiao's work is already in the gallery. Don't make this harder than it needs to be.

AN YIRUO listens to the phone, her expression firm, her eyes revealing confidence. She ends the call, picks up her brush.

title: Belated Judgment
description: A lawyer's daughter, a bar waitress, is assaulted by a powerful young man. When the court dismisses her case amid public humiliation, her father must choose between compromise and conscience as his daughter's life hangs in the balance.
context:
Title: Belated Judgment
Description: A lawyer's daughter, a bar waitress, is assaulted by a powerful young man. When the court dismisses her case amid public humiliation, her father must choose between compromise and conscience as his daughter's life hangs in the balance.

INT. COURTROOM - DAY

Through the high windows, a distant figure in white balances on a rooftop edge.

CHEN XIAOYA (20s, bar waitress) sits motionless at the plaintiff's table, her white dress a stark contrast to the dark wood.

At the defense table, SHEN HAO (20s, real estate heir) signs documents with a gold pen.

PRESIDING JUDGE
The charge of rape against Shen Hao is dismissed. Evidence of forcible assault remains inconclusive.

Shen Hao's lawyer hands him a phone. He glances at the screen and smiles.

PRESIDING JUDGE
The plaintiff is hereby notified: the defendant's family has filed civil suits for defamation and extortion. Surrender your passport pending the first hearing.

The gavel strikes. Gallery murmurs swell into audible contempt.

SHEN HAO
(quietly, to Chen Jianjun)
She should have taken the offer.

CHEN JIANJUN
(slamming the table)
She was unconscious. The medical report showed--

SHEN HAO'S LAWYER
Voluntary alcohol consumption. Nothing more.

Shen Hao turns to leave. A COURT OFFICER approaches Xiaoya.

COURT OFFICER
Miss Chen, I'll need your identification.

GALLERY VOICES rise in judgment.

SPECTATOR 1
Bar girls like her always have a price.

SPECTATOR 2
These girls see a rich kid and think it's their lottery ticket.

CHEN XIAOYA
(standing, voice shaking)
I sold drinks. Not myself.

SHEN HAO
(at the door)
Your resume suggests otherwise.

CHEN JIANJUN
(blocking his path)
My daughter worked to pay my medical bills. You think that gives you the right--

SHEN HAO
I think it makes her desperate. And desperation is negotiable.

He exits. The gallery erupts.

SPECTATOR 3
He offered settlement, didn't he? She just wanted more.

SPECTATOR 4
Probably has a dozen sugar daddies.

CHEN JIANJUN
(to the room)
She's twenty-two. She poured whiskey for tourists. Does that make her a prostitute?

SPECTATOR 5
In my day, decent girls became teachers.

SPECTATOR 6
She's got a lawyer for a father and still can't win. Must be guilty.

CHEN XIAOYA
(quietly, to her father)
You fought so hard. Every hearing. Every deposition.

CHEN JIANJUN
Because you're innocent--

CHEN XIAOYA
Then why did I see you take his money?

FLASHBACK:
continuation:
INT. LAW OFFICE - NIGHT (ONE WEEK AGO)

Shen Hao slides a bank card across Chen Jianjun's desk.

SHEN HAO
Surgery's expensive. This covers it. Advise your client that memory can be... uncertain.

Through the door's crack, Chen Xiaoya watches her father's hand hesitate above the card.

BACK TO:

INT. COURTROOM - CONTINUOUS

CHEN XIAOYA
You let me believe. You let me testify.

CHEN JIANJUN
Xiaoya, I returned it. That same night--

CHEN XIAOYA
But you considered it. For three minutes, you considered it.

She walks toward the exit. Spectators' final barbs follow.

SPECTATOR 7
Like father, like daughter.

SPECTATOR 8
Two hundred thousand yuan buys a lot of silence.

CHEN JIANJUN
(to himself)
Not silence. Never silence.

He looks at Shen Hao's empty chair.

EXT. COURTHOUSE ENTRANCE - DAY

Chen Jianjun pushes through REPORTERS.

REPORTER 1
Lawyer Chen, did you advise your daughter to refuse settlement?

REPORTER 2
Your daughter worked at three bars in six months. Comment?

SHEN HAO
(emerging behind him)
Miss Chen was desperate to save her father. We understand.

REPORTER 3
Will you drop the civil suit, Mr. Shen?

SHEN HAO
If they apologize publicly, perhaps. I'm not cruel.

CHEN JIANJUN
You drugged her.

The crowd freezes. Shen Hao's smile tightens.

SHEN HAO
Allegations require proof. Your expert witness couldn't confirm it.

Chen Jianjun's phone BUZZES. He reads a text: "You promised."

A second message appears: a photo of white fabric against concrete.

CHEN JIANJUN
(to Reporter 3)
Which building is in that stream?

REPORTER 3
(looking at phone)
Financial district tower. Why?

Chen Jianjun sprints down the steps. Shen Hao watches, then taps his phone.

SHEN HAO
(into phone)
Make sure she doesn't jump before she recants.

He pockets the phone. In the distance, Chen Jianjun's figure grows smaller.

SHEN HAO
(to no one)
Desperation is so predictable.

title: Heaven's Favored Son
description: A young celestial being, favored by the Heavenly Dao itself, accidentally falls to the mortal realm and awakens in a bathhouse to find his mortal family in turmoil.
context:
Title: Heaven's Favored Son
Description: A young celestial being, favored by the Heavenly Dao itself, accidentally falls to the mortal realm and awakens in a bathhouse to find his mortal family in turmoil.

EXT. CELESTIAL REALM - DAY

A vast sea of clouds roils beneath towering jade pillars. Golden light fractures through mist.

LI WANWAN (8), a small boy in flowing celestial robes, balances atop a pillar's tip, eyes closed, a simple wooden fan resting on his knees. Around him, the air shimmers with suppressed power.

Four divine figures materialize from golden light: TAISHANG LAOJUN with his longevity staff, ERLANG ZHENJUN with his third eye gleaming, DAOXING TIANZUN bearing a jade tablet, and WENQU XINGJUN holding a calligraphy brush.

LI WANWAN's eyes snap open. He doesn't turn.

LI WANWAN
You're late.

The four deities flinch collectively. DAOXING TIANZUN's jade tablet cracks in his grip.

DAOXING TIANZUN
Forgive us, Little Master. The Eastern Star needed realigning—

LI WANWAN
(fanning himself lazily)
I said I'd practice my new technique on you today.

He flicks the fan. A single, delicate petal of golden flame drifts toward them.

The four deities VANISH in a panic, reappearing a hundred yards away—except WENQU XINGJUN, whose beard singes.

WENQU XINGJUN
(inspecting his beard)
Seventeen millennia of cultivation... singed by a child with a fan.

LI WANWAN pouts. He hops down, misjudges the distance—

LI WANWAN
Ouch!

—and vanishes into the clouds. His fan spirals upward. A final golden spark flickers, then dies.

INT. MORTAL BATHHOUSE - DAY

Steam hangs thick. Tiled walls drip with condensation.

LI WANWAN lies on a massage bed, now in a faded sweater and patched jeans. He jolts awake, clutching his head. A wooden fan—identical to the celestial one—sits clutched in his hand.

MAN A (O.S.)
Old Liu's luck turned yesterday. Won three months' wages at cards.

MAN B (O.S.)
Take me next time. My daughter needs school fees.

LI WANWAN stares at the fan. It feels wrong—heavy, powerless. He touches his chest, feeling a strange hollowness.

LI WANWAN
(whispering)
Where... am I?

A FEMALE ATTENDANT ushers out two customers, her smile tired but warm.

FEMALE ATTENDANT
Come again. We'll have hot water waiting.

ZHOU XIUFANG (30s), calloused hands and quiet eyes, approaches with a steaming towel. She freezes seeing LI WANWAN awake.

ZHOU XIUFANG
Wanwan?

She kneels beside him, pressing the warm towel to his forehead. Her fingers tremble slightly.

ZHOU XIUFANG
(softly)
You fell asleep in the changing room again. Here—

She produces a bamboo basket covered with a red cloth. Inside, three eggs nestle like treasures.
continuation:
ZHOU XIUFANG
Your teacher said you fainted at school. I saved these from our hens. Eat one. Please.

LI WANWAN looks at her. A memory surfaces: this same face, younger, offering him a candied hawthorn in winter. The memory feels like his own and borrowed at once.

LI WANWAN (V.O.)
Mother.

ZHOU XIUFANG's smile fades as laughter erupts at the entrance. LI ZHENDONG (40s), glasses crooked and tie loosened, stumbles in with ZHAO MENGJIE (20s), her designer bag clashing with the humble setting.

LI ZHENDONG
(shouting)
Boss! Couple's room. Now!

ZHAO MENGJIE
(adjusting his tie)
We don't need service, darling. Just privacy.

ZHOU XIUFANG stands, smoothing her apron. Her back straightens with quiet dignity.

ZHOU XIUFANG
I'm the attendant today. Private room's twenty yuan.

She meets LI ZHENDONG's eyes. Recognition hits him like a slap.

LI ZHENDONG
...Xiufang? What're you— You work here?

He looks from her to LI WANWAN. Comprehension dawns, then twists into something ugly.

LI ZHENDONG
(low, dangerous)
You're hiding my son in this... place? While I'm paying your rent?

LI WANWAN stands, the wooden fan gripped tight. Another memory: this man, younger, taking the hawthorn, laughing. Not cruel—just careless.

LI WANWAN (V.O.)
Father.

LI ZHENDONG
(pointing at LI WANWAN)
He's mine. I'm taking him.

ZHOU XIUFANG
(surprisingly firm)
 courts gave him to me. You signed the papers.

LI ZHENDONG laughs, a broken sound.

LI ZHENDONG
Courts? I owe more than I own. Mengjie's family needs an heir. A clean one.

He grabs LI WANWAN's wrist. The boy's eyes flash—not with celestial power, but with mortal fury.

LI WANWAN
Let go.

Something in his voice makes LI ZHENDONG pause. The lights flicker. The steam swirls.

LI WANWAN steps between his parents, facing his father. He looks small, but his shadow stretches wrong—tall, winged, impossibly large.

LI ZHENDONG stares at the shadow, then at his son's eyes. He sees something ancient and terrible looking back.

LI ZHENDONG
(releasing him, voice shaking)
What... what are you?

LI WANWAN's shadow snaps back to normal. He smiles, a child's smile with edges like broken glass.

LI WANWAN
I'm your son.

He touches the wooden fan to his father's chest. LI ZHENDONG stumbles back as if pushed by a hurricane.

ZHOU XIUFANG watches, not with fear, but with something like recognition.

FADE OUT.

title: Fortune Baby's Destiny
description: A young girl cursed with bad luck must leave her temple home to find her destined family and break the curse that affects all around her.
context:
Title: Fortune Baby's Destiny
Description: A young girl cursed with bad luck must leave her temple home to find her destined family and break the curse that affects all around her.

INT. DILAPIDATED TEMPLE - DAY

A dusty sunbeam cuts through the temple. FU BAO (8) sits on a cushion, perfectly still.

MASTER SHAN YUAN (O.S.)
Fu Bao.

FU BAO opens her eyes.

FU BAO
Master?

MASTER SHAN YUAN (60s), robes frayed at the edges, hurries in clutching a bowl of rice.

MASTER SHAN YUAN
Look. Actual alms.

He offers it. A silver ingot drops in. They watch it harden into stone. Neither reacts—it's routine.

Master sighs, then produces a steamed bun from his sleeve.

MASTER SHAN YUAN
Plan B.

He turns toward the altar. His foot catches the threshold. He stumbles. The bun arcs through the air, petrifying before it lands.

FU BAO
Master!

She catches his elbow. For a moment, she just holds him steady.

FU BAO
(quietly)
I'll mend the threshold.

MASTER SHAN YUAN
The threshold is fine.

He straightens with a wince.

FU BAO (V.O.)
Three brothers gone. Two winters without worshippers. Master says the temple follows the seasons, but I know which season I brought.

MASTER SHAN YUAN
What did I teach you about cause and effect?

FU BAO
That they're tangled like kites.

MASTER SHAN YUAN
Find where your string leads. It untangles everything.

A roof tile SHATTERS beside them. This, too, is routine.

But then Master flexes his fingers. The tips crumble to dust.

FU BAO
(staring)
Master—

MASTER SHAN YUAN
The mountain is done with me, Little Fortune. It needs you to leave.

FU BAO
I can try harder. I'll—

MASTER SHAN YUAN
Then I'll be dust by winter. Is that what you want?

Fu Bao's lip trembles. She shakes her head.

MASTER SHAN YUAN
(softening)
Find the family that shares your name. They'll be the ones who need you most.

He draws three yellow talismans. Presses them into her palm.

MASTER SHAN YUAN
One for true danger. That's all I can anchor from here.

FU BAO
What if I can't find them?

MASTER SHAN YUAN
What if you can?

He turns her toward the gate.

Fu Bao hesitates, then kneels. Three quick kowtows. She doesn't look back as she walks out.

Master Shan Yuan watches her go, his hand still crumbling.
continuation:
EXT. MOUNTAIN PATH - DAY

The HE family bounces in a donkey cart. WU CHUNHUA (40s) counts coins. LI XIUE (50s) sits rigid. HE DA JIANG (30s) drives. HE DA SHAN (28) lounges.

WU CHUNHUA
Six hundred wen. For the risk.

LI XIUE
We agreed on four hundred.

WU CHUNHUA
That was before your pig died. Bad luck has a price.

HE DA JIANG
(quietly)
The pig was sick.

WU CHUNHUA
The village calls it cursed. My cart, my rules.

HE DA SHAN
Just pay her, Big Brother. It's not like fortune favors you.

LI XIUE
A hundred wen for the return. Not one more.

WU CHUNHUA
Ha! Your eldest dead, your second sick—who else would take your grain? Who else would take you?

LI XIUE
You go too far!

HE DA JIANG
(firmly)
Enough.

The donkey STUMBLES. The cart jerks to a halt.

HE DA SHAN
Watch the road!

HE DA JIANG
(staring ahead)
There's a child.

FU BAO stands in the path, robes too big, smile too bright. Like she's holding something back.

HE DA SHAN
Perfect. More bad luck.

Silence.

FU BAO
Excuse me. Are you the He family?

Li Xiue's breath catches. He Da Jiang's knuckles whiten on the reins.

FU BAO
I think I'm supposed to come with you.

A long beat. The wind moves through the trees.

WU CHUNHUA
(coldly amused)
A hundred wen and you can have the whole lot of them. They're good at finding strays.

Fu Bao's gaze never leaves Li Xiue's face. Searching.

HE DA JIANG
(to his wife, low)
We can't even feed our own.

LI XIUE
(considering Fu Bao)
We've lost enough.

She doesn't reach for Fu Bao. But she doesn't turn away.

FU BAO
Thank you.

She climbs into the cart. The axle CREAKS. The donkey brays again, but Wu Chunhua flicks the reins, and they roll forward.

Fu Bao sits very still among them, holding her breath. Then she looks up and meets Li Xiue's eyes.

Her smile is real this time. And desperate. And hopeful. All at once.

title: The Prince's Manor
description: Over two thousand years ago, a loyal general pledges eternal devotion to a woman destined to become Crown Princess, but betrayal and tragedy await them both.
context:
Title: The Prince's Manor
Description: Over two thousand years ago, a loyal general pledges eternal devotion to a woman destined to become Crown Princess, but betrayal and tragedy await them both.

TITLE CARD: THE PRINCE'S MANOR - OVER 2000 YEARS AGO

EXT. PRINCE'S MANOR CORRIDOR - TWILIGHT

SHENG WEIRAN and SHEN MUBAI stand at the corridor's edge, their armor catching the last light. The weight of unspoken history hangs between them.

SHENG WEIRAN
The Emperor's edict arrives tomorrow. I'll be named Crown Princess. After fifteen years of fighting beside you... is this truly all you have to say?

SHEN MUBAI
(struggling to keep his voice steady)
My duty doesn't change with titles. I will protect the Crown Princess with my life. As I have always done.

SHENG WEIRAN
(quietly fierce)
I didn't ask about your duty. I asked about you.

SHEN MUBAI meets her eyes for a heartbeat, then deliberately breaks contact. He kneels, drawing his sword in a fluid motion—the blade flashes between them.

SHEN MUBAI
(formal, hollow)
The Crown Princess must not disobey the Emperor. Your honor is the empire's honor. But if you ever turn back... I will be behind you.

SHENG WEIRAN watches him kneel, understanding the gulf his words create. She turns away before he can see her face crumble.

SHENG WEIRAN
(softly, to herself)
I understand.

INT. SHENG WEIRAN'S BEDCHAMBER - NIGHT

SHENG WEIRAN sleeps fitfully. The door lock—visible in the foreground—has been cleanly broken.

A strange man, LOVER, materializes from the shadows. His fingers, cold as iron, trace her cheek. He takes her hand, pressing something into her palm.

SHENG WEIRAN
(mumbling in her sleep)
Mubai...

She jerks awake. The LOVER smiles—a predator's expression.

SHENG WEIRAN
(terror mixing with confusion)
The guards—how did you—

LOVER
(lifting her hand to show a jade token pressed against her skin)
The Crown Prince sends his regards, my beloved consort.

The door explodes inward. The CROWN PRINCE enters, flanked by soldiers holding torches. His eyes fix on the token in her hand.

CROWN PRINCE
(oiling rage with triumph)
Sheng Weiran.

SHENG WEIRAN scrambles backward, the token slipping from her fingers. It clatters to the floor—identical to the one the Prince wears.

CROWN PRINCE
I offered you legitimacy. My father's favor. And you repay me with this?

SHENG WEIRAN
(desperate, seeing the trap)
Your Highness, I was drugged! The wine—

FLASHBACK:
continuation:
INT. VICTORY BANQUET HALL - PREVIOUS NIGHT

The CROWN PRINCE personally pours wine for SHENG WEIRAN, his thumb pressing a white powder against the cup's rim.

CROWN PRINCE
(charming)
To the hero of Great Jin.

She drinks. He watches.

END FLASHBACK.

SHENG WEIRAN
(whisper)
The rebellion is over. My family's army is the threat now.

CROWN PRINCE
(coldly pleased)
The Sheng name is too powerful. But an adulterous Crown Princess? Her name will be erased. Guards—execute her.

Soldiers advance. SHEN MUBAI crashes through the window, glass shattering. He moves like a storm, his sword taking two soldiers in the opening heartbeat. But more pour through the door.

A blade finds his back. He staggers.

SHENG WEIRAN
(scream)
Mubai!

SHEN MUBAI drops to his knees before her, blood seeping through his armor. She catches him, her hands coming away red.

SHEN MUBAI
(barely audible)
I broke my vow. I turned back.

Their eyes meet. For the first time, no duty stands between them.

CROWN PRINCE
(raising his sword)
How touching. Let history remember you together.

He drives his blade through SHEN MUBAI's chest. The sword's tip emerges through SHENG WEIRAN's back. Their blood mingles on the floor.

SHENG WEIRAN's hand finds SHEN MUBAI's as the light leaves her eyes.

TRANSITION TO MODERN DAY

Time-space bleeds through blood—ancient red fades to neon.

INT. WALK-IN CLOSET - DAY

SHENG WEIRAN in modern dress is pinned against the wall by SHEN MUBAI in modern dress, his hand around her throat.

SHENG WEIRAN
(struggling)
Mubai...

title: The Lady's Rebellious Nature
description: A modern woman finds herself transported into an ancient world after reading a mysterious novel, where she must navigate danger and romance with seven attendants.
context:
Title: The Lady's Rebellious Nature
Description: A modern woman finds herself transported into an ancient world after reading a mysterious novel, where she must navigate danger and romance with seven attendants.

EXT. FOREST - NIGHT

Black screen. White Song typeface text: Episode One

A woman in ancient costume (THE LADY) lies unconscious on fallen leaves.

A BLACK-CLAD MASKED MAN crouches down and reaches out to check THE LADY's breath.

MASKED MAN (V.O.)
No breath. The poison worked.

The MASKED MAN begins searching THE LADY's body.

MASKED MAN (V.O.)
The Linglong Mirror must be on her.

Suddenly, THE LADY opens her eyes and sits up abruptly. She grabs his wrist. The MASKED MAN stumbles back in shock.

THE LADY (V.O.)
The last thing I remember—a truck's headlights. Then darkness. This isn't a hospital.

MONTAGE:

INT. CAR - NIGHT

A woman in modern clothes sits in the driver's seat, eyes vacant. A book lies open on the passenger seat, pages glowing.

NARRATOR (V.O.)
Yuewen Platinum author Nangong Qiuyue's new work "The Lady's Rebellious Nature" is now available.
continuation:
INT. OFFICE - NIGHT

A man types on a laptop, screen showing the same book.

NARRATOR (V.O.)
They say the final chapter binds your fate. They say you wake as the Lady herself.

EXT. FOREST - NIGHT

THE LADY runs through the forest in panic.

THE LADY
(screaming)
No—this isn't real!

END MONTAGE.

EXT. FOREST - NIGHT

THE LADY and the MASKED MAN face off.

MASKED MAN
Where is the Linglong Mirror?

THE LADY
I don't know what you're—

MASKED MAN
Then I'll take it from your corpse.

The MASKED MAN grabs her wrist. She twists free and kicks at him.

THE LADY
I'm not your damsel!

The MASKED MAN catches her ankle, sending her sprawling. He draws his sword.

MASKED MAN
You're whatever I say you are.

He swings. A CRIMSON FAN snaps open, catching the blade. The ATTENDANT appears.

MASKED MAN
The Qiushui fan!

ATTENDANT
I'm taking the Lady home.

The ATTENDANT and MASKED MAN engage in fierce combat. Blades flash, the fan whirls.

After an intense fight, the ATTENDANT drives off the MASKED MAN.

The ATTENDANT runs to THE LADY, who clutches her arm.

ATTENDANT
My lady! Your arm—

THE LADY
Who are you?

ATTENDANT
Your seventh attendant. San.

THE LADY
I don't need saving.

SAN
smiles faintly.

SAN
You need a guide.

The ATTENDANT holds the LADY, his expression full of concern.

FADE OUT.

title: After Heartbreak, I Rise in the Workplace with My Boss
description: After a painful breakup, Xiao Xi finds support from his female boss as he confronts his ex-girlfriend and begins a new chapter in his career.
context:
Title: After Heartbreak, I Rise in the Workplace with My Boss
Description: After a painful breakup, Xiao Xi finds support from his female boss as he confronts his ex-girlfriend and begins a new chapter in his career.

INT. COFFEE SHOP - DAY

XIAO XI and his EX-GIRLFRIEND sit across from each other. The FEMALE BOSS sits beside XIAO XI.

XIAO XI slams the table and stands up, glaring at his EX-GIRLFRIEND.

XIAO XI
I signed the papers. We're done. Why are you here?

EX-GIRLFRIEND
Your mom says you're a mess. I wanted to see if you'd changed your mind.

XIAO XI
I haven't. I'm done.

XIAO XI stands up, walks behind the FEMALE BOSS, and places his hands on her shoulders.

XIAO XI
This is my girlfriend. She trusts me. That's richer than anything.

EX-GIRLFRIEND
(standing up angrily)
Your boss? How convenient. President Sun wants to marry me. He's everything you're not.

XIAO XI
While he's still married? You're still the same.

XIAO XI gets emotional and moves forward. The FEMALE BOSS grabs his wrist to stop him.
continuation:
FEMALE BOSS
(coldly)
You've made your point. Let her leave.

The EX-GIRLFRIEND glares at them, then turns and storms off angrily.

XIAO XI and the FEMALE BOSS watch her leave.

XIAO XI and the FEMALE BOSS sit back down.

XIAO XI
(gratefully)
Sis, thank you for being with me today. If it weren't for you, I wouldn't have known how to break up with her.

FEMALE BOSS
Xiao Xi, she was the one who had a change of heart. Why did you play the bad guy?

XIAO XI
Men need to save face. If she spoke first, how awkward would that be?

FEMALE BOSS
Do you feel better now?

XIAO XI
(relieved)
I'm at peace with it. Life is free from now on.

FEMALE BOSS
I'm glad you're happy. Oh right, there's a meeting in a bit. Come with me and stay sharp.

The FEMALE BOSS's expression becomes serious.

title: A Woman's Rebirth (Episode 46)
description: A mother endures mistreatment from her mother-in-law while her husband remains indifferent, as her young daughter is punished unfairly.
context:
Title: A Woman's Rebirth (Episode 46)
Description: A mother endures mistreatment from her mother-in-law while her husband remains indifferent, as her young daughter is punished unfairly.

INT. HOME - DAY

Opening scene. The WIFE huddles on the sofa, QIANQIAN cradled in her arms. Both are tear-streaked, their cries quiet and exhausted.

INT. KITCHEN - DAY

The WIFE stirs a pot mechanically, her shoulders slumped in quiet resignation.

INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

QIANQIAN sits on the carpet, whispering to a worn plush cow.

QIANQIAN
Little cow, time for medicine. All better now.

Her brother DOUDOU rushes over, bigger and bolder, and snatches the toy.

DOUDOU
Mine now!

QIANQIAN scrambles after him.

QIANQIAN
(crying out)
Give it back! That's mine!

QIANQIAN trips and falls hard. The MOTHER-IN-LAW emerges from the bedroom holding a bowl. Seeing DOUDOU on the floor, she gasps.

MOTHER-IN-LAW
Doudou! My treasure!

The MOTHER-IN-LAW rushes to help DOUDOU up, checking him thoroughly.

MOTHER-IN-LAW
Let Grandma see. Are you hurt?

The MOTHER-IN-LAW whirls on QIANQIAN, her face darkening.

MOTHER-IN-LAW
What have you done? You clumsy, unlucky girl—how dare you hurt your brother!

The MOTHER-IN-LAW yanks QIANQIAN up and shoves her onto the sofa.

MOTHER-IN-LAW
Sit there! That mother of yours raised a disrespectful, ungrateful child!

QIANQIAN looks up, her small face set in a defiant glare.

MOTHER-IN-LAW
(stabbing a finger)
Don't you look at me like that!

The MOTHER-IN-LAW seizes QIANQIAN, pulling her toward the guest room and slamming the door with a bang.

MOTHER-IN-LAW
(shouting)
No manners! Just like your mother!
continuation:
INT. DINING ROOM - DAY

The family sits around the dining table. MOTHER-IN-LAW, DOUDOU, SISTER-IN-LAW, and the husband MINGLIANG. MINGLIANG stares at his phone, indifferent to his surroundings. MOTHER-IN-LAW heaps food onto DOUDOU's plate.

MOTHER-IN-LAW
Eat up, my good boy.

SISTER-IN-LAW
Where's the soup? We're waiting.

MOTHER-IN-LAW
Soup?

The WIFE comes out carrying a large bowl and places it on the table.

MOTHER-IN-LAW
(dismissive)
Can't earn, can't cook. What did my son see in you?

The SISTER-IN-LAW tastes the soup and grimaces.

SISTER-IN-LAW
Salt. Did you empty the whole jar, sister-in-law?

WIFE looks embarrassed, glancing around.

WIFE
Mingliang, where's Qianqian?

MINGLIANG
(without looking up)
Didn't notice.

WIFE
(anxiously)
How could you not notice? Where is she?

MOTHER-IN-LAW
(calmly)
She's reflecting in the back room. She hit Doudou and dared talk back.

WIFE
(in disbelief)
Mom, Qianqian would never—

MOTHER-IN-LAW
(raising her voice)
So I'm a liar now? In my own home?

The WIFE stops arguing. She gets up, worry etched on her face, and walks toward the room.

INT. BEDROOM - DAY

The WIFE opens the door to find QIANQIAN facing the wardrobe, her small frame trembling in the dim light.

The WIFE walks over and kneels in front of QIANQIAN. QIANQIAN's face is wet with tears, but she makes no sound.

The WIFE's face crumples, tears falling freely.

The WIFE pulls QIANQIAN tightly into her arms. Mother and daughter cry bitterly together.

ON SCREEN: TO BE CONTINUED

title: The Female Scholar
description: A gifted young woman from a concubine's background excels in both literature and martial arts, earning praise from her tutor and hope for a future in the imperial examinations, but faces jealousy and danger within her own household.
context:
Title: The Female Scholar
Description: A gifted young woman from a concubine's background excels in both literature and martial arts, earning praise from her tutor and hope for a future in the imperial examinations, but faces jealousy and danger within her own household.

INT. YAN RESIDENCE - COURTYARD - DAY

YAN RUYU flows through her forms, sparring with YAN YAOZU. Her flying kick lands with controlled precision, sending him to the ground without injury.

TUTOR ZHAO
(muttering to himself)
Remarkable. She reads three moves ahead.

TUTOR ZHAO steps forward, clapping.

TUTOR ZHAO
Excellent! Brother Yan, your daughter's mind is as sharp as her blade.

MASTER YAN watches from the doorway, pride warring with concern.

MASTER YAN
She does us honor, Teacher.

Several CHILDREN watching from the gate begin to chant:

CHILDREN
Ruyu-jiejie wins again! Ruyu-jiejie wins again!

YAN YAOZU lies on the ground, humiliation burning his face.

YAN YAOZU
(quietly)
This isn't over.

MASTER YAN
(to Tutor Zhao)
It's wasted effort, Teacher. Official careers require more than skill. They require status.

TUTOR ZHAO
The new Emperor values merit above bloodline. I've seen the edicts in draft. Women may yet sit for the examinations.

MASTER YAN's eyes widen. He turns to SU WENPEI, who stands with her tea tray forgotten.

MASTER YAN
Wenpei... did you hear?

SU WENPEI
(voice barely audible)
I hear many things, Master.

YAN RUYU
Father, Tutor Zhao says my essay on water tactics shows promise.

MASTER YAN
(overwhelmed)
Yes. Yes, show him. Teacher, please. Stay for the evening meal. We must discuss this further.

TUTOR ZHAO
The future waits for no man. Or woman.

As they exit, QIN SHUANG appears at the courtyard entrance. Her eyes track them, then settle on her son still on the ground. Her expression hardens.
continuation:
INT. ROOM - NIGHT

YAN RUYU kneels on the floor, her essay scroll in hand. SU WENPEI stands over her, trembling.

SU WENPEI
Do you know your mistake?

YAN RUYU
I controlled the blow. He wasn't injured.

SU WENPEI
You injured something more precious. His place.

YAN RUYU
Father said excellence is the only path for women like me.

SU WENPEI
(fiercely)
NOT. FOR. YOU.

She slaps Yan Ruyu, the crack echoing. Then she collapses beside her, gathering her daughter close.

SU WENPEI
(anguished)
Child, why won't you listen? I hide you to protect you. Every brilliance you show is a target on your back.

YAN RUYU
(pulling back)
Then I'll be too bright to shoot.

SU WENPEI
You cannot outshine the sun. We are swallows under the eaves. We bow our heads, or we are swept away.

YAN RUYU
I won't be swept. And I won't bow.

She pushes free and runs. Su Wenpei crawls after her, falling at QIN SHUANG's feet. Qin Shuang holds a bowl, steam rising like a ghost.

QIN SHUANG
Wenpei, you've raised a phoenix in a sparrow's nest. Master Yan speaks of nothing else. He even mentions... making you his legitimate wife.

SU WENPEI looks up in horror.

SU WENPEI
No... I never wanted...

QIN SHUANG
I know. But your daughter's talents have ambitions of their own. So. You drink this, and she becomes mine. Legitimate, protected, destined for greatness. Or she continues as she is, and I cannot answer for what becomes of either of you.

She holds out the bowl. The liquid within reflects only darkness.

title: The Female CEO's Personal General
description: A capable CEO meets a mysterious amnesiac general disguised as a beggar, who demonstrates extraordinary combat skills when she is threatened by thugs.
context:
Title: The Female CEO's Personal General
Description: A capable CEO meets a mysterious amnesiac general disguised as a beggar, who demonstrates extraordinary combat skills when she is threatened by thugs.

EXT. COMPANY BUILDING STAIRWAY - DAY

QIAO WEIRAN, sharp-suited and visibly stressed, walks down the exterior stairway outside her corporate headquarters. Subtitle introduction: QIAO WEIRAN, President of Qiao Group.

Her phone screen glows with her father's face—SONG ZHIGUO, apoplectic.

SONG ZHIGUO (V.O.)
Qiao Weiran, get back here this instant!

QIAO WEIRAN stops. At the bottom of the stairs sits YE FENG, mid-30s, his tattered clothes belying a soldier's posture. Subtitle introduction: YE FENG, Amnesiac National Guardian General.

QIAO WEIRAN
(into phone)
Why should I? I'm president of Qiao Group. My decisions are mine alone.

SONG ZHIGUO
On what grounds? I'm still chairman! Hongyuan Group is worth billions. If you sign with Chen Tech, you're burning our future!

QIAO WEIRAN
Then watch me.

She angles her phone camera toward Ye Feng, then extracts a stack of cash from her bag. She fans the bills—letting them flutter down.

SONG ZHIGUO
You stubborn—Weiran! Weiran!

QIAO WEIRAN hangs up. A fifty-dollar bill lands on Ye Feng's lowered head.

QIAO WEIRAN
Heads up. Free money. Aren't you going to take it?

YE FENG slowly opens his eyes—alert, calculating.

YE FENG
I don't take charity. I trade value.

QIAO WEIRAN
(thinking)
A businessman beggar?

YE FENG takes the bill off his head and picks up another from the ground.

YE FENG
This covers a day's wage. For that, I'll demonstrate.

YE FENG stands up in one fluid motion.

YE FENG
Consider it a preview.

QIAO WEIRAN
(thinking)
Great. A performance artist.
continuation:
QIAO WEIRAN turns to leave.

YE FENG
Miss Qiao.

She freezes. She never told him her name.

A black van screeches to a halt. Four MEN in suits emerge—led by SCAR-FACED THUG LEADER.

THUG LEADER
Miss Qiao. Boss Wei wants a word.

QIAO WEIRAN turns back to Ye Feng.

QIAO WEIRAN
That demonstration. Can it involve breaking bones?

YE FENG
(smiles faintly)
Only if they insist.

THUG LEADER
(to Ye Feng)
Walk away, drifter. This is corporate business.

YE FENG
You're blocking the lady's path.

THUG LEADER
(laughs)
And you're blocking my patience—

YE FENG moves. A blur. His palm strikes the Leader's sternum—sends him stumbling.

THUG LEADER
Get him!

The thugs surge. Ye Feng snatches a fallen tree branch—whips it in a tight arc. Two men drop.

QIAO WEIRAN ducks behind a concrete planter.

QIAO WEIRAN
(thinking)
Holy shit.

The Thug Leader draws a knife. More men rush forward. Ye Feng spins the branch like a baton. Crack. Crack. Everyone down.

QIAO WEIRAN runs out from behind the planter.

QIAO WEIRAN
Behind you!

YE FENG whips around to see a thug with a metal pipe. The frame freezes.

FADE OUT.

title: She's Like the Distant Moon
description: A woman mistakenly enters the wrong hotel room and spends the night with a stranger, only to discover a shocking connection.
context:
Title: She's Like the Distant Moon
Description: A woman mistakenly enters the wrong hotel room and spends the night with a stranger, only to discover a shocking connection.

EXT. HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT

A dreamlike sequence. GU JINLAN wears a red dress, lying on the bed. SHI YE leans over her, gripping her wrist.

SHI YE
Who are you?

SHI YE (V.O.)
This dream feels more real than any before.

INT. HOTEL CORRIDOR - NIGHT

GU JINLAN, wearing a red dress, staggers through the hotel corridor while on the phone.

GU JINLAN
I'm almost there, stop rushing me.

WOMAN ON PHONE (V.O.)
The Song family doesn't know you changed your surname. Remember to use the name Ye Jinlan.

GU JINLAN
I know.

WOMAN ON PHONE (V.O.)
When you meet Song Yuze, you better behave. He's someone we can't afford to offend.

GU JINLAN
You better... you better show me some respect.

WOMAN ON PHONE (V.O.)
Have you been drinking?

GU JINLAN
Yeah, I... Mr. Ye was buying, it would be a waste not to drink.

WOMAN ON PHONE (V.O.)
You're really causing trouble. The room number is 807, don't get it wrong.

GU JINLAN
I know.

GU JINLAN hangs up and leans against the wall unsteadily. She looks at room number 804 through blurry eyes.

GU JINLAN
(to herself)
807...

She continues walking, searching for the room.

GU JINLAN
(to herself)
807... 807...

She stops at room 808, the door slightly ajar.

GU JINLAN
(to herself)
807... here it is.

She pushes the door open and enters.
continuation:
INT. HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT

SHI YE sits against the headboard with his shirt unbuttoned, eyes closed. Water and prescription bottles on the nightstand.

GU JINLAN enters and looks at SHI YE on the bed.

GU JINLAN (V.O.)
My God. My husband of four years... and he's devastating. But so ill.

GU JINLAN walks to the bed and touches his forehead.

GU JINLAN
Hey. Wake up.

She leans closer. Suddenly, SHI YE opens his eyes, grabs her wrist, and pulls her onto the bed.

GU JINLAN
Ah!

They entangle on the bed.

GU JINLAN (V.O.)
Hongyu drugged me. And him...

GU JINLAN looks at SHI YE beneath her.

GU JINLAN
I'll bring you good fortune, you be my antidote. That's not too much to ask, right?

She lowers her head and kisses SHI YE. They embrace and kiss passionately.

INT. HOTEL ROOM - DAY

Morning. Sunlight streams through the window. GU JINLAN and SHI YE lie in bed.

GU JINLAN wakes up, rubbing her head in pain. She sits up and adjusts her torn dress.

GU JINLAN (V.O.)
Ridiculous. How does someone so ill have such strength?

She walks to the table by the bed and picks up a welcome card.

The card reads "Esteemed Mr. Shi, Suite 808."

GU JINLAN
(shocked)
Mr. Shi? Oh God, I slept with the wrong person!

She turns back in horror to look at SHI YE, still asleep in bed.

title: Mom, I Don't Hurt
description: A traffic accident forces a doctor to choose between his own daughter and another patient, revealing his misplaced priorities and cold heart.
context:
Title: Mom, I Don't Hurt
Description: A traffic accident forces a doctor to choose between his own daughter and another patient, revealing his misplaced priorities and cold heart.

EXT. ROAD UNDER OVERPASS - DAY

A multi-car pileup. Twisted metal and broken glass litter the asphalt. BYSTANDERS pull survivors from vehicles.

BYSTANDER A
(shouting)
Need help here!

BYSTANDER B
They're coming!

An ambulance ROARS up. TWO NURSES sprint out with trauma kits.

NURSE 1 moves to a YOUNG MAN bleeding from his head.

NURSE 1
Sir, don't move.

She checks his pupils, attaches a GREEN tag to his wrist: "Level 1 Minor."

Nearby, XIA MENG sits holding her daughter HAN NIANNIAN (8), whose leg is soaked in blood.

XIA MENG
(choking up)
Niannian, hold on. Dad's hospital is three minutes away.

HAN NIANNIAN
(whispering)
Mom... it's not that bad.

NURSE 2 runs over, kneels, begins assessing.

NURSE 2
Name?

XIA MENG
Han Niannian. I'm an ER nurse. How's the distal pulse?

NURSE 2
Weak. Might be the femoral.

She wraps a pressure bandage, attaches a YELLOW tag: "Level 2 Moderate."

NURSE 2
Keep pressure. Doctor's coming.

XIA MENG
(to Niannian)
You're stable. You're so brave.

A second ambulance SCREECHES to a halt. DR. HAN DONG jumps out, pulling on gloves. He scans the scene: professional, methodical.

His eyes land on his wife and daughter. He takes a step toward them.

PARAMEDIC
(shouting)
Doctor! Red tag! Chest trauma!
continuation:
Han Dong turns. In a crushed sedan, PARAMEDICS work on YAOZHU (17). The boy coughs blood.

DR. HAN DONG
(to paramedic)
Status.

PARAMEDIC
Tension pneumothorax. Lost pulse once.

Han Dong sees the RED tag on Yaozhu's wrist. He glances at Niannian's YELLOW tag.

XIA MENG
(shouting)
Han Dong! Please! She's your daughter!

DR. HAN DONG
(strained)
He's coding. Two minutes. I stabilize, then I'm there.

XIA MENG
Her pressure's dropping!

DR. HAN DONG
(over shoulder)
You're a nurse, Xia Meng. You know RED means now.

He turns away. Xia Meng trembles.

XIA MENG
(whispering)
She's slipping to Level 3.

Niannian's head lolls. Her breathing shallows.

HAN NIANNIAN
Mom... Dad's helping them first... because they're worse.

Blood seeps through the bandage. The YELLOW tag is now meaningless.

XIA MENG
(whispering fiercely)
No. He's coming back.

She presses harder with both hands.

XIA MENG
(shouting, raw)
Han Dong! Her tag's wrong! It's RED!

Niannian's fingers touch her mother's face.

HAN NIANNIAN
Mom... I don't hurt anymore.

Her hand falls. Xia Meng screams.

XIA MENG
Niannian! Niannian!

Sirens wail. Smoke rises. Han Dong works on Yaozhu, his back to his family. The PARAMEDICS call out vitals. Yaozhu's monitor shows a steady rhythm returning.

Han Dong's shoulders tighten. He knows.

title: The Last Gift
description: A terminally ill mother records a final video message for her son, preparing gifts for every birthday she will miss, before taking her own life to spare her family further pain.
context:
Title: The Last Gift
Description: A terminally ill mother records a final video message for her son, preparing gifts for every birthday she will miss, before taking her own life to spare her family further pain.

INT. BEDROOM - DAY

Opening shot: A warm family photo of three sits on a table.

Cut to: MOM sits on the floor of a bedroom. In front of her is an open suitcase filled with clothing. A video camera on a tripod is recording her.

MOM
(to the camera)
Chenchen... these are for you. The birthdays I'll miss.

MOM picks up a pair of blue denim shorts, her smile strained.

MOM
Clothes until you're a teenager. Probably all the wrong styles by then. Exchange whatever you don't want.

MOM puts down the shorts and picks up a beautifully wrapped blue gift box.

MOM
Your birthday gifts. Age five to eighteen. Take care of yourself. I'll be watching.

MOM's eyes redden, her voice begins to choke.

MOM
Forgive me for missing your eighteenth. Please don't hate me for this.

MOM holds back tears. After finishing, she reaches out and turns off the camera. The moment the camera shuts off, she can no longer hold back. She covers her face and breaks down crying.

MOM
(sobbing uncontrollably)
Forgive me... I'm sorry...

Suddenly, a childish voice calls out.

CHENCHEN (O.S.)
continuation:
Mom?

MOM immediately stops crying, wipes away her tears, and turns to look at the doorway. Her son CHENCHEN stands there in pajamas.

CHENCHEN
When is Dad coming home? I'm hungry.

MOM
(gently)
Soon. Come here. Sleep with me for a bit?

CHENCHEN nods obediently and climbs onto the bed.

MOM also walks over and lies down beside CHENCHEN.

MOM
(gently stroking CHENCHEN's hair)
You sleep so deeply now. Remember last week's thunderstorm? You didn't even stir.

CHENCHEN
Mm. I like sleeping with you.

MOM
Good boy.

MOM holds CHENCHEN tightly, closes her eyes, and begins to hum a lullaby softly.

MOM
(singing)
The dark sky hangs low, bright stars follow along, insects fly, insects fly...

Sound effect: A gunshot.

CHENCHEN
(murmuring in his sleep)
Mom...

A children's choir version of the lullaby plays. MOM lies quietly, no longer breathing. The expression on her face is one of pain released, not peace.

title: When Will Sister Return
description: A woman searches for her lost brother for twenty years while working at a quarry, unaware he has been adopted by a wealthy family and is now searching for her.
context:
Title: When Will Sister Return
Description: A woman searches for her lost brother for twenty years while working at a quarry, unaware he has been adopted by a wealthy family and is now searching for her.

EXT. QUARRY - DAY

SHEN XIAOYU and many WORKERS labor hard with hammers and shovels on the rugged quarry terrain.

A shrill bell rings.

LIU DAYI (V.O.)
Hurry up, everyone! It's Mid-Autumn Festival, the factory is handing out mooncakes! Come line up! Come get your mooncakes!

The WORKERS put down their tools and walk toward a table to line up for mooncakes. LIU DAYI sits behind the table distributing them.

SHEN XIAOYU runs to the front of the line, holding a yellowed black-and-white photo in her hand, anxiously showing it to the workers.

SHEN XIAOYU
Please... this is my younger brother. Missing twenty years. I'll give everything I have for a real lead.

Most workers shake their heads expressionlessly and walk around her to get their mooncakes.

SHEN XIAOYU walks disappointedly to LIU DAYI's table.

LIU DAYI
(flippantly)
Hey, Xiaoyu, your mooncake isn't here. Tonight, go to the factory manager's office to get it.

SHEN XIAOYU
(coldly)
If there isn't one, forget it.

SHEN XIAOYU turns and leaves.

LIU DAYI
(disdainfully)
What's she pretending for!

SHEN XIAOYU walks to the side and sits down. She takes out a cloth-wrapped steamed bun from the basket on her back and eats it silently. She looks at the bun in her hand, then touches a tooth-shaped pendant hanging around her neck, her eyes filled with sadness.
continuation:
INT. FLASHBACK - COUNTRY ROAD - 20 YEARS AGO - RAINY NIGHT

A car speeds past and hits a little boy (young SHEN XINCHENG).

A well-dressed couple (BAI XIA and her HUSBAND) get out of the car and discover the boy lying in the rain.

BAI XIA
Whose child is this? Does anyone know?

BAI XIA'S HUSBAND
To the hospital!

The couple picks up the little boy and hurries into the car.

Not far away, young SHEN XIAOYU runs through the rain, crying.

YOUNG SHEN XIAOYU
(crying out)
Little brother, where are you?

INT. LUXURIOUS LIVING ROOM - DAY

Twenty years later, SHEN XINCHENG, dressed in a suit, excitedly hands a document to his adoptive mother BAI XIA.

SHEN XINCHENG
Mom, the investigator found her. She's been searching for me online for five years. At a quarry.

BAI XIA
(smiling)
You've done well. The company is yours. But Xincheng—how many impostors have we seen?

SHEN XINCHENG
She has the pendant. The one from the hospital. And our photo together. Mom, I need to bring her home.

BAI XIA
(sighing)
Then go. But wear humility like armor. Test her heart before you trust.

SHEN XINCHENG listens, then falls into thought.

EXT. VILLA - DAY

SHEN XINCHENG changes into simple, worn clothes and walks toward an old car. An ASSISTANT in a suit follows behind him.

ASSISTANT
President, the DNA kit and documents are ready. This vehicle...

SHEN XINCHENG
Take me to the quarry, then head back right away. I'll go see my sister by myself.

SHEN XINCHENG gets in the car.

EXT. QUARRY - DAY

SHEN XIAOYU is working when a CO-WORKER runs over.

CO-WORKER
Sister Xiaoyu, there's someone at the bottom of the mountain looking for you. He says he's your younger brother. Showed the guards a photo... of you both as children.

SHEN XIAOYU suddenly looks up, her face full of shock and disbelief.

SHEN XIAOYU
(urgently)
Where is he? Where?

Tears well up in SHEN XIAOYU's eyes, filled with hope.

title: Sister, Please Run Wild With Me
description: After a passionate night in a hotel, a woman in a wedding dress leaves money for the man, claiming it was just for fun. The man, revealed to be a powerful CEO, has been unable to forget her for years and decides to pursue her by assuming a student identity.
context:
Title: Sister, Please Run Wild With Me
Description: After a passionate night in a hotel, a woman in a wedding dress leaves money for the man, claiming it was just for fun. The man, revealed to be a powerful CEO, has been unable to forget her for years and decides to pursue her by assuming a student identity.

INT. HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT

HUO YANCHUAN has just finished showering, a white towel wrapped around his lower body, emerging from behind a frosted glass door. His physique is strong, with well-defined muscles.

NAN WAN, wearing a magnificent wedding dress, sits on the messy bed, holding her phone, looking at him warily.

HUO YANCHUAN walks toward the bed step by step, his eyes full of aggression as he looks at NAN WAN.

NAN WAN
First time since Barcelona?

HUO YANCHUAN
Does it matter?

NAN WAN
It does. I don't share.

HUO YANCHUAN
Is that so? Then why the dress?

NAN WAN picks up a wine glass and takes a light sip of red wine.

NAN WAN
Can you handle a wedding dress, or does sister need to teach you?

HUO YANCHUAN
(chuckling)
Sister?

NAN WAN
I'll show you how it's done.

HUO YANCHUAN leans closer to NAN WAN, one hand propped on the bed, the other caressing her cheek.

HUO YANCHUAN
You won't be walking tomorrow.

HUO YANCHUAN kisses NAN WAN. The two kiss passionately and fall onto the bed. The room's lights dim.

FADE TO:
continuation:
INT. HOTEL ROOM - DAY

The next morning. Broken wine glasses and clothing are scattered on the floor.

On the bed, NAN WAN has changed into silk pajamas. She gently strokes the chest of HUO YANCHUAN sleeping beside her, her expression complex.

NAN WAN
(to herself)
This was a mistake. It can't happen again.

NAN WAN gets out of bed, changes into a sharp black business suit, puts on high heels, and prepares to leave.

HUO YANCHUAN suddenly wakes up and grabs her hand from behind. NAN WAN turns around, and the two lock eyes.

NAN WAN breaks free, takes out a stack of cash from her handbag, and throws it on HUO YANCHUAN.

NAN WAN
For the room. We're even.

HUO YANCHUAN
(sitting up, covering himself with the blanket)
You think money solves this?

NAN WAN
What do you want? A ring? I'm engaged. This was goodbye.

HUO YANCHUAN
You're running.

NAN WAN
(leaning close to him, lifting his chin with her finger)
We were always good together. But some things don't change.

NAN WAN straightens up and removes a diamond ring from her hand.

NAN WAN
Here. My engagement ring. I've been trying to forget you since the day I accepted it.

NAN WAN places the ring in front of HUO YANCHUAN, turns and leaves gracefully. At the door, she turns back and blows him a kiss, then closes the door and leaves.

HUO YANCHUAN watches her leave, his expression shifting from shock to determination. He takes out a photo from his wallet - a younger NAN WAN at a beach bar.

HUO YANCHUAN (V.O.)
Three years, Nan Wan. You really think I'd let you go?

HUO YANCHUAN picks up his phone and makes a call.

HUO YANCHUAN
Cancel the Tokyo deal. Get me a student identity at Nancheng University. Business school.

ASSISTANT (O.S.)
Yes, Mr. Huo.

HUO YANCHUAN hangs up the phone, picks up the ring NAN WAN left behind, his eyes becoming determined and possessive.

HUO YANCHUAN (V.O.)
You want to run? Then we'll play it your way.

FADE OUT.

title: Betrayal and Revenge
description: A once-wealthy heir, poisoned and betrayed by his lover and best friend, discovers the horrifying truth behind his terminal illness.
context:
Title: Betrayal and Revenge
Description: A once-wealthy heir, poisoned and betrayed by his lover and best friend, discovers the horrifying truth behind his terminal illness.

INT. RUNDOWN BLACK CLINIC - DAY

Thunder and lightning rage outside. A rat steals chocolate beans from a messy table.

LU HAOSHEN (30s), wearing a stained hospital gown, deathly pale and trembling, lies face down on a rusted bedframe. His fingers—once steady enough to sign million-dollar deals—struggle toward a cracked medicine bowl on the nightstand.

His hand convulses. The bowl shatters on the concrete floor. Bitter medicine pools with blood from his bleeding gums.

LU HAOSHEN rolls off the bed, crawling through the ceramic shards. He scoops the tainted residue with trembling hands, forcing it into his mouth. Blood trickles from his lips.

He moans, eyes fixed on the poison bottle on the table. He inches forward.
