A pair of designer heels clicks over, crushing his fingers against the floor.

ZHAO JINGKUN (O.S.)
My God. Is that really you, Haoshen?

JI XIAOXIAO (O.S.)
Three months. You look like a corpse we forgot to bury.

JI XIAOXIAO and ZHAO JINGKUN loom over LU HAOSHEN, their reflections warped in the medicine puddle.

JI XIAOXIAO
(laughing)
Oh, this is precious.

LU HAOSHEN
(weakly)
Xiaoxiao... please. Just enough for one dose.

LU HAOSHEN
The antidote... you know the price. Xiaoxiao, for everything I gave you...

ZHAO JINGKUN kicks his hand away. LU HAOSHEN's fracture from last week screams.

LU HAOSHEN
(moaning)
Ah...!

ZHAO JINGKUN
(crouching, gripping LU HAOSHEN's jaw)
Still begging from her? After you signed over your family's shares? Your penthouse? Tell me, was it my idea or yours to make you my personal ATM?

LU HAOSHEN
(agitated)
I gave you everything. The company, the properties... You said you'd save me. Xiaoxiao, remember? When I bankrolled your 'art career'—when you needed that Taipei gallery—

JI XIAOXIAO
(kicking his ribs, precisely)
That was consulting fee. For teaching a spoiled boy to pretend he had talent. But pigs can't paint, and pigs don't get to marry their owners.

LU HAOSHEN
(desperately)
Our agreement. The transfer documents. You signed them—you promised marriage. A life together.

JI XIAOXIAO
You're a dead man walking. I'd rather burn in hell.

ZHAO JINGKUN
Let's go. The smell of failure is contagious.
continuation:
EXT. OUTSIDE CLINIC - NIGHT

Heavy rain pours. ZHAO JINGKUN hauls LU HAOSHEN by the collar, throwing him into the mud. A final kick to the spine.

LU HAOSHEN
(in pain)
Not possible... the doctors said... autoimmune...

ZHAO JINGKUN
(grabbing LU HAOSHEN's hair)
Autoimmune? Cute. Ever wonder why your 'vitamins' tasted metallic? Xiaoxiao's special thallium blend. Slow. Undetectable. Terminal. She painted the capsules herself.

FLASHBACK: Young LU HAOSHEN paints, chewing his pencil nervously. JI XIAOXIAO beside him gently corrects his grip, her smile warm. She produces a vitamin case. He swallows without looking.

LU HAOSHEN
(shocked)
The... supplements? Three years?

JI XIAOXIAO
(walking over with an umbrella, finally sincere)
I loved watching you trust me. Every morning, like a good little dog.

JI XIAOXIAO and ZHAO JINGKUN kiss—slow, deliberate, inches from LU HAOSHEN's face.

LU HAOSHEN
(heartbroken)
No... not you both. Not since—

JI XIAOXIAO
(triumphantly)
Since university. Your money bought our vacations. Our apartment. This baby.

She touches her stomach. ZHAO JINGKUN covers her hand with his.

ZHAO JINGKUN
(laughing)
Tomorrow's our wedding. Black tie. Your family chapel. We'll save you a seat—if you can crawl.

ZHAO JINGKUN and JI XIAOXIAO leave in the rain, his arm around her shoulders.

LU HAOSHEN lies in the mud, convulsing. Not from poison—from truth.

LU HAOSHEN
Ahhh—!

A red sports car hydroplanes to a stop. The door flies open. GU JIA (28, in a business suit) sprints out, phone already dialing emergency.

GU JIA
(anxiously)
Haoshen! Stay with me—stay—

GU JIA lifts LU HAOSHEN's head from the water. Her ID badge dangles: LU CORPORATION HEAD OF FORENSICS.

GU JIA
I have the lab results. I know what they did.

LU HAOSHEN's eyes focus for the first time in months. He sees her. He sees everything.

LU HAOSHEN loses consciousness as sirens wail in the distance.

title: I Am the Female Lead
description: A wife confronts an actress she believes is having an affair with her husband on a film set.
context:
Title: I Am the Female Lead
Description: A wife confronts an actress she believes is having an affair with her husband on a film set.

INT. DRESSING ROOM - DAY

GUO ZILU bursts through the door, her trembling hand gripping YAO JIALING's shoulder. YAO's makeup brush clatters into the mirror.

GUO ZILU
(low, trembling)
You look smaller in person, Yao Jialing.

YAO JIALING
(jerking away)
Do I know you? Who let you—

GUO ZILU
(her voice cracks)
Three months of deleted texts. Four hotel charges. One bracelet I never received.

YAO JIALING
(thin smile)
Ah. The wife. How... method of you.

GUO ZILU
Security! Get this—

YAO JIALING
(overlapping, louder)
Security! Remove her!

ASSISTANT (O.S.)
(urgent whisper into phone)
Mrs. Tie found out. Yes, THAT Mrs. Tie.

GUO ZILU
You wanted his money? Fine. But you posted that bracelet on Weibo. Tagging our anniversary date.

YAO JIALING
I post a lot of things. So does your husband. Like that "business trip" to Sanya—
continuation:
TIE GUO charges in, blocking the doorway from gathering crew.

TIE GUO
(to hallway)
Show's over! Back to work!

GUO ZILU
(to YAO)
You think this is a show? These people should know what their "female lead" does after wrap.

TIE GUO
(pleading under his breath)
Zilu, not here. The project's fifty million in—

YAO JIALING
(smoothing her costume)
Sixty. And I earned every frame.

ASSISTANT (O.S.)
(overheard, snapping fingers)
That's why she got Li Lin to post that denial. Damage control.

YAO JIALING
(spins toward door)
My sister already clarified those screenshots. Find better gossip.

GUO ZILU
Your sister defended you. Your "boyfriend" denied you. Your investor's wife is standing right here. Who's the lead in your life story, Jialing?

TIE GUO
(to GUO ZILU, desperate)
She's just talent. A contract. You think I'd risk us for... for craft services and a call sheet?

YAO JIALING
(trembling, but projects to the room)
I survived three years of auditions and your husband's "meetings." I AM the female lead. And you?

She slams the dressing table. The mirror cracks between their reflections.

title: Beloved Wife in Arms: The 80s Factory Director's Endless Love
description: A young woman marries a man she barely knows to fulfill her grandmother's dying wish. On their wedding night, he is called away for work, and they don't see each other again for five years.
context:
Title: Beloved Wife in Arms: The 80s Factory Director's Endless Love
Description: A young woman marries a man she barely knows to fulfill her grandmother's dying wish. On their wedding night, he is called away for work, and they don't see each other again for five years.

EXT. COUNTY CIVIL AFFAIRS BUREAU - DAY

MOTHER ZHOU paces anxiously at the entrance of the Civil Affairs Bureau, clutching a cloth-wrapped bundle.

ZHOU YUCHEN rides up on a bicycle patched with tape, carrying ZHANG XIUYING. Both have dust streaked across their faces, sleeves rolled to the elbow.

ZHOU YUCHEN
Mom. The shuttle bus from the commune broke down.

MOTHER ZHOU
(angrily)
You're getting your marriage certificate and you look like you've been dragged through a rice paddy! This is your first time laying eyes on each other.

ZHANG XIUYING
(meeting Zhou Yuchen's gaze)
At least we know neither of us shirks hard work.

A shared, tentative smile passes between them.

MOTHER ZHOU
Wipe your faces. Registrar Lao Wang is waiting.

She hands them a damp towel. Zhou Yuchen passes it to Zhang Xiuying first.

INT. MARRIAGE REGISTRATION OFFICE - DAY

ZHOU YUCHEN and ZHANG XIUYING sit before the REGISTRAR, still faintly smudged. The Registrar adjusts his glasses.

REGISTRAR
Occupations?

ZHOU YUCHEN
Hydraulic engineer. Third Machine Tool Factory.

ZHANG XIUYING
Embroidery mechanic. Fifteenth Production Brigade.

The Registrar stamps two certificates with a loud CLACK and slides them forward. They reach for them simultaneously, hands brushing.
continuation:
INT. BRIDAL CHAMBER - NIGHT

Wedding night. ZHANG XIUYING sits on the kang in a simple red jacket, fingers tracing the wood grain of the kang table. ZHOU YUCHEN sets down a kerosene lamp.

He opens a wooden box containing a slender gold bracelet, its clasp shaped like a tiny gear.

ZHOU YUCHEN
My grandmother wore this while operating the textile machines. She said it brought her luck during the toughest years.

He fastens it around her wrist. The lamp light catches the gold.

ZHANG XIUYING
Then I'll wear it while I work.

ZHOU YUCHEN
When the factory bonus comes through this winter, we'll have a proper ceremony.

For the first time, he lifts her face to the light, studying it carefully.

INT. BRIDAL CHAMBER - MORNING

ZHOU YUCHEN, fully dressed, watches Zhang Xiuying sleep. He touches a Polaroid photo on the nightstand—her Mao badge ID photo, obtained through his mother.

Urgent knocking. He opens the door to COLLEAGUE LI, breathless.

COLLEAGUE LI
(nervously)
Zhou! The Guangzhou foundry's new hydraulic press failed. Four workers injured. They need our specs and you on site immediately. Train leaves in forty minutes.

He thrusts a hard-won ticket into Zhou Yuchen's hand. Zhou Yuchen glances back at the bedroom, then grabs a scrap of paper and a pencil stub.

INT. XIXIAN EMBROIDERY FACTORY - DAY

ON SCREEN: Five years later

FEMALE WORKERS crowd the hall, gossiping. ZHANG XIUYING crouches by a broken treadle machine, adjusting the shuttle with a screwdriver. A SMALL GIRL with her hair in tufted braids clutches her leg.

FEMALE WORKER A
They say the new director demanded our best mechanic be present for inspection. That's why we're all lined up like ducks.

FEMALE WORKER B
If he's single, I'll volunteer to oil his bicycle chain every morning!

The workers giggle. Zhang Xiuying stands, wiping her hands on her work apron.

ZHANG XIUYING (V.O.)
Four letters sent, none returned. The factory moved last year. Maybe he can't find us.

A black sedan stops. ZHOU YUCHEN steps out, older, leaner, carrying a leather portfolio. He moves through the bowing workers, eyes scanning the machinery, not the people—until he sees Zhang Xiuying's hands. The screwdriver. The gear-shaped bracelet.

He stops mid-step. She looks up.

ZHOU YUCHEN
(quietly)
You're supposed to be at the Fifteenth Brigade.

ZHANG XIUYING
We transferred when the factory opened. I... kept the bracelet oiled.

The little girl peers out from behind her. Zhou Yuchen's knuckles go white on his portfolio.

title: The Delicate County Magistrate Enters Your Embrace
description: An emperor in disguise travels to Peach Blossom County and encounters a mysterious modern woman who has become the county magistrate.
context:
Title: The Delicate County Magistrate Enters Your Embrace
Description: An emperor in disguise travels to Peach Blossom County and encounters a mysterious modern woman who has become the county magistrate.

EXT. PEACH BLOSSOM COUNTY OUTSKIRTS - DAY

A carriage creaks along a dusty path beneath weathered ramparts.

The carriage passes through the city gate, where a wooden sign reads "PEACH BLOSSOM COUNTY."

EXT. PEACH BLOSSOM COUNTY STREET - DAY

The carriage halts amid market chaos. Vendors shout. Children scatter.

XIAO LIZI (O.S.)
Your Majesty...

XIAO LIZI jumps down, dusting his sleeves as he approaches the cabin.

XIAO LIZI
We've reached Peach Blossom County. The Empress Dowager's envoys won't find you here.

FANG SHANG (late 20s, regal bearing poorly hidden by plain robes) emerges, eyes scanning the street with sharp interest.

FANG SHANG
I came to see the wonders I've heard of—caravan separators, strange laws, and talents that could serve the realm.

XIAO LIZI
As you wish! Onward!

FANG SHANG
(softly)
We're simple travelers, Xiao Lizi. Not an imperial procession.

XIAO LIZI
My apologies, mas— master.

The carriage inches forward through crowds.

XIAO LIZI
Master... there's talk of a woman running this county. Some say she's overturned tradition.

FANG SHANG
That remains to be seen.

FLASHBACK
continuation:
INT. PALACE - DAY (THREE YEARS AGO)

Black and white. FANG SHANG (younger, curious) stands before a glowing bronze vessel. He pins a strange badge to his robe.

The water swirls. XUE FEIFEI (20s, pink bunny pajamas, toothbrush in mouth) emerges.

Their eyes meet.

FANG SHANG
A spirit?

XUE FEIFEI
(amazed)
Time travel—

FANG SHANG
(stumbling back)
Assassin!

He looks back. She's vanished. Only the badge pulses in his hand.

FANG SHANG
(whisper)
A woman from nowhere... or from years ahead?

END FLASHBACK

EXT. PEACH BLOSSOM COUNTY STREET - DAY

FANG SHANG touches his chest where the badge hangs hidden.

FANG SHANG
Drive on.

EXT. COUNTY OFFICE ENTRANCE - DAY

XUE FEIFEI (early 20s, face half-veiled) steps out, reading a bamboo slip. The carriage clips her.

She drops the slip, catching herself mid-fall.

XUE FEIFEI
(rapid-fire)
Ow! Spine, elbow, dignity...

She tests her limbs.

XUE FEIFEI
Still functional.

FANG SHANG and XIAO LIZI approach.

FANG SHANG
You're mobile. Good. Then—

XIAO LIZI
You ran into us deliberately.

XUE FEIFEI
(brandishing the slip)
This is a marked crosswalk. Even merchants must yield.

She points to chalk lines on the ground.

LING'ER (severe bun, watchful eyes) appears at the doorway.

LING'ER
The Magistrate hears disputes at noon. Wait inside.

XUE FEIFEI
Yes.

She turns, veil slipping.

FANG SHANG
That voice—

XIAO LIZI
Master, we should depart—

FANG SHANG
(cutting him off)
This county has... unusual priorities.

LING'ER
Our priorities are fairness. Even nobles must obey the crossing law.

XIAO LIZI
You'd apply law to—the highest authority?

LING'ER
The Magistrate wrote it. All bow to precedent.

INT. COUNTY OFFICE HALL - DAY

XUE FEIFEI has shed her veil. In official robes, she strikes the gavel.

XUE FEIFEI
Court is in session!
(eyes drifting to the doorway)
Bring in the travelers from the street incident.

She fingers her sleeve's hem, where a tiny embroidered rabbit peeks out.

title: The Feisty Pregnant Wife
description: A young village girl's university acceptance letter is burned by her family, who plan to marry her off to pay for her sister's education.
context:
Title: The Feisty Pregnant Wife
Description: A young village girl's university acceptance letter is burned by her family, who plan to marry her off to pay for her sister's education.

EXT. VILLAGE PATH - DAY

Golden afternoon light. SONG AIWEI (18), a determined girl in patched floral clothing, balances a bamboo basket of firewood on her back while clutching a worn math textbook.

She reaches a stone bridge where AUNT WANG and AUNT LIU rest with their hoes.

AUNT WANG
(noticing the book)
Aiwei! The postmaster just came through—university letters arrived. You still waiting on Jiangcheng?

SONG AIWEI
(eyes lighting up)
I am. Thought I'd finish gathering wood first.

AUNT LIU
Go, child. Education won't wait for firewood.

SONG AIWEI
(grateful smile)
Thank you. I'll go now.

She sets down the basket and hurries off, book still in hand.

SONG AIWEI (V.O.)
Two years of studying by lantern light. It has to be enough.

EXT. FARMHOUSE COURTYARD - DAY

MOTHER (40s, tired eyes) stands guard near the door while FATHER (50s, calloused hands) crouches behind the water barrel, feeding papers to a small cookfire.

MOTHER
(low voice)
The postmaster said afternoon. She could be—

FATHER
(quiet, grim)
We finish this now.

SONG AIWEI rounds the corner, breathless.

SONG AIWEI
Did anything come from—

She freezes. Sees the corner of a familiar red-and-white envelope turning to ash.

SONG AIWEI
(voice cracking)
No...

She lunges, grabbing the scorched remnant. The university seal half-visible.

SONG AIWEI
(whisper)
Why?

MOTHER
(stepping forward)
Your sister got her acceptance yesterday. City university. Expensive.

FATHER
(staring at the fire)
Two college girls? We can't.
continuation:
SONG AIWEI
(desperate)
I'll work. Loans. Anything—

MOTHER
(hard voice)
Don't be foolish. Wang Mazi offered sixty thousand. For Yuqing's tuition. Your sister's future.

SONG AIWEI
And mine?

YUQING (17, nervous energy) appears in the doorway, avoiding eye contact. YUHANG (16, sullen) loiters behind her, rope coiled at his waist.

SONG YUHANG
You're the oldest. It's your duty.

SONG AIWEI
(to Yuqing)
You knew?

Song Yuqing flinches but says nothing.

WANG MAZI (40s, limping) enters the courtyard, grin revealing missing teeth.

WANG MAZI
Your parents speak true, girl. You'll be well cared for.

SONG AIWEI
(backing against the wall)
I'd rather die.

MOTHER
Don't be dramatic. You'll thank us—

SONG AIWEI
I won't go!

She makes a break for the gate. Yuhang blocks her. Father grabs her arm.

SONG YUHANG
(getting rope)
Hold her still!

She kicks, struggles. Yuqing watches, trembling.

SONG AIWEI
(to Yuqing)
Help me! Please!

Song Yuqing reaches into her pocket—for tape or something else? Her hand shakes.

Suddenly, Yuqing lunges—not at Aiwei, but at Wang Mazi, shoving him off-balance.

SONG YUQING
(terrified, to Aiwei)
RUN!

Aiwei hesitates only a moment, then bolts past her stunned brother.

Mother screams. Father curses. But Aiwei is already through the gate, the scorched admission letter clutched to her chest.

The half-burned paper flutters from her hand as she flees—she doesn't stop to retrieve it.

FADE OUT.

title: Awakening
description: A talented embroidery master breaks free from decades of family constraints to reclaim her life after winning an international competition.
context:
Title: Awakening
Description: A talented embroidery master breaks free from decades of family constraints to reclaim her life after winning an international competition.

INT. AWARDS CEREMONY VENUE - DAY

Background sound: Stirring music and car engine sounds.

LI XIUFEN, dressed in elegant traditional Chinese clothing, walks on the red carpet holding a crystal trophy, her expression serious. Subtitle introduces: Genius Suzhou Embroidery Master LI XIUFEN.

HOST (V.O.)
Please welcome the champion of this year's International Embroidery Competition, the inheritor of Suzhou embroidery intangible cultural heritage, Ms. Li Xiufen!

LI XIUFEN stands on stage, smiling, accepting photos from the media.

LI XIUFEN (V.O.)
For five decades, I disappeared into obligation.

FLASHBACK MONTAGE

- INT. OLD-STYLE KITCHEN - DAY
  Young LI XIUFEN, dressed plainly in an apron, wearily washes dishes. Embroidery needles are tucked in her hair, hidden.

- INT. OLD-STYLE LIVING ROOM - DAY
  LI XIUFEN busies herself in the living room, wiping tables, tidying clutter. Her hands move with practiced artistry even in this menial work.

LI XIUFEN (V.O.)
I perfected every role except my own.

END MONTAGE

INT. AWARDS CEREMONY VENUE - DAY

LI XIUFEN holds the trophy, her eyes determined.

LI XIUFEN (V.O.)
Today, I stitch my own story.
continuation:
EXT. HOTEL ENTRANCE - DAY

LI XIUFEN walks out the main door. Several BODYGUARDS in black stand respectfully beside a Rolls-Royce—part of the competition's winner's package.

LIU BAOGUO, wearing a security uniform, rushes over and pushes past the bodyguards.

LIU BAOGUO
Xiufen!

LIU QICHENG, Li Xiufen's son, follows closely behind, looking at his mother with desperate frustration.

LIU QICHENG
Mom, the neighbors' committee called three times. My boss saw your interview. Please—just come home and we can talk.

LI XIUFEN
(coldly)
You filed for divorce. You changed the locks. You chose this stage.

LIU BAOGUO
(furiously)
You think this trophy pays bills? You're sixty-two! You can't just—

LIU QICHENG
Mom, your pension is still in our joint account. Don't be reckless.

LIU BAOGUO
You think you're something special? Fine! Let's see you survive without family!

LI XIUFEN turns and walks toward the Rolls-Royce, ignoring them. The BODYGUARDS step forward—professional, not menacing.

BODYGUARD
Sir, this is private transport arranged by the competition. Please step back.

LIU BAOGUO
(struggling)
Forty years! You owe us—

LI XIUFEN gets into the car.

LIU BAOGUO
(shouting)
You're not coming back? Fine! Don't ever come back!

The car door closes, and the car slowly drives away.

LI XIUFEN
(through the car window, speaking softly)
I stopped owing when I started winning.

LIU BAOGUO and LIU QICHENG stand dumbfounded, watching the luxury car drive away.

FLASHBACK

ON SCREEN: TEN YEARS AGO

INT. OLD APARTMENT HALLWAY - DAY

Young LI XIUFEN carries large and small bags, walking with difficulty. A small embroidered bird peeks from one bag—her secret commission.

INT. OLD-STYLE LIVING ROOM - DAY

LI XIUFEN brings the prepared dishes to the table. Young LIU BAOGUO sits at the table, devouring his food while reading the newspaper, his mouth full of grease.

LI XIUFEN changes into a brand new red velvet cheongsam and walks somewhat shyly to LIU BAOGUO.

LI XIUFEN
Baoguo, can I wear this to the birthday banquet tomorrow? I embroidered it myself.

LIU BAOGUO looks up, glaring at her with disgust, and violently slams his glass on the table.

LIU BAOGUO
(roaring)
I told you! No wasting time on vanity! You belong in this kitchen!

LI XIUFEN's smile freezes on her face, her eyes instantly dimming. Her fingers brush the hidden phoenix she embroidered inside the hem—her secret signature.

ON SCREEN: TO BE CONTINUED

title: The Smart Doorbell Mystery
description: A husband sees a disturbing video on his smart doorbell showing a woman who looks like his wife with another man, leading to suspicion and confrontation on their important anniversary night.
context:
Title: The Smart Doorbell Mystery
Description: A husband sees a disturbing video on his smart doorbell showing a woman who looks like his wife with another man, leading to suspicion and confrontation on their important anniversary night.

INT. APARTMENT - NIGHT

HUSBAND bursts through the front door in a panic, phone clutched tight. He scans the room, pulse hammering. Rushing to the bedroom, then bathroom, he finally stops at the closed door.

WIFE walks out wearing a red nightgown.

WIFE
You're home. Finally. Why are you searching the house like a madman?

LELE runs out from the room.

LELE
Mommy?

WIFE hugs LELE.

WIFE
Lele, go back to bed. I told you no more phone tonight.

HUSBAND
Listen to your mother.

After WIFE takes LELE back to the room, she comes out to face HUSBAND.

HUSBAND
(seriously)
Doorbell footage. 8:47 PM. You in the hallway. With him.

WIFE
That's impossible. I've been inside all night. The camera faces out, not in.

HUSBAND's expression becomes grave. He suddenly turns and rushes toward the door. WIFE follows him.
continuation:
INT. APARTMENT ENTRANCE - NIGHT

HUSBAND and WIFE stand in front of the smart doorbell at the entrance. The doorbell screen displays footage of a woman in a red nightgown embracing a man - the hallway identical to theirs. A butterfly tattoo marks her waist.

WIFE
Look at the details. That's not our wallpaper. And the tattoo—do you think I'd hide that from you?

NEWS BROADCAST (V.O.)
Citywide wireless interference reported due to atmospheric conditions. Smart devices receiving crossed signals from nearby units.

HUSBAND takes the doorbell off the wall.

HUSBAND
The apartment across the hall. Same layout. Our signals crossed.

FLASHBACK: HUSBAND returns home carrying a cake and gifts.

INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

HUSBAND and WIFE are in the living room. HUSBAND holds WIFE's hand and apologizes.

HUSBAND
I should have known better. I've been on edge lately.

HUSBAND
Why the nightgown? Were you expecting someone?

WIFE
I was expecting my husband. On our anniversary. Before midnight.

HUSBAND
I told you not to wait. The project—

WIFE
How could I not wait? It's our anniversary.

WIFE
I'm going to shower. Try not to destroy any more electronics.

WIFE turns around. HUSBAND sees kiss marks on her neck and his expression changes.

HUSBAND
(shocked)
Your neck. What are those marks?

ON SCREEN: TO BE CONTINUED

title: My Best Friend Gave Me a Xiuhe Dress Before My Wedding
description: Before her wedding, Chen Jingyi receives a luxurious Chinese wedding dress from her best friend, but memories from her past life reveal a deadly betrayal.
context:
Title: My Best Friend Gave Me a Xiuhe Dress Before My Wedding
Description: Before her wedding, Chen Jingyi receives a luxurious Chinese wedding dress from her best friend, but memories from her past life reveal a deadly betrayal.

INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

CHEN JINGYI, wearing an elegant plain suit, stands in front of a full-length mirror.

YAO QINGQING enters carrying a gorgeous red Chinese Xiuhe wedding dress, accompanied by WANG YANZHE.

YAO QINGQING
Jingyi, for your wedding, I specially hired a designer for over a hundred thousand yuan to custom-make this Chinese Xiuhe dress for you.

CHEN JINGYI (V.O.)
Before my wedding, my best friend spent everything on this Xiuhe dress. But I remember—vividly, impossibly—how this gift once killed me.

YAO QINGQING
Try it on quickly and see if it fits.

CHEN JINGYI (V.O.)
Because in that life, this is how she deceived and framed me.

CHEN JINGYI takes the Xiuhe dress, her expression carefully neutral, but her knuckles whiten.

CHEN JINGYI
No, Qingqing, I can't accept this. You still need treatment. This is all your savings, isn't it?

YAO QINGQING
(fingers trembling against the silk)
Jingyi, I've been diagnosed with late-stage breast cancer. There's no cure. You're my best friend, and my last wish...

YAO QINGQING steps forward and hugs CHEN JINGYI with surprising force.

YAO QINGQING
(whispered)
Is to see you wear the Xiuhe dress I gave you and marry Yanzhe.

WANG YANZHE
(quietly)
Jingyi, accept it. That way Qingqing can have peace of mind.

CHEN JINGYI
(after a long beat)
Alright. I'll go try it on right now.

CHEN JINGYI takes the Xiuhe dress and leaves. YAO QINGQING and WANG YANZHE exchange a glance—relief flickering between them.
continuation:
Later, CHEN JINGYI comes down the stairs wearing the Xiuhe dress, looking radiant.

WANG YANZHE
(admiringly)
You look beautiful.

CHEN JINGYI smiles, then notices YAO QINGQING's collarbone—red and raw where she's scratched a mole until it bled.

CHEN JINGYI
Qingqing! Your wound—I'll get the first aid kit.

YAO QINGQING
(holds her back, nails sharp)
No need. I just saw how beautiful you looked and got overwhelmed. Jingyi, promise me you'll wear this Xiuhe dress and practice more over the next few days to make sure everything is perfect on your wedding day.

As she finishes speaking, CHEN JINGYI's breath hitches. She clutches her chest subtly, hiding the spasm.

CHEN JINGYI (V.O.)
The poison works faster than I remember.

She forces herself upright, meeting their eyes.

CHEN JINGYI
I'll hang it properly upstairs.

She exits calmly, leaving YAO QINGQING and WANG YANZHE staring after her.

INT. HOSPITAL WARD - DAY

CHEN JINGYI lies in the hospital bed, conscious, a phone recording on the side table. WANG YANZHE enters with a DOCTOR.

WANG YANZHE
Are the results in?

DOCTOR
Yes. We've identified a fast-acting toxin absorbed through the skin. The fabric sample she brought in confirmed it.

CHEN JINGYI
(quietly)
Toxin.

She looks at her phone—recording still active. Then at the IV bag, deliberately placed away from her bed.

CHEN JINGYI (V.O.)
They couldn't wait. The same plan, the same dress. This time, I'm the one setting the trap.

title: Rise of the Son-in-Law (Episode 35)
description: A son-in-law with a special constitution is discovered by a martial arts master, while his wife's family plans to force a divorce before a major sect competition.
context:
Title: Rise of the Son-in-Law (Episode 35)
Description: A son-in-law with a special constitution is discovered by a martial arts master, while his wife's family plans to force a divorce before a major sect competition.

EXT. NIGHT

Beneath ancient pine trees, moonlight filters through the canopy. The GRAND UNCLE MASTER, a wandering hermit with wild hair and tattered robes, squats on a fallen log, tearing into a roasted chicken with his fingers.

DISCIPLE
Grand Uncle Master, how does the chicken taste?

GRAND UNCLE MASTER
Mm, delicious. Very good, very good.

DISCIPLE
You've returned from your travels. Do you have any interesting stories to share with this disciple?

GRAND UNCLE MASTER
Stories? Heh. Ever heard of someone whose bones sing when the wind blows through them? The Innate Flawless Body?

DISCIPLE
You mean the legendary constitution where one day of practice equals ten years for others?

GRAND UNCLE MASTER
Hmph. You only know the bedtime story. The truth? Meridians flow backward. Acupoints shift like desert sands. Teach them standard forms, and you'll cripple them for life—make them less than common brawlers. But...

GRAND UNCLE MASTER
(pausing)
...find the right path, the one carved just for them, and they'll leave your so-called geniuses choking on dust. Half a month. That's all it would take to surpass every elder in your precious sect.

DISCIPLE
Then such a talent—please, Grand Uncle Master, bring him to us!

GRAND UNCLE MASTER
Alas. This old man arrived too late. Someone already filled the boy's head with garbage techniques, poisoned his foundation. To make him whole again, he'd have to unlearn everything. Forget every strike, every stance.

DISCIPLE
A tragedy.

GRAND UNCLE MASTER
Tragedy? Perhaps. Or perhaps the boy has his own ideas about being "whole." The time is near. Tonight at the third watch, I keep an appointment. The chicken, though—your salt merchant thanks you. Pay attention next time.

DISCIPLE
This disciple understands.

The Grand Uncle Master tosses the bone into the shadows, wipes his mouth on his sleeve, and melts into the forest.
continuation:
INT. SECT MAIN HALL - NIGHT

Polished jade pillars tower over polished marble floors. The disciple bows before Sect Leader ZHANG JINGHE, a man whose silk robes cannot hide his coiled ambition.

DISCIPLE
Sect Leader.

ZHANG JINGHE
The Supreme Elder never takes disciples. Now that he has the intention to pass on his legacy, we shouldn't let this opportunity go to outsiders. Why not hold a sect competition and select an excellent disciple for him?

DISCIPLE
(nods)

ZHANG JINGHE
I'll arrange it right away. Send invitations to the three great families. Make it known—the prize is not just apprenticeship, but access to the Scripture Pavilion's seventh floor.

INT. XIA FAMILY HALL - NIGHT

The hall drips with conspicuous wealth—gilded lanterns, ivory-inlaid screens, but the air itself feels cold. Calligraphy scrolls boasting "HARMONY" and "PROSPERITY" hang crooked on the walls.

The XIA FAMILY HEAD slams a sect notice onto the rosewood table, his laughter echoing off the expensive decor.

XIA FAMILY HEAD
(laughing loudly)

XIA DALONG
Father, this is wonderful news! In this Mingshan Sect competition, the first place winner will have the chance to become the Supreme Elder's disciple.

XIA ERLONG
Even if we don't take first place, if we get a good ranking, we'll have the chance to catch the eye of the elders, or even the Sect Leader.

XIA FAMILY HEAD
Exactly. This Mingshan Sect internal competition is a great opportunity for the Xia family to bring glory to our name.

XIA ERLONG
Then Third Brother's useless son-in-law must be dealt with before then. His presence is a stain.

XIA DALONG
Every day he remains, our family loses face. Our cousins in the Zhang clan already whisper.

XIA DALONG
(turning to the Xia Family Head)
Don't worry, I've already discussed this with Father.

XIA FAMILY HEAD
Father has his own plan.

XIA YAN and her son-in-law SUI JIN enter the hall. Sui Jin moves with a quiet economy, his threadbare robe a stark contrast to the family's finery. Xia Yan walks beside him, her hand unconsciously protective near his elbow.

XIA YAN
Father, you called for us so urgently. What's the matter?

SUI JIN
(cupping his fists)
Father-in-law.

XIA FAMILY HEAD
Sit down, both of you.

Sui Jin moves toward a sandalwood chair. XIA ERLONG suddenly darts forward, shoving him hard in the chest. Sui Jin catches himself against the wall, his knuckles white against the plaster, but says nothing. Xia Yan's hand flies to her mouth.

XIA ERLONG
You, a son-in-law, have no place to sit here. Go stand in the corner with the servants!

XIA YAN
(angrily)
Since when does the Xia family have such a rule?

XIA ERLONG
Since I decided our reputation matters more than your feelings. What, a useless live-in son-in-law wants to turn the tables and manage Xia family affairs?

XIA YAN
You're going too far! Father, control them!

XIA FAMILY HEAD
(sternly)
Enough. Erlong, Dalong, silence.

Xia Yan rushes to Sui Jin, steadying him with a hand on his shoulder. Her touch lingers, checking for injury.

XIA FAMILY HEAD
I called you back because the sect competition changes everything. Sui Jin.

SUI JIN
Father-in-law.

XIA FAMILY HEAD
Xia Yan.

XIA YAN
Father.

XIA FAMILY HEAD
I want the two of you to divorce immediately. The papers are prepared. Sign them, and a thousand spirit stones will compensate you for your trouble, Sui Jin. Consider it a... severance package.

Both XIA YAN and SUI JIN freeze. Sui Jin's gaze drops to the floor, not in submission, but as if seeing something no one else can. Xia Yan's face drains of color, her hand tightening on his arm.

title: Married Into Wealth, The Poor Girl Is Spoiled to Heaven (Episode 79)
description: A young woman wakes up in a hospital to discover she is pregnant with triplets after a fateful encounter with a mysterious man.
context:
Title: Married Into Wealth, The Poor Girl Is Spoiled to Heaven (Episode 79)
Description: A young woman wakes up in a hospital to discover she is pregnant with triplets after a fateful encounter with a mysterious man.

INT. HOSPITAL WARD - DAY

WEN YOU lies in a hospital bed, slowly opens her eyes, and sits up.

A NURSE pushes the door open and enters with a clipboard.

NURSE
Morning. Your labs are back.

The NURSE hands WEN YOU an ultrasound photo.

NURSE
Three distinct heartbeats. Quite a surprise.

WEN YOU stares at the image, frozen.

NURSE
We need an emergency contact. Family? Partner?

WEN YOU suddenly crumples the photo, voice shaking.

WEN YOU
No!

WEN YOU
(pleading)
Please. No calls. My family will—

She cuts off, terror raw in her eyes.

TRANSITION: One month ago
continuation:
INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - DAY (FLASHBACK)

WEN YOU, wearing a green dress, walks through the hospital corridor, checking a room number on a scrap of paper.

INT. HOSPITAL WARD - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)

The ward is dimly lit. WEN YOU carefully pushes the door open and peeks inside.

WEN YOU
(softly)
Mom? Are you here?

WEN YOU enters the ward and closes the door. As she turns around, she collides with LU HUO CHEN, a man wearing glasses with a dazed look in his eyes. He steadies himself against the bedframe, breathing hard.

WEN YOU
(startled)
Wrong room. I'm sorry—

LU HUO CHEN's hand catches her arm, his grip firm but not cruel. His gaze is feverish, searching.

LU HUO CHEN
Tell me you weren't sent.

WEN YOU
(frightened)
I'm looking for my mother. Please let go.

LU HUO CHEN releases her with a bitter laugh, pressing his palm to his forehead. His tie hangs loose.

LU HUO CHEN
The board's final move. They'll have their scandal.

He sways. WEN YOU instinctively steadies him. Their eyes meet—his clouded with pain, hers with reluctant understanding.

LU HUO CHEN
(tugging at his tie)
Run. Now.

WEN YOU doesn't move. The moment stretches.

LU HUO CHEN
...or stay.

He leans in. She doesn't pull away.

CLOSE-UP: Their hands tightly clasped together on the bedsheet. Text appears on screen: "THREE HEARTBEATS BEGIN."

TRANSITION: TO BE CONTINUED

title: A Son's Lack of Filial Piety Is Not the Father's Fault
description: A dying father with brain cancer is abandoned by his older children, but finds hope in his youngest son who promises to care for him.
context:
Title: A Son's Lack of Filial Piety Is Not the Father's Fault
Description: A dying father with brain cancer is abandoned by his older children, but finds hope in his youngest son who promises to care for him.

INT. HOSPITAL WARD - DAY

A heart monitor beeps rhythmically.

ZHANG FUGUO lies in the hospital bed, eyes closed, receiving an IV drip.

In the ward are his eldest son ZHANG DASHAN, second daughter ZHANG YUHU, and third son ZHANG XIAOCHUAN. ZHANG DASHAN sits on another bed, scrolling through his phone with tight shoulders. ZHANG YUHU stands by the window, checking her watch. ZHANG XIAOCHUAN leans against the wall, his phone dark in his hand.

ZHANG DASHAN
Project meeting's in two hours.

ZHANG YUHU
Mom's picking up Yuanyuan at three.

ZHANG XIAOCHUAN
Still no signal in here.

DOCTOR (O.S.)
Family members?

The DOCTOR appears at the ward door, holding a medical chart.

DOCTOR
Could we talk for a moment?

ZHANG DASHAN
Is it serious?

ZHANG YUHU
His blood pressure again?

ZHANG XIAOCHUAN
Just tell us here, Doctor.

DOCTOR
(consulting the chart)
The MRI shows a mass. In his brain.

ZHANG YUHU
A tumor?

DOCTOR
We'll need a biopsy. But I'd like to discuss treatment options.

ZHANG DASHAN
Treatment... meaning surgery?

ZHANG XIAOCHUAN
How much would that cost?

ZHANG YUHU
My husband's company just downsized.

ZHANG DASHAN
Tuition's due next month. Both kids.

ZHANG XIAOCHUAN
I have a job interview tomorrow.

DOCTOR
This is a discussion for the family. I'll give you some time.

(The DOCTOR leaves. Silence.)

ZHANG YUHU
Big brother, your company—

ZHANG DASHAN
Cash flow's tight. You know expansion drained everything.

ZHANG XIAOCHUAN
Yuanyuan's parents already think I'm not good enough. If they find out—

ZHANG YUHU
(checking her phone)
Dad could stay with you. Just until we figure something out.

ZHANG DASHAN
continuation:
Mei-Ling's parents live with us. You know that.

ZHANG YUHU
(softly)
Third brother, what about your place?

ZHANG XIAOCHUAN
My roommate's allergic to everything. Dad would hate it.

ZHANG YUHU
Xiaohai then?

ZHANG XIAOCHUAN
He hasn't answered.

ZHANG DASHAN
Call me after the biopsy. We'll... talk.

(ZHANG DASHAN exits. ZHANG YUHU looks at her phone.)

ZHANG YUHU
Daycare closes in an hour. Can you stay?

ZHANG XIAOCHUAN
It's my first interview in six months.

ZHANG YUHU
Then... we'll split a nurse? Fifty-fifty?

ZHANG XIAOCHUAN
I don't have it, sister. I'm sorry.

(ZHANG XIAOCHUAN leaves. ZHANG YUHU watches him go, then looks at her father.)

ZHANG YUHU
(whispering)
Dad, I... I'm sorry.

(She hesitates, then follows her brothers out.)

(Only ZHANG FUGUO and the hospital equipment remain.)

ZHANG FUGUO slowly opens his eyes, tears welling up. He stares at the empty doorway.

ZHANG XIAOHAI (O.S.)
Excuse me.

ZHANG XIAOHAI appears at the door in a delivery uniform, breathless, holding his phone.

ZHANG XIAOHAI
Dad. I came as soon as—

(He sees his father's face.)

ZHANG XIAOHAI
Did they already leave?

ZHANG FUGUO
(whispering)
You have a wedding to save for.

ZHANG XIAOHAI
(quietly)
She'll understand. Or she won't.

(He kneels beside the bed.)

ZHANG XIAOHAI
You're my father.

ZHANG FUGUO
I didn't want to be a burden.

ZHANG XIAOHAI
You're not.

(They sit in silence as the heart monitor continues its steady rhythm.)

EXT. STREET - DAY (FLASHBACK)

Young ZHANG XIAOHAI, exhausted from work, carries a sleeping ZHANG FUGUO on his back down a quiet street. His grip tightens to keep his father from slipping. He smiles faintly, despite the weight.

title: The Lone Brave
description: A determined athlete faces discrimination and challenges as he strives to prove his worth against foreign competitors.
context:
Title: The Lone Brave
Description: A determined athlete faces discrimination and challenges as he strives to prove his worth against foreign competitors.

INT. STADIUM - DAY

A packed stadium roars as athletes line up. Flags wave—some Daxian, some foreign.

The crowd's chant builds rhythmically.

REFEREE
On your marks!

Close on LIU CHANGHE (23), muscles taut, eyes fixed on the finish line. There's something laser-focused, almost isolating, about his concentration.

The starting pistol cracks. Liu explodes forward, his form perfect.

AUDIENCE
(chanting)
Chang-he! Chang-he!

COMMENTATOR (V.O.)
And Liu Changhe surges ahead—but Meichuan is closing!

Liu lunges across the finish line—first, but barely. He immediately turns, searching the crowd, his "victory" face already collapsing into something more complicated.

EXT. TRAINING TRACK - DAY

THREE MONTHS EARLIER. The track is cracked concrete, weeds sprouting through.

Liu crosses an imaginary finish line, breathing hard. He checks his own stopwatch—disappointment flickers, then resolve.

His teammates watch from the sideline.

WU (O.S.)
Again?

Liu nods, already resetting.

TEAMMATE A (LIN, 22) holds a phone timer, tries to hide a wince.

LIN
(off the phone)
Ten point nine... uh, point nine eight.

Liu's jaw tightens. Not quite enough.

TEAMMATE B (WU, 24)
That's still faster than my morning commute.

WU tosses him a water bottle. Liu catches it without looking.

LIU CHANGHE
(quietly)
Joint Games qualifying is ten point eight five.

TEAMMATE C (MEI, 20)
You'll get there. You always do.

They share a look—less certainty than she's trying to project.

MEI
Besides, selection isn't just about times. Director Zhao—

WU
(cutting her off)
—has his own "selection process."

The air sours. Liu drinks, saying nothing.
continuation:
EXT. TRAINING TRACK - DAY

Same day. A polished sedan pulls up. DIRECTOR ZHAO (50s, always sweating) leads KAWASAKI (60s, sharp suit) and MEICHUAN (25, Olympic rings tattoo) across the track.

ZHAO
(to Kawasaki)
As you can see, we've made improvements since your last visit. New facilities in the annex.

KAWASAKI
(glancing at the cracked track)
Improvements? This is what Daxia calls progress?

Meichuan hangs back, studying Liu's team training with professional interest.

ZHAO
(nervous laugh)
We're still developing, yes. But our athletes show promise—

KAWASAKI
Promise doesn't win medals, Director. Our biomechanics lab offered you funding. You refused.

ZHAO
It was... politically sensitive. The Joint Games committee—

KAWASAKI
—prefers winners. Meichuan needs real competition, not...

He gestures at Liu's group, one finger circling the air.

KAWASAKI
...rehearsals.

Liu, within earshot, stops mid-stretch. His teammates freeze.

LIN
(quietly, to Liu)
Don't.

LIU CHANGHE
(stands, to Zhao—not Kawasaki)
Director, we're conducting timed trials today. If Meichuan-san needs competition...

He bows slightly to Meichuan, not Kawasaki.

LIU CHANGHE
...we would be honored.

Meichuan returns the bow, subtle but genuine. Kawasaki's smile doesn't reach his eyes.

KAWASAKI
(to Zhao)
How... quaint. A demonstration.

Zhao mops his forehead, trapped between factions.

ZHAO
(to Liu, voice low)
This isn't your concern, Changhe.

LIU CHANGHE
(meeting his gaze)
Then whose is it?

The question hangs. Not defiance—genuine confusion. Zhao has no answer.

title: All In: The Woman Who Disappeared (Episode 72)
description: A man discovers a horrifying truth about his lover's heart transplant and its connection to his missing girlfriend.
context:
Title: All In: The Woman Who Disappeared (Episode 72)
Description: A man discovers a horrifying truth about his lover's heart transplant and its connection to his missing girlfriend.

INT. HOT SPRING/BOTANICAL GARDEN - DAY

Steam drifts through dense foliage. DU SHA, in a white chiffon dress, walks toward the hot spring pool, fingers absently tracing the scar hidden beneath her collarbone.

CUT TO:

A DONG, wearing a black leather jacket, moves through the garden. He glances at a worn photograph—XIAO YU—then continues forward with hollow eyes.

Du Sha sinks into the steaming water, letting out a quiet breath of relief.

A Dong stops, peering through the leaves. His gaze fixes on Du Sha's left shoulder: a crescent birthmark above her surgical scar.

A MYSTERIOUS WOMAN in a leather jacket appears.

MYSTERIOUS WOMAN
Six months. You still carry her picture?

A DONG
The clinic in Macau. What did you find?

MYSTERIOUS WOMAN
Dr. Chen's last patient. Emergency transplant. No donor on record.

MYSTERIOUS WOMAN
(leaning in, quiet)
The heart arrived the day Xiao Yu disappeared.

A DONG
(turning on her)
Why didn't you tell me?

MYSTERIOUS WOMAN
Would you have believed me?

Du Sha's voice echoes across the pool.

DU SHA
A Dong? I can feel you out there.

A DONG
(calling)
Wait for me.
continuation:
MYSTERIOUS WOMAN
(grabbing his arm)
She doesn't know. The heart will reject—

A DONG
(shoves her off)
It's already rejecting.

A Dong staggers toward the pool, clutching his chest.

A DONG (V.O.)
Xiao Yu...

DU SHA (V.O.)
(whispered)
...don't leave me.

A Dong's head jerks up. Du Sha stands in the shallow water, hand pressed to her chest, Xiao Yu's eyes staring back.

DU SHA
A Dong, my heart hurts. Does yours?

A Dong stares at the scar. His face empties.

FLASHBACK: XIAO YU on an operating table, screaming through a gag: "Let me go!"

FLASHBACK: A DOCTOR's hands hold a bloody heart.

A DONG
(voice breaking)
That heart... it's beating her fear.

DU SHA
(surprised)
What?

A DONG
(coldly)
You're lying. Your heart is as cold and hard as stone. How could it feel pain? No, you don't have a heart at all. You're the one who killed Xiao Yu.

A DONG's eyes turn vicious.

A DONG
(through gritted teeth)
I'm going to kill you.

title: A Lone Boat Carrying Dreams Across the Starry River
description: A woman disappears after a staged accident to escape her betrayers and returns transformed to dismantle those who wronged her.
context:
Title: A Lone Boat Carrying Dreams Across the Starry River
Description: A woman disappears after a staged accident to escape her betrayers and returns transformed to dismantle those who wronged her.

INT. MANSION CORRIDOR - NIGHT

A magnificent deep red door is slowly pushed open.

LI MENGYAO, wearing a black evening gown and cascading dark hair that conceals her features, moves with deliberate grace. Her posture alone commands attention.

She walks forward along a white marble corridor, where shadows pool between the recessed lighting.

LI MENGYAO (V.O.)
They buried a ghost six months ago. Tonight, the ghost collects debts.

INT. MEMORIAL HALL - DAY

A solemn memorial gathering. The centerpiece is a framed photograph—Li Mengyao's former self, tired and dowdy in an ill-fitting dress, her face blurred by movement.

A plaque reads: "In Memory of Li Mengyao, Devoted Mother."

XU LINGCHEN and a circle of associates in muted tones stand before it, their grief performative.

LI MENGYAO observes from a secluded balcony, her face partially obscured by a wide-brimmed hat.

LI MENGYAO (V.O.)
They held this ceremony before the fire even cooled. Now I'll show them how ghosts are made.

TRANSITION: One Year Ago
continuation:
EXT. STARLIGHT KINDERGARTEN - DAY

Sunny day. Parents gather at the gates, a tableau of modern perfection.

LI MENGYAO, exhausted in worn clothes, hair scraped back, clutches a homemade snack bag.

Children stream out. KEKE emerges, his eyes scanning past her.

LI MENGYAO
Keke! I made your favorite—

KEKE
(quietly, without looking at her)
The nanny's supposed to get me. You're embarrassing.

He drops the snack bag. It spills on the pavement. Li Mengyao's face crumples as other parents glance, then politely look away.

She checks her phone. A news notification glows: "Xu Lingchen: Tech Titan of Longdu Reveals Secret to Success—Family First."

She taps it. Xu Lingchen's interview plays.

XU LINGCHEN (ON SCREEN)
(real smile, eyes distant)
The woman who believed in me when I had nothing... she deserves everything.

LI MENGYAO
(whispering)
He still remembers. We started in a basement apartment.

Her phone buzzes with a text from XU LINGCHEN: "Emergency board meeting. Can't make Keke's recital. You understand."

She types: "Of course," then deletes it, replaces it with: "We'll celebrate after."

A shadow crosses her face.

EXT. MANSION GARDEN - DAY

Li Mengyao leads Keke through the side entrance, avoiding the main foyer. She spots unfamiliar silk scarves draped over the garden chair—a vibrant color she'd never wear.

INT. PRIVATE STUDY - DAY

LI MENGYAO places Keke's bag down. A muffled laugh echoes from the main bedroom above.

She pads upstairs, each step careful.

INT. MASTER BEDROOM DOORWAY - DAY

Through the cracked door: FANG YUNROU—not young, but sharp and tailored, her confidence like armor—sits on the edge of the bed fastening a Cartier watch. XU LINGCHEN hands her a glass of champagne.

FANG YUNROU
The board's impatient. They want a proper partner at these events, not a ghost from your past.

XU LINGCHEN
She raised me when I was nothing.

FANG YUNROU
(places hand on his chest)
And now I'll make you a dynasty. That's worth more than nostalgia.

LI MENGYAO's phone slips from her hand. It clatters on the hardwood.

Both heads turn toward the door.

title: Child Waiting for Emergency Treatment While You Accompany Your First Love
description: A father discovers his wife is meeting her ex-boyfriend on their daughter's birthday, raising suspicions about their relationship.
context:
Title: Child Waiting for Emergency Treatment While You Accompany Your First Love
Description: A father discovers his wife is meeting her ex-boyfriend on their daughter's birthday, raising suspicions about their relationship.

INT. LUXURIOUS LIVING ROOM - DAY

LELE lies on the floor coloring. Her hand trembles.

LIANG ZI walks into the living room.

LIANG ZI
Lele.

LELE looks up and tries to stand, wobbling slightly.

LELE
Daddy! You're home early.

LIANG ZI steadies her, his smile strained.

LIANG ZI
For your birthday? Always.

MOM walks over from the side, carrying a covered medicine tray.

MOM
Dinner's ready. I made the boiled shrimp.

INT. DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS

LIANG ZI puts LELE in a chair and sits down. The table holds a birthday cake and a large platter of boiled shrimp arranged in a distinct pattern.

LIANG ZI
Lele, where's Mommy?

LELE
Mommy's not home.

LIANG ZI takes out his phone. A text from his wife: "Emergency at the clinic. Start without me."

LIANG ZI
Mommy had an emergency?

LELE
She said she's helping Uncle Zi Cong.

LIANG ZI
(quietly)
Uncle Zi Cong.
continuation:
MOM comes over carrying the shrimp.

MOM
Liang Zi, where's Xiao Lin? I made it the way she insisted—the special arrangement.

LIANG ZI looks at the shrimp, his expression darkening.

The door opens. XIAO LIN walks in, wearing a red dress and clutching a medical file.

MOM
Xiao Lin's back.

XIAO LIN
Mom, I'm not eating.

LIANG ZI stands.

LIANG ZI
Emergency at the clinic?

XIAO LIN
What?

LIANG ZI
With Zhao Zi Cong?

XIAO LIN's face pales.

XIAO LIN
You don't understand. He's back because—

LELE runs over and grabs XIAO LIN's hand, gasping for breath.

LELE
Mommy... I can't...

XIAO LIN drops the file. Papers scatter, revealing ZHAO ZI CONG's name on a medical chart.

XIAO LIN
Her medication—I left it in his car—

She turns to run out. LIANG ZI grabs her arm, his face stricken.

LIANG ZI
You left her medicine with him?

LELE slumps to the floor.

BLACKOUT.

title: Falling for My Younger Lover
description: A wealthy young man searches for the woman who helped him three years ago, only to find an unexpected connection that challenges their age difference.
context:
Title: Falling for My Younger Lover
Description: A wealthy young man searches for the woman who helped him three years ago, only to find an unexpected connection that challenges their age difference.

INT. HIGHWAY - DAY

Fast-paced music plays.

JIANG HANYU, wearing racing gear and a helmet, rides a heavy motorcycle speeding down the highway. He executes a perfect drift and stops.

He removes his helmet, revealing his handsome face. A faded scar near his left temple hints at a past he doesn't advertise.

Several black sedans arrive and stop. ASSISTANT LI and a group of BODYGUARDS exit, standing respectfully in a row.

BODYGUARDS (in unison)
Welcome home, sir!

JIANG HANYU
Assistant Li. Tell me you've found her.

ASSISTANT LI
Song Yan, Longchuan TV investigative journalist. Tonight at Hanting Bar. She's working undercover on the financial piece.

JIANG HANYU
Then I'll go find her now.

ASSISTANT LI
But the old lady has arranged a matchmaking banquet—

JIANG HANYU
—is every week. This is three years coming.

He pulls his helmet back on. The engine roars as he speeds away.

EXT. STREET - THREE YEARS AGO

Flashback to three years ago.

Rain drizzles on an empty side street. YOUNG JIANG HANYU, early twenties, soaked and shivering, sits on concrete steps with a cracked phone and empty wallet beside him. His expensive watch hangs loose on his wrist.

A PASSERBY glances at him.

PASSERBY
Could call someone. Family?

JIANG HANYU (looking up, defensive)
Phone's dead. And I don't have any family left to call.

The Passerby hurries away.

SONG YAN, mid-twenties, appears under a broken streetlamp with an umbrella and takeout container. She notices his watch, his posture—someone who had everything and lost it in a day.

SONG YAN
(sitting beside him)
Pork ramen from the place around the corner. Still hot.

She slips folded bills into his jacket pocket and wraps her scarf around his neck. It's striped blue and white.

SONG YAN
(not meeting his eyes)
Just pay it forward someday.

She stands to leave. He grabs her wrist instinctively.

JIANG HANYU
I don't even know your name.

SONG YAN
(turning back, smiling faintly)
The one who should have checked the weather report.

She touches his wet hair lightly, then disappears into the rain.

JIANG HANYU (V.O.)
The teardrop mole at the corner of her eye. How did I not notice?

He touches the scarf. It's still warm.

INT. HIGHWAY - DAY

Cut back to the present.

ASSISTANT LI
She's investigating money laundering at Hanting. Undercover. Be careful she doesn't mistake you for a story.

JIANG HANYU
Then I won't give her a story. I'll give her the truth.
continuation:
INT. BAR - NIGHT

Hanting Bar pulses with electronic music. SMOKE MACHINES obscure visibility.

SONG YAN, wearing a red slip dress that doesn't quite fit her style, sits at the bar with a club soda disguised as gin. A hidden camera pen clips to her clutch. She watches a group of suits in the VIP section.

SONG YAN (to herself)
Come on, Zhao. One incriminating conversation. That's all I need.

A hand reaches past her for a napkin. The wrist wears a vintage watch—familiar. She looks up.

JIANG HANYU, in a white silk shirt and black vest, meets her gaze. The scar on his temple. The confident posture. Nothing like the broken boy from the rain.

But the eyes are the same.

SONG YAN (V.O.)
Focus, Yan. Not another handsome distraction.

JIANG HANYU
Are you... waiting for someone?

SONG YAN
(sly smile)
Depends. Are you someone?

Later, in a dark corner booth. She shows him her press pass, hidden in her clutch.

SONG YAN
Promise you won't tell my editor I'm fraternizing with the target demographic.

JIANG HANYU
Your secret's safe with me.

He touches the corner of his eye instinctively. She notices the gesture, but can't place why it matters.

As they leave together, rain begins outside—just like three years ago. She shivers. He drapes his jacket over her shoulders.

The gesture stops her cold. She studies his face in the neon light. Recognition flickers.

SONG YAN
(quietly)
Have we...?

JIANG HANYU
(softly)
Not yet. But we could.

He leans in. She lets him. The kiss is soft, questioning. Not the transaction she expected.

INT. HOTEL LOBBY - NIGHT

They stumble in, drenched from the run through rain. The desk clerk raises an eyebrow. Jiang slides a credit card across the marble.

INT. HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT/MORNING

The room is modern, impersonal. Rain streaks the floor-to-ceiling windows.

On the bed, they pause. Her dress strap slides down her shoulder. His fingers trace it.

SONG YAN
(between kisses)
How old are you?

JIANG HANYU
Twenty.

She stills completely.

SONG YAN
Twenty.

(a beat)
God, when I graduated university, you were still in high school.

JIANG HANYU
Does it matter?

SONG YAN
It matters that I almost—

She pushes back, sitting up. The bedsheet falls. She's suddenly vulnerable.

SONG YAN
I have rules about this. About... everything.

JIANG HANYU
(sitting up too)
I know.

SONG YAN
(frowning)
How could you possibly—

JIANG HANYU
Because three years ago, you taught me that sometimes the people who save you don't even know they did.

He reaches for his discarded jacket, pulls out a folded, faded scarf. Blue and white stripes.

Her breath catches. She touches the corner of her eye.

SONG YAN
(whisper)
That was you. The boy in the rain.

JIANG HANYU
I'm not a boy anymore.

She looks at him—the man he's become.

SONG YAN
(stronger now)
Then prove it. Tell me your real name.

JIANG HANYU
Jiang Hanyu. I have a penthouse, a corporation, and a grandmother who wants to marry me off. But the only person I want...

He touches her cheek. The teardrop mole.

JIANG HANYU
...is the one who saw me when I was invisible.

A long beat. The rain stops outside.

SONG YAN
(standing, dressing)
Then you need to leave.

JIANG HANYU
What?

SONG YAN
Not because you're younger. Because I'm not some fantasy you built up. I'm messy. I'm a journalist who takes stupid risks. I don't do penthouses or grandmothers or...

She stops, fully dressed now. Picks up her hidden pen from the nightstand.

SONG YAN
...or men who might be the story I'm investigating.

JIANG HANYU
(realizing)
The money laundering.

SONG YAN
(sad smile)
See? You're quick. That's dangerous.

She heads for the door. He follows, still shirtless.

INT. HOTEL HALLWAY - MORNING

JIANG HANYU chases her, holding her shoes.

JIANG HANYU
Song Yan!

She stops. Turns.

JIANG HANYU
If I'm the story, investigate me. But don't run because you're scared I might be real.

He holds out her shoes.

She takes them slowly.

SONG YAN
Meet me tomorrow. Public place. You bring your financials, I'll bring my questions.

JIANG HANYU
Deal.

She walks away, but slower now. The shoes dangle from her fingers. He watches until she turns the corner, then touches the corner of his eye—where her teardrop mole would be.

The door clicks shut behind him.

title: Doomed, I'm Surrounded by Them (Episode 82)
description: Pan Xiaoxian, inheritor of ancient Chinese medical arts, must hide his identity for three years while working as a ride-share driver in Beijing, only to get entangled with a poisoned heiress.
context:
Title: Doomed, I'm Surrounded by Them (Episode 82)
Description: Pan Xiaoxian, inheritor of ancient Chinese medical arts, must hide his identity for three years while working as a ride-share driver in Beijing, only to get entangled with a poisoned heiress.

EXT. MOUNTAIN ROAD - NIGHT

A black sedan speeds along a winding mountain road.

ON SCREEN: PAN XIAOXIAN
ON SCREEN: INHERITOR OF ANCIENT CHINESE MEDICAL ARTS

PAN XIAOXIAN (V.O.)
My name is Pan Xiaoxian, inheritor of ancient Chinese medical arts. At eighteen, I descended from the mountain to heal the world with my medical skills.

INT. PAN XIAOXIAN'S CAR - NIGHT

PAN XIAOXIAN drives with quiet confidence, his face illuminated by passing streetlights. Brief, dreamlike images flash: golden needles, a corporate logo, a woman's silhouette.

PAN XIAOXIAN (V.O.)
Money, power, and the freedom to choose your own road.

PAN XIAOXIAN's expression shifts, the bravado fading into something more resigned.

PAN XIAOXIAN (V.O.)
Master's condition: three years in the mortal world, unable to reveal my identity.

PAN XIAOXIAN
(to camera)
So everything you just imagined? I have none of it.

The camera pulls back through the window to reveal the ordinary ride-share car navigating city streets.

PAN XIAOXIAN (V.O.)
To survive, I became a Beijing drifter.

PAN XIAOXIAN turns to the passenger with a weary but genuine smile.

PAN XIAOXIAN
Hello, passenger. I'm happy to serve you.

In the passenger seat, SONG YANRAN is crying, but her tears are silent, controlled. She clutches her purse like a shield.

PAN XIAOXIAN
Miss?

ON SCREEN: SONG YANRAN
ON SCREEN: HEIR TO SONG CORPORATION

SONG YANRAN
Can you drive faster?

PAN XIAOXIAN
Sure thing!

PAN XIAOXIAN (V.O.)
Four months in. Sometimes I forget who I was supposed to be.
continuation:
EXT. HOTEL ENTRANCE - NIGHT

LIN AODONG rushes out, his expensive suit disheveled, eyes scanning the street with predatory frustration.

ON SCREEN: LIN AODONG
ON SCREEN: SONG YANRAN'S FIANCÉ

LIN AODONG
That woman got away again!

INT. PAN XIAOXIAN'S CAR - NIGHT

The car moves through traffic. SONG YANRAN's controlled exterior cracks—she presses her fingers to her temples, a flush spreading across her neck.

SONG YANRAN
(moaning)
Mmm... so hot... I... I need...

She lunges forward, her hand landing on the steering wheel with surprising force.

PAN XIAOXIAN
What are you doing! Hey!

The car swerves sharply, stopping at the roadside, hazard lights blinking.

PAN XIAOXIAN
Are you trying to get us killed!

SONG YANRAN
Let go of me!

PAN XIAOXIAN notices her symptoms: the flushed skin, dilated pupils, labored breathing. He hesitates, then gently but firmly takes her wrist, feeling her pulse.

PAN XIAOXIAN
(to himself)
Hehuan Powder? But the synthesis requires...

SONG YANRAN
I need...

PAN XIAOXIAN
You've been poisoned. I can save you.

SONG YANRAN suddenly slaps PAN XIAOXIAN—not hard, but enough to shock. Then she grabs his face, her feverish eyes desperate.

PAN XIAOXIAN
What are you doing?

SONG YANRAN
Don't move.

SONG YANRAN kisses PAN XIAOXIAN. The screen goes black.

INT. PAN XIAOXIAN'S CAR - NIGHT

SONG YANRAN straightens her clothes and opens the car door. PAN XIAOXIAN slumps in the driver's seat, his face and collar covered with lipstick marks and a faint scratch.

SONG YANRAN takes out a stack of cash from her bag and throws it at PAN XIAOXIAN.

SONG YANRAN
I'm warning you, forget this ever happened.

SONG YANRAN gets out of the car, closes the door, and leaves.

PAN XIAOXIAN
(moaning in pain)
So what if you're rich? Don't let me see you again!

PAN XIAOXIAN sits up and sees money that fell on the ground. He glances after her retreating figure, conflicted.

PAN XIAOXIAN
Oh, there's more here.

He opens the car door and crouches down to pick up the money on the ground.

EXT. ROAD - NIGHT

PAN XIAOXIAN crouches in the middle of the road picking up money. A car speeds toward him, its blinding headlights shining on him.

PAN XIAOXIAN looks up, his expression terrified.

A flash of bright light crosses the screen.

title: Divorce at the Civil Affairs Bureau
description: A woman attempts to divorce her abusive, gambling husband but is stopped by her parents who care more about reputation than her safety.
context:
Title: Divorce at the Civil Affairs Bureau
Description: A woman attempts to divorce her abusive, gambling husband but is stopped by her parents who care more about reputation than her safety.

INT. CIVIL AFFAIRS BUREAU - DAY

SHEN ANNING (30s, quiet strength) and SONG PEIYANG (40s, leashed menace) sit at the divorce counter. Two certificates await the stamp.

CLERK
Have you two thought this through? Are you sure you want a divorce?

SHEN ANNING
Yes.

The Clerk raises the stamp. It begins to descend—

ANNING'S FATHER and ANNING'S MOTHER rush in, breathless.

ANNING'S FATHER
(shouting)
Stop!

SHEN ANNING
(whisper)
No...

Anning's Father lunges, gripping her wrist.

ANNING'S FATHER
Ungrateful! Get up!

ANNING'S MOTHER
(to Anning)
The neighbors whisper. What will they say?

ANNING'S FATHER
Shame!

SONG PEIYANG watches, arms crossed, a small smile.

SHEN ANNING
(to her parents)
Your reputation. Over my life?

She yanks up her sleeve, revealing fresh bruises above yellowing ones.

SHEN ANNING
He beat me for hours last week. Gambled away our rent. I won't survive this.

ANNING'S MOTHER
(eyes downcast)
When you marry a chicken, you follow the chicken.

SONG PEIYANG steps forward, smooth.

SONG PEIYANG
Dad, Mom. I'm ashamed she troubled you. I've been stressed, but I love her—

ANNING'S FATHER
(to Anning)
No divorce. I will disown you.
continuation:
SHEN ANNING
Mom, please...

ANNING'S MOTHER
Behave.

She pulls her husband back.

SONG PEIYANG
(waving)
Dad, Mom, thanks.

He turns to Anning, dropping pretense.

SONG PEIYANG
I warned you.

SHEN ANNING
You called them.

SONG PEIYANG
Your phone. While you slept.

He extends his palm.

SONG PEIYANG
Salary. Now.

Anning stares at his hand. Then reaches into her purse—but pulls out her phone.

SHEN ANNING
Show me the text. Or I'll show them the photos. The hospital report.

Song's smile vanishes. The Clerk watches, alert.

CLERK
(quietly)
Madam, we have a safe room.

Anning's parents hover in the doorway, doubt on their faces.

ANNING'S MOTHER
Anning... photos?

SHEN ANNING
(to Clerk)
Please continue.

The stamp falls. Anning exhales.

SONG PEIYANG
(furious)
This isn't over—

SHEN ANNING
It is. I made copies.

She holds up a USB drive, walks out past her parents.

A bright light flashes—the Bureau's security camera.

Anning doesn't look back.

title: Six-and-a-Half-Year-Old Twin Treasures at Home
description: A young woman agrees to bear a child for a wealthy man in exchange for money to save her mother, but her life takes a tragic turn when her family betrays her.
context:
Title: Six-and-a-Half-Year-Old Twin Treasures at Home
Description: A young woman agrees to bear a child for a wealthy man in exchange for money to save her mother, but her life takes a tragic turn when her family betrays her.

INT. LUXURIOUS BEDROOM - NIGHT

FANG SITONG adjusts a black dress before a mirror. Her hand trembles applying lipstick. A wine glass sits untouched.

QIN YI in an open white shirt, watches from the bed.

QIN YI
Five million. One child.

FANG SITONG
(quietly)
After?

QIN YI
Clean break. That's the contract.

She touches the glass, seeing her mother's reflection there.

FANG SITONG
(after a beat)
She needs surgery by Friday.

QIN YI
Then we begin tonight.

He approaches. Their eyes meet in the mirror.

TITLE CARD: EIGHT MONTHS LATER

INT. BURNING ROOM - NIGHT

Smoke fills a locked bedroom. FANG SITONG, now heavily pregnant, pounds the door.

FANG SITONG
(coughing)
It's jammed!

Through the keyhole: STEPMOTHER and FANG YINGYING watch smoke seep under the door.

STEPMOTHER
(to Fang Yingying)
Shares transfer when she's declared dead. Your father made certain.

FANG SITONG freezes. Presses her ear to the wood.

FANG YINGYING
She never wondered why Dad favored his "stepdaughter"?

FANG SITONG
(whispered)
No...

She clutches her stomach. A contraction ripples across it.

FANG SITONG
(to herself)
Mom's waiting. Not like this.

She grabs a candlestick, hurls it at the window. Glass shatters.
continuation:
AUNT SONG appears below with a ladder.

AUNT SONG
Miss! Climb!

FANG SITONG struggles through the frame as flames erupt behind her.

AUNT SONG
Hurry!

She descends rung by rung. Reaches the ground.

AUNT SONG rushes toward the house.

FANG SITONG
Aunt Song, no!

AUNT SONG
The contract! In your bag!

She disappears inside. A moment later, the roof collapses.

FANG SITONG
(collapsing)
No!

She doubles over, gripping her belly.

FANG SITONG
(strangled)
The twins...

INT. QIN YI'S MANSION - DAY

QIN YI reads when the BUTLER rushes in with two infant carriers.

BUTLER
Master! Twins. Left at the gate. And a note—

The butler hands him a blood-stained scrap.

A blood-stained scrap reads: "Our agreement fulfilled. -F"

QIN YI stares at the babies. At the medical band: "Baby A: 3.2 lbs."

QIN YI
(quietly)
She survived.

He looks closer at the second band, smudged incomplete.

QIN YI
Find her.

EXT. MODERN OFFICE BUILDING - DAY

Six and a half years later. FANG SITONG, hardened and sharp, walks with a little girl toward the entrance.

The girl clutches a magazine photo of QIN YI.

They are stopped by QIN YI and his bodyguards.

FADE OUT.

title: Sorry, I'm Not Your White Moonlight
description: An interior designer unexpectedly reunites with a CEO from a fateful night four years ago when she arrives late to present her design proposal.
context:
Title: Sorry, I'm Not Your White Moonlight
Description: An interior designer unexpectedly reunites with a CEO from a fateful night four years ago when she arrives late to present her design proposal.

INT. BEDROOM - DAY (FLASHBACK)

CLOSE UP on a man's black dress shoes pacing, then a woman's pointed heels step into frame.

A woman (NING XI) looks up at a man (ZHAN LIHAN).

CLOSE UP on the woman's hands on the man's bare chest.

The couple lies in bed, embracing.

INT. NING XI'S BEDROOM - DAY

An alarm rings on a smartphone showing 08:00.

NING XI, an interior designer, wakes with a start, hand pressed to her chest.

NING XI (V.O.)
Four years, and that night still follows me.

The bedroom door creaks open. A little girl, NING ANXING, peeks in.

NING ANXING
Mommy, you'll be late.

Ning Xi bolts up, rushes to her daughter.

NING XI
Xingxing.

She kneels, smoothing her daughter's hair.

NING XI
Big meeting today. Grandma's picking you up, okay?

Ning Xi kisses her daughter's cheek.

NING XI
Land this contract, and that princess dress is yours.

NING ANXING
Good luck, Mommy!

Ning Xi smiles and rushes off.
continuation:
EXT. OFFICE BUILDING - DAY

Establishing shot of a modern glass skyscraper.

INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY

ZHAN LIHAN, a CEO, sits at the head of a long conference table. He pushes aside a design portfolio.

ZHAN LIHAN
This is your standard?

ANNA, Ning Xi's superior, stands nervously.

ANNA
Mr. Zhan, our senior designer is en route—

Ning Xi enters, breathless. She freezes at the sight of Zhan Lihan—her portfolio tumbling onto the table.

Zhan Lihan and Ning Xi lock eyes. Shock ripples between them. NING YANG, another woman at the table, tenses.

NING YANG
(under her breath)
Ning Xi...

ANNA
(relieved)
Finally.

ANNA
(to Zhan Lihan)
Mr. Zhan, Ning Xi—our lead designer.

ANNA
(to Ning Xi)
Show him your design.

As Ning Xi reaches for her scattered work, her finger grazes the mole behind Zhan Lihan's ear. She freezes.

A quick montage: their intimate night four years ago, the mole, a scent.

Zhan Lihan's hand tightens on the table. Ning Xi jerks back.

ZHAN LIHAN (V.O.)
That scent...

Zhan Lihan stands, his chair scraping the silence.

ZHAN LIHAN
May 20th. Four years ago. Where were you?

Ning Xi stares back, the papers trembling in her hands.

title: Finding the Secret of Cinderella
description: A reclusive fashion prodigy, scarred by public humiliation, becomes easy prey for her stepsister's long con—until a single interview exposes the true cost of her hunger for acceptance.
context:
Title: Finding the Secret of Cinderella
Description: A reclusive fashion prodigy, scarred by public humiliation, becomes easy prey for her stepsister's long con—until a single interview exposes the true cost of her hunger for acceptance.

EXT. FASHION GALA - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)

SCISSORHAND BAI stands before a shattered mannequin, scissors still in hand. The CROWD erupts.

CROWD
(laughing, chanting)
Scissorhands! Scissorhands!

SCISSORHAND BAI flees, dropping her shears.

EXT. TOWNHOUSE - DAY

SCISSORHAND BAI trudges home, clutching her portfolio.

INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

Her stepsister BAI XUE arranges orchids.

BAI XUE
(not turning)
The gallery called again. I told them you were unwell.

SCISSORHAND BAI
You didn't have to—

BAI XUE
(facing her, concerned)
But who else will protect you?

She gestures to a cake—two tiers, sugar scissor motif.

BAI XUE
Your favorite. It took all morning.

SCISSORHAND BAI
(softening)
You shouldn't have.

BAI XUE
You deserve sweetness, sister. After all that ugliness.

SCISSORHAND BAI approaches the cake.

BAI XUE (V.O.)
(low, melodic)
Yes. Eat. Fill that hollow place.

BAI XUE
(aloud, bright)
The Gallery sketches—you promised them.

SCISSORHAND BAI
(mouth full)
Mmf. Desk drawer.

BAI XUE
My angel.
continuation:
INT. DESIGN STUDIO - DAY

SCISSORHAND BAI hunches over her drafting table. BAI XUE brings coffee, sees the laptop—an email from NEW FASHION.

BAI XUE
(reading aloud, casual)
'Dear Scissorhand Bai—intriguing portfolio... feature opportunity...'

BAI XUE
(turning to SCISSORHAND BAI, delighted)
They want you! You must go.

SCISSORHAND BAI
I can't—

BAI XUE
You won't have to. I'll be your voice.

INT. PRESS CONFERENCE VENUE - DAY

REPORTERS crowd around. SCISSORHAND BAI, ill-fitting gown. BAI XUE, custom couture.

REPORTER
Which of you is—

BAI XUE
(smiling for cameras)
I'm Scissorhand Bai. This is my assistant... also Bai. Family tradition.

BAI XUE squeezes SCISSORHAND BAI'S shoulder—possessive, final. SCISSORHAND BAI stares at the cameras, hollow.

SCISSORHAND BAI
(whisper)
My shears...

BAI XUE
(whisper back)
Don't worry. I'll cut for you now.

SCISSORHAND BAI watches her sister become her. The cake turns to acid in her stomach.

title: Bringing the Human Trafficker to Justice
description: A young waitress discovers her childhood abductor on the news and must confront her painful past.
context:
Title: Bringing the Human Trafficker to Justice
Description: A young waitress discovers her childhood abductor on the news and must confront her painful past.

INT. RESTAURANT - DAY

Young waitress TAO GUIJIA, wearing an apron, carries a plate of steamed buns and a bowl of porridge, walking out from the kitchen with a practiced smile.

TAO GUIJIA
Your order is ready.

She places the food in front of a table of customers.

CUSTOMER (O.S.)
Check, please!

TAO GUIJIA
Coming.

TAO turns toward the cashier. Her hand drifts to her pocket, touching something small. She winces slightly, rubbing her lower back.

MALE CUSTOMER
Waitress—beer.

TAO GUIJIA
(quickly composing herself)
Right away.

TAO moves toward the cooler. Her phone VIBRATES. Caller ID: "Mom." The smile drops. She stares at it, then answers.

INT. RESTAURANT BY THE WINDOW - DAY

TAO stands by the window, phone pressed to her ear, gaze distant.

TAO'S MOTHER (V.O.)
Your brother's school fees are due. Why is it only two thousand this month? The doctor again?

TAO
(faintly)
It got worse.

TAO'S MOTHER (V.O.)
Then stop wasting money on doctors. Send everything next time. Your brother needs—

The call cuts. TAO lowers the phone, her fingers shaking. She swallows hard, eyes glassy. Her hand returns to her pocket, clutching something hidden there.

TAO (V.O.)
Five hundred yuan. Just to hear there's nothing they can do.
continuation:
INT. RESTAURANT - DAY

The OWNER appears, his voice sharp but controlled.

OWNER
Table four's been waiting. You're here to work, not stare out windows, understood?

TAO GUIJIA
(quickly wiping her eyes)
Yes, boss. Sorry.

OWNER
Get back to it.

TAO hurries toward the tables.

INT. RESTAURANT - DAY

TAO approaches a MALE CUSTOMER and FEMALE CUSTOMER with a bottle of beer. A TV on the wall plays the evening news.

TV NEWS ANCHOR (V.O.)
...in the case against human trafficking suspect MEI, prosecutors presented surveillance footage showing...

MALE CUSTOMER
(glancing at his phone)
They caught her because of some traffic camera. Eight years.

TV NEWS ANCHOR (V.O.)
...sentenced to eight years imprisonment.

TAO freezes mid-step. The glass bottle slips in her grip.

FEMALE CUSTOMER
Should've been longer. They say she's appealing.

MALE CUSTOMER
They all do.

TAO sets the beer down with forced steadiness.

TAO GUIJIA
Your beer.

She turns. The TV screen shows a courthouse photo of MEI, 50s, unremarkable. TAO's breath catches. She stares, unblinking. Her hand drifts to her pocket again, emerging with a broken green plastic ring—child-sized, faded.

FLASHBACK - EXT. FOREST PATH - 20 YEARS AGO

Desaturated footage.

MEI (30s) drags a small figure by the wrist through dense trees. YOUNG TAO (6) stumbles, her green ring catching light.

YOUNG TAO
(whispered, terrified)
Please... I dropped my ring...

MEI
Keep walking.

Young Tao looks back at something glinting in the dirt. Mei yanks her forward. The ring is left behind, half-buried.

INT. RESTAURANT - DAY

TAO stands motionless, tears streaming down her face. She turns the broken ring over in her fingers, fitting the pieces together. Her jaw tightens.

TAO (V.O.)
You left something behind.

TAO pulls out her phone with her free hand. Her thumb hovers over the emergency number on the screen, her gaze fixed on the TV.

title: Junior Martial Sister Only Wants to Be Expelled Every Day
description: A writer who sympathizes with a doomed sect in her novel is transported into the story, becoming a minor character destined to be expelled. She tries to get kicked out, but things don't go as planned.
context:
Title: Junior Martial Sister Only Wants to Be Expelled Every Day
Description: A writer who sympathizes with a doomed sect in her novel is transported into the story, becoming a minor character destined to be expelled. She tries to get kicked out, but things don't go as planned.

EXT. WASTELAND - DAY

Corpses litter scorched earth. Blood pools in the dirt. A man lies still, blood masking his features.

CHU FENG (Taiyi Sect Saintess)
If the Demon Cult is not eliminated, the righteous path cannot be upheld!

YING WUJI (Wuqing Sect Senior Brother)
Today, I shall grant your wish!

YING WUJI channels energy—blue lightning crackles across his body. CHU FENG lunges, blade glinting. Their clash erupts in a shower of sparks and light. SU YAO presses against a boulder, watching through trembling fingers.

INT. SU YAO'S STUDY - DAY

SU YAO hunches before her computer, fingers frozen above the keyboard. The screen glows with final paragraphs.

SU YAO (V.O.)
They bleed for honor. Wuqing bleeds because I wrote it. Cannon fodder for someone else's story.

Her jaw tightens. She types one last sentence. The monitor blazes—white light floods the room, swallows her. The chair spins, empty.

INT. WUQING SECT GREAT HALL - DAY

The hall looms—mahogany pillars, incense smoke curling toward rafters. Disciples whisper as they enter. A flash of light. SU YAO materializes center floor, ancient robes over modern clothes, pink keyboard clutched to her chest.

SU YAO
(to herself)
Alive. Not home.

She sees a white-haired, one-eyed man—HONG XIVIANG—seated on the high throne. YING WUJI and YU WEI stand at the base.

HONG XIVIANG (Wuqing Sect Master)
Confess. Who killed your fellow disciple?

YING WUJI
(urgently)
Junior Sister, explain!

SU YAO (internal)
Cannon fodder junior sister. Perfect.
continuation:
HONG XIVIANG
Confess now, and I will merely expel you.

YU WEI
Master, I witnessed it. Junior Sister struck the blow.

A vision flashes: YU WEI's triumphant smirk, the words "Divine assist from the enemy camp" glowing beside her.

SU YAO
(loudly)
Yes! I confess—I killed him! Unforgivable! Expel me as example!

YING WUJI
(shocked)
Your skill couldn't—

SU YAO
(cutting him off)
I cheated! Hidden weapons! Sneak attack!

HONG XIVIANG
(slamming the armrest—stone cracks)

Flames erupt in his palm. He thrusts it toward Su Yao.

YING WUJI
(blocking)
No, Master!

The flaming palm strikes YING WUJI's chest—he flies back, crumpling at Su Yao's feet. The flames sputter out.

HONG XIVIANG's expression shifts. He smiles—a slow, approving curve.

HONG XIVIANG
Little Yao... you understand at last.

SU YAO
(blank confusion)
Huh?

ON SCREEN: TO BE CONTINUED

FADE OUT.

title: The Little Ancestor Became Obedient
description: A young woman is reborn to the day she was framed for drugging her fiancé, determined to change her fate and make those who wronged her pay.
context:
Title: The Little Ancestor Became Obedient
Description: A young woman is reborn to the day she was framed for drugging her fiancé, determined to change her fate and make those who wronged her pay.

INT. HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT

ON SCREEN: Inside the hotel

LU ZHIXIA (the Gu family's adopted daughter) lies on the bed, eyes closed. SHEN CI (Lu Zhixia's fiancé) leans over her, his expression pained, breathing ragged.

SHEN CI
Zhixia... I think I've been drugged. My head...

His hand reaches for her face. LU ZHIXIA's eyes snap open—wide with sudden, horrifying recognition. Her body goes rigid.

LU ZHIXIA (V.O.)
This moment. This exact moment.

She lunges for a red decorative box on the nightstand. SHEN CI grabs her wrist.

SHEN CI
Don't fight it. We belong together--

LU ZHIXIA
(struggling)
Let go!

She wrenches free, swings the box with desperate force. It connects with his temple. SHEN CI collapses, groaning.

Panting, LU ZHIXIA staggers to her feet. She stares at him with pure loathing.

LU ZHIXIA (V.O.)
Not this time.

She straightens her clothes, smooths her hair, and walks out with measured steps.
continuation:
INT. HOTEL CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS
