Dark clouds gather. Typhoon winds batter the car. MU JIN drives one-handed, phone to her ear.

NEWS BROADCAST (V.O.)
Typhoon Kaigeli intensifies to Signal No. 9...

MU JIN
I'm almost home.

CHEN TIANYOU (V.O.)
(over phone)
Old Bai's barbecue. Mom's craving it.

MU JIN
I already bought some.

CHEN TIANYOU (V.O.)
(over phone, tighter)
She wants the one from Old Bai's. Turn back.

MU JIN
The storm—

CHEN TIANYOU (V.O.)
(over phone)
Two hours, Jin. That's all.

Mu Jin's jaw tightens. She checks the rearview. A red truck barrels forward, horn blaring. She jerks the wheel.

Metal screams. Glass shatters.
continuation:
EXT. ACCIDENT SCENE - DAY

MU JIN's car lies overturned, debris scattered. She lies unconscious beside it, typhoon rain washing over her.

TITLE CARD: THREE MONTHS LATER

INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY

MU JIN sits in a wheelchair, back to the door, watching the storm outside.

SONG YUQING enters with water. She hesitates.

SONG YUQING
(kneeling, holding Mu Jin's hand)
You should drink.

MU JIN
You're here every day.

SONG YUQING
Where else would I be?

Her phone VIBRATES. She silences it without looking.

MU JIN
I dream about walking.

JIANG MEI (O.S.)
(outside door, sharp)
The settlement will take years! What are we supposed to do—wait while she drains us?

SONG YUQING
(reluctant)
I'll handle it.

SONG YUQING stands and exits.

INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

JIANG MEI throws her purse on the table. CHEN TIANYOU sits apart, silent. SONG YUQING enters.

JIANG MEI
(to Chen Tianyou)
She can't even give you a child. Now this? Your father built this family—
(to Song Yuqing)
You. Explain to your friend what happens to wives who become furniture.

CHEN TIANYOU
Mom, stop.

JIANG MEI
Fine. I can't look at this anymore. Son?

CHEN TIANYOU stands. He and SONG YUQING exchange a glance—fleeting, weighted.

CHEN TIANYOU
(to Jiang Mei)
I'll walk you down.

JIANG MEI
Don't bother. You're needed here.

She leaves. The door clicks shut. CHEN TIANYOU and SONG YUQING remain still, the silence between them growing dense with complicity.

CHEN TIANYOU turns. SONG YUQING doesn't step back.

FADE OUT.

title: Stars Fall Into Your Eyes
description: A man discovers his wife has been manipulated against him after her parents' fatal car accident, leading to a devastating betrayal orchestrated by a rival.
context:
Title: Stars Fall Into Your Eyes
Description: A man discovers his wife has been manipulated against him after her parents' fatal car accident, leading to a devastating betrayal orchestrated by a rival.

INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - DAY

The hospital corridor is chaotic. Medical staff rush by pushing a gurney. LU CHENZHOU runs in, his face pale, scanning the corridor frantically.

NARRATOR (V.O.)
Breaking news: Shen Group President Shen Deming and his wife Liu Xinlan have been airlifted to the hospital after a fatal car accident. Their daughter Shen Qingwan was also in the vehicle.

LU CHENZHOU spots a gurney covered with a white sheet being wheeled into emergency surgery. He sprints toward it.

LU CHENZHOU
Wanwan!

A NURSE bursts from a side room, chart in hand, intercepting him.

NURSE
Mr. Lu! The blood bank is critically low on her rare type.

LU CHENZHOU
Take mine. I'm O-negative.

The Nurse hesitates, then another medical professional in scrubs rushes past.

DOCTOR
Her optic nerve trauma is severe. We may not be able to save her vision.

LU CHENZHOU stops in shock, his face crumpling with despair.

LU CHENZHOU
(murmuring)
Wanwan... no...

LU CHENZHOU grabs the Doctor's arm with trembling hands.

LU CHENZHOU
(grabbing the doctor's arm)
Anything. Please. Test me for compatibility—

A second NURSE walks over, double-checking a tablet.

NURSE
Are you Shen Qingwan's emergency contact?

LU CHENZHOU freezes and slowly turns his head.
continuation:
INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY

LU CHENZHOU pushes open the door. SHEN QINGWAN stands by the window in a hospital gown, turning a blood-stained envelope in her hands. Her eyes are clear but empty.

LU CHENZHOU exhales sharply, relief flooding his face.

LU CHENZHOU
Wanwan. Thank God you're okay. I thought—

He takes a step forward, arms opening. SHEN QINGWAN's hand trembles inside the envelope.

SHEN QINGWAN
(quietly)
Did you think I wouldn't find out?

She pulls a scalpel from the envelope. In one fluid motion, she drives it into LU CHENZHOU's abdomen. Not deep—but enough.

LU CHENZHOU clutches the wound, blood seeping between his fingers. Disbelief wars with agony.

LU CHENZHOU
(in pain)
Wanwan... why?

FLASHBACK - INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY

Three hours earlier. HUI YUNZE stands beside SHEN QINGWAN's bed, his tablet casting a cold glow on her tear-stained face. He scrolls through financial transfers, timestamped emails, a blurred photo of a cut brake line.

HUI YUNZE
(softly)
Your father discovered the embezzlement the day before the accident. Chenzhou had access to the car. Connect the dots, Qingwan.

SHEN QINGWAN stares at the screen, her breathing shallow. Her fingers trace the brake line photo.

END FLASHBACK - INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY

HUI YUNZE enters with a BODYGUARD, his footsteps deliberate.

HUI YUNZE
Qingwan—

He moves between them, shielding her with his body, studying Lu Chenzhou's wound.

HUI YUNZE
(to the bodyguard)
Miss Shen needs her medication. Now.

The BODYGUARD gently takes SHEN QINGWAN's elbow. She doesn't resist.

LU CHENZHOU
Wanwan!

He reaches for her, staining the floor with drops of blood.

LU CHENZHOU
(in pain)
It wasn't me. I'd never—

SHEN QINGWAN
(coldly, not turning)
I saw the accounts. I saw the report. For the rest of my life, Lu Chenzhou... I want to unsee you.

She lets the bodyguard lead her out.

LU CHENZHOU
(desperately)
Wanwan!

HUI YUNZE kneels beside him, his voice low and venomous.

HUI YUNZE
She chose you once. Against everything. Starting now, she chooses herself.

He shoves LU CHENZHOU back. From his pocket, he produces a phone. The screen shows a video: HUI YUNZE, hours ago, caressing Shen Qingwan's unconscious face in this very room, whispering in her ear.

HUI YUNZE
She'll never believe a word you say. I made sure of it.

LU CHENZHOU sees the timestamp on the video—the moment she woke up. Realization dawns. He lunges, but the pain blinds him. Blood fills his mouth. He collapses, one hand still reaching toward the empty doorway.

title: Spring Romance
description: A woman chooses a mysterious man for a one-night stand, only to discover he is her fiancé's uncle who has just returned home.
context:
Title: Spring Romance
Description: A woman chooses a mysterious man for a one-night stand, only to discover he is her fiancé's uncle who has just returned home.

INT. KTV - NIGHT

Neon lights fragment across black marble floors. Bass throbs through the walls.

JIANG FU (V.O.)
Tonight, I'm not the fiancée who smiles at the right moments. Tonight, I'm just... me.

JIANG FU, in a champagne slip dress, moves through the private room like a knife through smoke. Her eyes find CHENG JIALUO alone in a velvet booth, rolling a tumbler of whiskey.

JIANG FU (V.O.)
There. The one who doesn't belong.

She slides into the booth, leaving precise distance between them.

JIANG FU
Your solitude looks expensive. Mind if I try it?

CHENG JIALUO
I'm waiting for someone.

JIANG FU's laugh is soft, barely audible over the music.

JIANG FU
Aren't we all.

She starts to rise. His hand shoots out, circling her wrist—not rough, but unbreakable.

JIANG FU
Sir?

CHENG JIALUO
What if they're not coming?

JIANG FU
Then I'd hate to waste a good dress.

He studies her face for a beat that stretches. Then releases her wrist, but his eyes hold her still.

CHENG JIALUO
Neither would I.

He stands, offering his arm. She takes it.
continuation:
INT. HOTEL ROOM - NIGHT

Moonlight cuts across tangled sheets. JIANG FU's back arches, her fingers twisting in CHENG JIALUO's hair. A crystal tumbler lies shattered on the floor, forgotten.

INT. HOTEL ROOM - MORNING

Sunlight pools on the carpet. They lie side by side in white robes, not touching.

JIANG FU (V.O.)
This was supposed to be simple. A transaction. So why does my chest feel like a locked room?

CHENG JIALUO
Running already?

JIANG FU
I don't do breakfast.

CHENG JIALUO
Who said anything about food?

He leans in. She doesn't pull away. His lips brush her forehead, her nose—each touch a question.

INT. HOTEL ROOM - LATER

JIANG FU wakes alone. Shower steam curls under the bathroom door. Her phone buzzes on the nightstand: "Aunt Qiu."

JIANG FU
Hello, Aunt Qiu.

AUNT QIU (V.O.)
Xiaofu! Jiamu's uncle returns tonight. The family dinner's at seven—don't be late. He's very... particular.

JIANG FU
Particular?

AUNT QIU (V.O.)
Just be your charming self. And wear the pearls Jiamu gave you.

The call ends. JIANG FU sits up slowly, ice forming in her veins.

JIANG FU (V.O.)
Cheng Jialuo. The monk in Gucci. Back from his temple in Kyoto.

She spots her dress pooled on the floor—beside a man's leather wallet. She opens it. A driver's license: CHENG JIALUO. Date of birth makes him older than she thought. Much older.

Her fingers tremble as she dresses. On the pillow beside hers: a single prayer bead, sandalwood. She leaves it there.

JIANG FU (V.O.)
One night. No names. No consequences.

She slips out. The door clicks shut.

INT. HOTEL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

CHENG JIALUO emerges, towel around his waist. He touches the abandoned prayer bead. Picks up his wallet—sees it slightly askew. A knowing smile plays on his lips.

He pulls out his phone, scrolls to a photo: JIANG FU and JIAMU at their engagement party. He sets it down, then notices her pearl necklace on the nightstand.

CHENG JIALUO
Game's just starting, little fiancée.

He pockets the pearls.

title: Radiant Youth
description: A young woman betrayed by her sister, who stole her face and future as empress, is given a chance at revenge by a mysterious figure.
context:
Title: Radiant Youth
Description: A young woman betrayed by her sister, who stole her face and future as empress, is given a chance at revenge by a mysterious figure.

EXT. PRIME MINISTER'S MANOR BACKYARD - NIGHT

Several corpses lie scattered on the ground. A man is thrown violently through a circular doorway, an arrow lodged in his forehead. He dies instantly.

XIE YUNING (dressed in white) staggers into the courtyard. She sees the carnage before her, terror-stricken.

XIE YUNING (V.O.)
I, Xie Yuning, was once the eldest daughter of the Prime Minister's manor. To heal my sister Xie Yuzhi's scarred face, I surrendered my place as future Empress, kissing the earth every three steps for ten miles.

XIE YUNING sees her parents lying on the ground. She collapses, running to them and falling to her knees.

XIE YUNING (V.O.)
I believed scars ran only skin-deep.

XIE YUNING
(crying out)
Mother... Father...

XIE YUZHI (scars on her face) emerges from the shadows, touching her own cheek with trembling fingers.

XIE YUNING
(looking up, disbelieving)
Yuzhi... the medicine—

XIE YUZHI
(quietly)
The medicine worked. Thanks to you.

XIE YUZHI crouches before XIE YUNING, gripping her jaw.

XIE YUZHI
Father raised your commoner parents to ensure your compliance. He never imagined you'd outshine me. But now...

She touches her own face, which begins to smooth and transform.

XIE YUZHI
Now I wear what was never yours to keep.

XIE YUZHI releases her, standing.

XIE YUZHI
Tomorrow, I ascend as Empress. You die nameless.
continuation:
EXT. MASS GRAVE - NIGHT

Thunder and rain. XIE YUNING's body is thrown onto a mass grave littered with bones.

A MYSTERIOUS FIGURE wearing a conical hat appears beside her. Rain cascades off the hat's rim.

MYSTERIOUS FIGURE
The dead have no use for vengeance. But you're not dead yet.

The MYSTERIOUS FIGURE extends a hand. XIE YUNING's fingers twitch, then grasp his.

MYSTERIOUS FIGURE
Some debts can only be paid in flesh and memory. Are you certain?

XIE YUNING
(weakly)
Yes.

INT. THATCHED HUT - NIGHT

XIE YUNING lies on a bed, her face bloody and raw. The MYSTERIOUS FIGURE (now wearing white robes and a half-mask) prepares tools for the face transplant.

MYSTERIOUS FIGURE (V.O.)
The legitimate daughter, the future Empress—stripped of face and destiny. But what is a face? A mask the world sees. A mask you choose to wear.

XIE YUNING
(whispered)
I want to be the nightmare they don't see coming.

MYSTERIOUS FIGURE
You will obey. You will forget who you were. And when you stand before them, even you won't recognize yourself.

The MYSTERIOUS FIGURE begins the procedure. XIE YUNING screams silently, her body arching.

MYSTERIOUS FIGURE
Remember this pain. It's the last honest thing you'll feel.

INT. THATCHED HUT - DAY

Sunlight streams through the window. XIE YUNING lies on the bed, white gauze covering her eyes.

She sits up, raises her hand, removes the gauze from her face.

The gauze falls away, revealing a face of perfect, breathtaking beauty—stranger than memory, colder than vengeance.

title: Dawn in the Dark Night
description: A young girl witnesses her father's death in a hit-and-run and her mother's murder by a powerful family. Fifteen years later, she returns for revenge.
context:
Title: Dawn in the Dark Night
Description: A young girl witnesses her father's death in a hit-and-run and her mother's murder by a powerful family. Fifteen years later, she returns for revenge.

EXT. ROAD - NIGHT

A small ball rolls across wet asphalt through a streetlight's glow.

SHU YU'S FATHER chases after it.

A little girl in a red dress, SHU YU (7), holds a photo frame, standing by the roadside.

SHU YU'S FATHER picks up the ball. A car speeds toward him, blinding headlights blazing.

SOUND: Sharp braking and collision.

The photo frame falls to the ground, glass shattering. The photo shows a family of three.

SOUND: Thunder.

SHU YU
(terrified)
Daddy!

SHU YU runs to her father lying in a pool of blood and holds him, crying.

SHU YU
Daddy!

SHU YU'S FATHER
(weakly)
Go... find your mother. At the big house.

SHU YU
(crying)
No, Daddy!

SHU YU'S FATHER
(weakly)
Go... go!

INT. FU RESIDENCE - NIGHT

SHU YU'S MOTHER kneels on the ground, clutching LIN PEIRONG'S hand, begging desperately. FU ZHENGXIN stands nearby, expressionless.

SHU YU'S MOTHER
Madam, I beg you, please spare me, spare our family, Madam!

BUTLER LIU walks to FU ZHENGXIN'S side.

BUTLER LIU
Young Master, we've confirmed it. She is indeed RH negative blood type. Young Miss Fu's surgery is at dawn. We can operate now.

FU ZHENGXIN
Do it.

Two men in black step forward and grab SHU YU'S MOTHER.

SHU YU'S MOTHER
(struggling)
Let me go! What are you doing! Let go!
continuation:
INT. MAKESHIFT OPERATING ROOM - NIGHT

SHU YU'S MOTHER is pressed onto the operating table. A masked person approaches with a syringe.

SHU YU'S MOTHER struggles violently but is eventually injected with the drug. A tear rolls down her cheek as she loses consciousness.

SHU YU'S MOTHER
(barely audible)
Shu Yu...

EXT. ROAD - NIGHT

SHU YU runs through the rain and falls on the grass.

SHU YU
(crying out)
Mommy!

INT. FU RESIDENCE - NIGHT

SHU YU runs down the stairs and sees two men carrying her mother out, covered with a white cloth.

SHU YU
(crying out)
Mommy! Mommy! Mommy! Look at me, wake up, Mommy!

SHU YU throws herself on her mother, crying.

FU ZHENGXIN and LIN PEIRONG walk out of the room.

SHU YU
(crying out)
Mommy, wake up!

LIN PEIRONG
What's all this noise? You little bastard! Grab her!

BUTLER LIU steps forward and grabs SHU YU.

SHU YU
(struggling, crying out)
You killed Daddy in the accident and hurt Mommy! You're bad people! Bad people!

SHU YU breaks free and bites BUTLER LIU'S hand.

LIN PEIRONG steps forward and slaps SHU YU hard, knocking her to the ground.

LIN PEIRONG
You dare bite me? You little brat! Filthy thing!

FU ZHENGXIN
Uncle Liu, clean this up together. Make it clean.

BUTLER LIU picks up the unconscious SHU YU. SHU YU lies on the ground, weakly reaching out her hand, watching her mother being carried away. A small birthmark shows on her wrist.

SHU YU
(weakly)
Mommy... Mommy...

INT. CAR - NIGHT

DRIVER
Butler Liu, what should we do with this little girl?

BUTLER LIU holds the unconscious SHU YU in the back seat.

BUTLER LIU
Send her abroad. I hope she never comes back... and forgets what she saw.

EXT. CITYSCAPE - SUNSET

ON SCREEN: Fifteen years later

INT. GRAND HALL - NIGHT

The adult SHU YU, wearing a red sequined gown, walks into a magnificent hall with a powerful presence. A jade bracelet circles her wrist. Her figure alternates with images of her childhood self.

SHU YU (V.O.)
Lin Peirong, Fu Zhengxin. It's been fifteen years. We meet again.

FU ZHENGXIN and LIN PEIRONG stand together, watching SHU YU approach.

SHU YU walks toward them with determined eyes, step by step. In her clutch: the preserved, torn photograph.

title: Dawn Light Enters Dreams and Shakes Mountains and Rivers
description: During a sacrificial ceremony to pray for rain, a consort desperately tries to save her newborn daughter from being sacrificed, but divine intervention strikes at the crucial moment.
context:
Title: Dawn Light Enters Dreams and Shakes Mountains and Rivers
Description: During a sacrificial ceremony to pray for rain, a consort desperately tries to save her newborn daughter from being sacrificed, but divine intervention strikes at the crucial moment.

INT. SACRIFICIAL ALTAR - DAY

A cavernous temple hall, heavy with incense and dread. DEMON-MASKED DANCERS move in hypnotic patterns across the taiji symbol carved into the stone floor. The EMPEROR sits rigid on the gilded throne, his knuckles white on the armrests. CONSORT SHEN stands beside him, her serene expression masking a watchful intensity. Below, CONSORT XIAN clutches her wrapped infant, her court robes stained with tears.

CONSORT XIAN
(quietly, to her child)
Stay quiet, little one. Stay quiet.

The High Priest's staff strikes the ground with three resonant thuds. Guards advance toward Consort Xian.

CONSORT XIAN
(sharply)
She is imperial blood! The ninth pearl of the Dragon's line!

CONSORT SHEN
(to Emperor, measured)
Your Majesty, the drought extends to nine provinces. The Oracle pronounced a royal sacrifice. We cannot show hesitation.

CONSORT XIAN
(turning on her)
You speak of hesitation? My daughter is forty days old.

CONSORT SHEN
My own son is six years, and would go if the divination had chosen him. This is the price of sovereignty.

CONSORT XIAN
The price is always paid by others.

EMPEROR
(quietly, to Consort Shen)
Is this truly the only path?

HIGH PRIEST
The heavens demand purity of conviction! Vacillation brings not rain, but thunder!

He gestures. The guards close in. Consort Xian backs toward the altar.

CONSORT XIAN
(to the court)
Will none of you speak? Will you let ash become of flesh?

The EMPEROR's face hardens, but he does not look away.
continuation:
EMPEROR
(to Consort Xian)
If she brings rain, she saves millions. She will be immortalized.

CONSORT XIAN
(fierce, low)
She will be dead.

The guards wrench the infant from Consort Xian's arms. She lunges, but they hold her firm. The baby, placed on the pyre, does not cry.

CONSORT XIAN
(her voice breaking)
At least let me hold her once more. Let me—please.

HIGH PRIEST
The vessel must be pure. Untouched.

He lifts the torch. The dancers' rhythm intensifies. Consort Xian's eyes fix on the Emperor—not pleading now, but branding his face into memory.

CONSORT XIAN
You will remember this. Every raindrop will whisper her name.

The EMPEROR flinches. The torch descends.

A sudden darkness swallows the temple. Lightning—brilliant, impossible—cracks through the ceiling, striking the altar dead center. The explosion of light and sound sends bodies sprawling. The pyre splinters. Smoke billows.

Silence.

Consort Xian is first to move. She scrambles through charred debris, not caring about the smoke or the crackling energy still dancing across the stones. She finds the baby—intact, breathing, asleep.

CONSORT XIAN
(whispering)
...impossible.

She unwraps the cloth with trembling fingers. The infant's eyes open—clear, ancient, knowing.

WEICHI XI (V.O.)
(soft, resonant)
Mother. The covenant is witnessed.

Consort Xian freezes, the baby's gaze holding hers. The child blinks and is simply an infant again, but the temple now smells of ozone and something else... rain on the wind.

MINISTER
(off-screen, awed)
The heavens... they have answered.

title: The Fabulous Model
description: At a glamorous fashion show, two businessmen meet to discuss a mysterious plan involving one of the models.
context:
Title: The Fabulous Model
Description: At a glamorous fashion show, two businessmen meet to discuss a mysterious plan involving one of the models.

EXT. FASHION SHOW VENUE - NIGHT

MONTAGE - FASHION SHOW

A fast-paced montage of beautiful models walking a runway under bright, flashing lights. Upbeat, heavy rock music plays.

LAN LINGWANG, in a sparkling gown, poses confidently.

XIAO MENGYUE, in a white mini-dress, smiles sweetly.

BBB JIANG, in a strapless dress, walks with purpose.

YEZI DAREN, in a black sequined dress, dances playfully.

SHI NI YIRAN, in a gold dress, winks at the camera.

XIAO XUEHUA, in a silver dress, strikes a pose.

CAI YUE, in a red dress, blows a kiss.

GU YAN, in a golden-yellow dress, walks gracefully.

CHENG ER, in a shimmering silver dress, makes a "shush" gesture.

The montage ends. The title card appears: THE FABULOUS MODEL.

Two men, WANG ZONG and DAVID, walk towards each other in a dimly lit area outside the venue. WANG ZONG is wearing a fur-collared coat. DAVID is in a suit.

DAVID
Wang Zong.

WANG ZONG
(smiling and shaking hands)
David. You're late to your own party.
continuation:
DAVID
I was making sure the merchandise was properly displayed.

WANG ZONG
Merchandise has a way of developing opinions.

DAVID
Which is why this particular asset interests me.

DAVID takes out his phone and shows a picture to WANG ZONG. On the screen is a photo of CHENG ER—different life. Younger. Bruised.

DAVID (V.O.)
Remember this girl?

WANG ZONG stares at the photo. His smile calcifies. Recognition hits like a memory of a wound. A sharp sound effect plays.

WANG ZONG
(quietly)
I remember a debt.

DAVID
Outstanding. Now she's worth ten million dollars to the right collector.

WANG ZONG looks toward the venue. CHENG ER's silhouette moves behind glass—free, famous, unaware.

WANG ZONG
She'll recognize you.

DAVID
She won't recognize herself.

A beat. WANG ZONG's hand tightens on the phone.

WANG ZONG
Then let's make it interesting.

The sharp sound effect crescendoes with a heartbeat.

title: A Dragon's Hope: The National Scholar Returns Home
description: A brilliant scientist returns to honor his parents after receiving the nation's highest award for his groundbreaking work in controlled nuclear fusion.
context:
Title: A Dragon's Hope: The National Scholar Returns Home
Description: A brilliant scientist returns to honor his parents after receiving the nation's highest award for his groundbreaking work in controlled nuclear fusion.

INT. AWARD CEREMONY HALL - DAY

A background screen displays "National Scholar Medal Ceremony."

An elderly man with white hair (ELDER JIANG), wearing a uniform, stands at the podium delivering a speech. The audience below is packed.

ELDER JIANG
Let us give a round of applause to welcome the Father of Controlled Nuclear Fusion, Chen Ming!

Thunderous applause erupts from the audience.

The young and handsome CHEN MING, wearing a black Zhongshan suit, walks up to the stage from below. He smiles, his steps steady and confident.

NARRATOR (V.O.)
Ten years at the Advanced Research Institute. Ten years since he left the mountains.

CHEN MING walks to center stage and bows to the audience.

A CEREMONIAL HOSTESS walks onto the stage carrying a wooden box containing a golden National Scholar Medal.

ELDER JIANG takes the medal from the box.

CHEN MING (V.O.)
Dad, Mom. Ten years.

ELDER JIANG personally pins the medal on CHEN MING.

ELDER JIANG picks up a red certificate of honor and hands it to CHEN MING.

ELDER JIANG
Comrade Chen Ming, it is my honor to present you with the National Scholar Medal. For ending a century of technological containment.

CHEN MING
Elder Jiang, you are too kind.

ELDER JIANG
Not too kind. The organization instructed me: whatever you need, name it.

CHEN MING walks to the podium, holding the certificate of honor, facing the microphone.

CHEN MING
Ten years ago, my parents sold our only pig. The village pooled eggs, basket by basket. This belongs to them.

CUT TO:
continuation:
EXT. RURAL FARMHOUSE COURTYARD - DAY

ON SCREEN: Ten Years Ago

CHEN MING'S FATHER, leaning on a crutch, excitedly looks at the "Qingbei University Admission Notice" in his hand.

FATHER
We did it!

CHEN MING'S MOTHER runs out from inside the house. Seeing the notice, she cries tears of joy.

MOTHER
My son... my brilliant son...

YOUNG CHEN MING, dressed plainly, stands to the side.

YOUNG CHEN MING
Dad, I can work at the mine. I don't have to go.

Father's smile freezes. He raises his crutch, his hand trembling.

FATHER
You'd throw this back in their faces?

MOTHER
(quickly stopping him)
Old Chen! He's trying to help!

YOUNG CHEN MING
The tuition is eight hundred yuan. You haven't eaten a full meal in a week.

FATHER
My stomach troubles me. That's not your burden.

YOUNG CHEN MING
Dad...

FATHER
I limp through that mine so you wouldn't have to! You think I want to see you back here in ten years?

Father steadies himself against the doorframe.

FATHER
A man lives for his pride. Don't make me die having failed at the only thing that matters.

YOUNG CHEN MING drops to his knees with a thud, sobbing uncontrollably.

YOUNG CHEN MING
(crying out)
I'll fail. I'll waste it all!

FATHER
Then fail trying. Not here.

Father suddenly clutches his chest. His crutch slips, and he steadies himself against the wall, breathing hard.

FATHER
(quietly)
The ticket is bought, son. Don't make them take it back.

title: The Mute Woman from the Mountains
description: A mother from a mountain village loses one daughter in a tragic accident and sacrifices everything to give her surviving daughter a chance at education and a better life.
context:
Title: The Mute Woman from the Mountains
Description: A mother from a mountain village loses one daughter in a tragic accident and sacrifices everything to give her surviving daughter a chance at education and a better life.

EXT. VILLAGE ROAD - DAY

A man holds an injured little girl (JIA'ER) in his arms, surrounded by VILLAGERS. The man shouts anxiously.

MAN
Someone call for help! The ambulance!

CUIHUA and her young daughter (WAN'ER) walk down the road and see a crowd gathered ahead. CUIHUA's heart tightens. She runs over, her hands already forming desperate signs.

CUIHUA
(frantically gesturing)
[Where? My daughter? Sweet shop?]

She grabs a VILLAGER's arm, pointing toward her small roadside sweet stand, then holding her hand at Jia'er's height.

FEMALE NEIGHBOR
Cuihua... the car came too fast. Your Jia'er... she's gone. Driver fled. The Zhangs called the police.

CUIHUA collapses to her knees, her face contorted in a silent scream. She stares at the chalk outline on the pavement, touches a smudge of red clay dust - not blood, just earth. Her shoulders convulse.

WAN'ER
(crying, kneeling down)
Mama, it's my fault. I wanted the ribbon for my hair. If I hadn't kept you at the market...

CUIHUA pulls Wan'er close, pressing the girl's head to her chest. She rocks them both, her mouth open in a soundless wail.

INT. CUIHUA'S HOUSE - NIGHT

TEN YEARS LATER

CUIHUA sits on the edge of the bed, knitting by oil lamp. Her hair is steel-gray. Rain pounds the tin roof. WAN'ER bursts in, clutching a wet envelope, rainwater streaming from her braids.

WAN'ER
Mom! Mom! I got it!

She thrusts the envelope into Cuihua's hands. Cuihua's fingers tremble as she opens it. Her eyes trace the characters. A smile cracks her weathered face. She stands and touches JIA'ER's memorial photo - a faded Polaroid tucked into the mud wall.

CUIHUA
(signing gently)
[She sees you. She's proud.]

WAN'ER
Beijing Normal University. Full tuition is ten thousand yuan.

Cuihua's smile doesn't fade. She pulls a cloth bundle from under the bed - layers of fabric unfold to reveal carefully sorted bills: tens, twenties, fives. She counts. Two thousand, three hundred. Wan'er watches her mother's quick, practiced fingers.

WAN'ER
(quietly)
Mama, I could stay. Teach at the township school.

CUIHUA
(signs sharply, eyes flashing)
[No. You will go.]

She reaches under the mattress, pulls out her bankbook. Points to the balance line. Then points to Wan'er, then skyward, gesturing: "I will find more."

WAN'ER
(moved, but controlled)
Mama...

Mother and daughter embrace. Cuihua's arms are rope-muscled and strong.

MONTAGE:
- CUIHUA loads her rusted tricycle at dawn, cucumbers and bok choy arranged like jade. She pedals uphill, her calves straining.
- CUIHUA carries mortar at a construction site, her small frame bent double. A WORKER points; she nods, points to her back, loads more.
- CUIHUA works a night market stall, hands flying in sign language to customers. She clears tables, her fingers blistered.
- CUIHUA harvests rice under white sun, her sickle a silver blur. She stumbles, rights herself, keeps cutting.
- CUIHUA clutches her side at the construction site. She waves off a coworker, tries to stand, then folds slowly to the ground like a closing fan.
continuation:
INT. HOSPITAL WARD - NIGHT

CUIHUA lies in bed, an oxygen mask covering her face. WAN'ER sits beside her, now a young woman in a university sweater. A DOCTOR enters with a clipboard.

WAN'ER
She opened her eyes once. Didn't sign.

DOCTOR
Your mother's coworker brought her medical card. She's been buying painkillers for months. The scans show advanced renal failure. She needs surgery within weeks.

Cuihua's eyes flutter open. She sees the doctor, sees the clipboard with cost estimates. She tears the mask off.

CUIHUA
(gasping, signing weakly)
[No. Wan'er's last year.]

WAN'ER
(firmly, tears streaming)
Mama, if you die, I leave school. I come back here. I plant rice. That's the end.

CUIHUA
(trying to rise, face pale)
[My life is mine to spend!]

WAN'ER
(holding her down)
And my life is mine to lose if you go!

The two women lock eyes. Cuihua's hands fall still. Wan'er turns to the doctor.

WAN'ER
She'll have the surgery. I'll get the money.

EXT. VILLAGE ENTRANCE - DAY

TEN YEARS LATER

CUIHUA sits at her doorstep, hair pure white. She shells peanuts with quick fingers, dropping them in a bowl. A few NEIGHBORS chat not far away.

NEIGHBOR
Her daughter's doing well in Jinghai. Two children now. Sends money every month.

CUIHUA hears this, slowly lifts her head, her eyes filled with complex emotions. She looks at the paved road leading out of the village, runs a finger along the rim of a porcelain bowl Wan'er sent last Spring Festival. Her hands remember the weight of mortar, the burn of hot oil. The bowl is empty. She fills it with shelled nuts anyway, arranges them in a neat circle, a silent offering.

A car horn sounds. She doesn't look up. The neighbors scatter. She keeps shelling, her fingers steady, her face giving nothing away.

title: Deception Game
description: A con artist team uses a fake charity scheme to lure villagers into their trap, starting with free mooncakes and a planted accomplice.
context:
Title: Deception Game
Description: A con artist team uses a fake charity scheme to lure villagers into their trap, starting with free mooncakes and a planted accomplice.

EXT. VILLAGE ENTRANCE CLEARING - DAY

A delivery truck idles in dust. LU HONG stands on the flatbed with stacked gift boxes. Villagers keep their distance.

LU HONG
(raising megaphone)
Mid-Autumn Festival! Free mooncakes from GreenSun Energy!

VILLAGER A
Free? What's the catch?

VILLAGER B
Probably expired stock.

XU HAOYU steps forward, holding a medical bill envelope.

XU HAOYU
Never heard of GreenSun.

LU HONG
New startup. Our CEO invests in villages, not TV ads.

XU HAOYU
Boss has a name?

LU HONG
CEO Wan. Come see for yourself.

XU HAOYU glances at his envelope, then accepts a box.

XU HAOYU
Real lotus paste.

VILLAGER C
My grandson loves these. We can't afford them this year.

CHEN HAO leans from the driver's window.

CHEN HAO
One per family, folks.

GANG ZI pushes through, holding a box.

GANG ZI
Another for my sister's place.

LU HONG
One per household, brother. Rules are rules.

GANG ZI
City rules or village rules?

Villagers relax, some chuckling. They line up.

LU HONG
(once distribution slows)
GreenSun seeks one local partner. Invest one thousand yuan today, get it back tomorrow with five percent dividend. Plus free solar consultation.

The crowd stills. XU HAOYU grips his medical bill.

XU HAOYU
Five percent in one day? What bank gives that?

VILLAGER B
Sounds like pyramid schemes.

GANG ZI
I've seen their East District office. Three floors, glass front.

CHEN HAO
(producing a laminated certificate)
License and tax records. All proper.

GANG ZI takes the certificate, examines the seal closely.
continuation:
GANG ZI
(to Xu Haoyu)
I've worked construction in the city. This seal is legit.

XU HAOYU
(quietly)
You're from here?

GANG ZI
Used to be. Back for my mother. City life's expensive.

XU HAOYU
My mother needs surgery. Ten thousand.

GANG ZI
A thousand's a thousand. Sometimes you gotta trust something.

GANG ZI pulls a worn wallet, counts out one thousand yuan. Villagers watch.

VILLAGER C
Brother, don't do it.

VILLAGER A
We've seen this story before.

GANG ZI
I've seen their office. What have you seen?

He hands cash to Lu Hong. Lu Hong writes a receipt.

LU HONG
Partnership recorded. Same time tomorrow, nine sharp.

GANG ZI
(nodding to Xu Haoyu)
See you tomorrow?

XU HAOYU
(staring at envelope)
Maybe.

LU HONG, CHEN HAO, and SU WAN in the cab exchange a look.

EXT. COUNTRY ROAD - DAY

The truck rumbles down the narrow road.

INT. TRUCK CABIN - DAY

LU HONG drives. SU WAN counts cash in the passenger seat. CHEN HAO and GANG ZI ride in back, silent.

SU WAN
One brings ten tomorrow.

LU HONG
(quiet)
The one with the hospital bill looked ready to break.

SU WAN
That's the business.

GANG ZI
(leaning forward)
He recognized me. From the factory.

SU WAN
So?

GANG ZI
Nothing. Just... he recognized me.

SU WAN
Get some sleep. Tomorrow we collect.

The truck passes the village sign. LU HONG checks the rearview mirror—GANG ZI watches the receding fields.

The truck accelerates into the distance.

title: Justice Will Not Be Absent
description: A celebrated lawyer is framed, murdered, and betrayed by his wife and rival, only to wake up ten years in the past with a chance to prevent his downfall.
context:
Title: Justice Will Not Be Absent
Description: A celebrated lawyer is framed, murdered, and betrayed by his wife and rival, only to wake up ten years in the past with a chance to prevent his downfall.

EXT. POOL PARTY - NIGHT

A lively pool party is in full swing. People dance, drink, and celebrate by the poolside. The music is loud and chaotic.

CHEN YANG, a man in an elegant suit and glasses, sits alone on a bench, quietly reading a book, out of place amidst the surrounding clamor.

A woman in an orange dress (FEMALE GUEST 1) walks up to CHEN YANG with a wine glass.

FEMALE GUEST 1
Congratulations, Lawyer Chen Yang, on becoming the first certified lawyer of the Grand Xia Bar Association.

Another woman in a yellow dress (FEMALE GUEST 2) also approaches.

FEMALE GUEST 2
They say the Sun Cong case was your biggest victory yet. That's the one that made history.

CHEN YANG closes his book, stands up, and picks up a glass of wine from the table.

CHEN YANG
History has a way of repeating itself.

FEMALE GUEST 1
Let's hope not. To your future!

Everyone raises their glasses together, shouting "Cheers," and drinks in one gulp.

Suddenly, a sharp sound of shattering glass. The party music and noise come to an abrupt halt.

Everyone turns back in shock.

ZHAO HAI, a disheveled, grief-stricken middle-aged man in catering staff uniform, emerges from the service entrance. He wears two black-and-white memorial portraits pinned to his chest and carries a black garbage bag. He staggers forward, grabbing handfuls of white paper money from the bag and scattering it into the air.

ZHAO HAI stumbles toward CHEN YANG, his eyes filled with hatred.

ZHAO HAI kneels before CHEN YANG, placing the portraits on the ground.

CHEN YANG
(carefully)
Zhao Hai? This is a private event—

CHEN YANG
(recognizing the portraits)
Wait. Is that your daughter?

YE QINGYAN, a woman in a sequined jacket, and ZHANG XUAN, a man in a white suit, walk forward from the back of the crowd, looking smug.

YE QINGYAN
He came because you destroyed his family. And ours.

CHEN YANG
(shocked)
Qingyan, what are you—

YE QINGYAN
Don't. The investigators have everything.

ZHANG XUAN takes out a document with "Notice of Disbarment Proceedings" written on the cover.

ZHANG XUAN
continuation:
The 2 million from Sun Corporation. The altered evidence. Your license is suspended pending trial. It's over.

CHEN YANG takes the document, looking at it in disbelief.

CHEN YANG
This is fabricated! Zhao Hai, you must believe me—I fought for your daughter's case with everything I had!

YE QINGYAN
(quietly cruel)
You fought just hard enough to lose. Just like I designed.

FEMALE GUEST 1
Eighty-seven wins, one loss... and that loss destroyed the Zhao family.

FEMALE GUEST 2
Was any of it real?

CHEN YANG
(shouting at the crowd)
Check the sealed evidence! Sun's lawyers knew our strategy before trial!

GUESTS
(murmuring among themselves)
The transfer records are public...
He's been embezzling for months...
We should have seen the signs...

CHEN YANG turns to ZHANG XUAN and grabs his clothes.

CHEN YANG
You were inside our systems. You and—

Suddenly, ZHAO HAI rushes forward from behind and stabs a knife into CHEN YANG's abdomen.

CHEN YANG groans in pain as blood spreads across his white shirt.

The party erupts into chaos with screams everywhere.

GUESTS
(terrified)
Someone call 911! Now!

CHEN YANG falls to the ground, clutching his wound. ZHAO HAI stands nearby, first laughing maniacally, then crying bitterly.

ZHAO HAI
(laughing and crying)
She's gone... they're all gone... and you helped him walk free...

CHEN YANG lies on the ground, looking weakly at YE QINGYAN and ZHANG XUAN.

CHEN YANG
Why... husband and partner... why...

YE QINGYAN
(leaning close, whispering)
I took the money. I gave Zhang Xuan access to your files. In ten years, when you're nothing but a memory... we'll control everything you built.

ZHANG XUAN
(smirking)
Justice, Chen. Sometimes it's just another service we provide.

CHEN YANG lies motionless. The sequined jacket sparkles as YE QINGYAN turns away.

title: These Stars Are Not Last Night's Stars (Episode 77)
description: A woman confronts her jealous friend who pushes her into traffic, revealing years of resentment over adoption and fortune.
context:
Title: These Stars Are Not Last Night's Stars (Episode 77)
Description: A woman confronts her jealous friend who pushes her into traffic, revealing years of resentment over adoption and fortune.

INT. ALLEY - NIGHT

A car drives up, its headlights blinding.

SHEN XINGCHEN and JIANG YE stand together. SHEN XINGCHEN looks terrified.

SHEN XINGCHEN
Watch out!

JIANG YE forcefully pushes SHEN XINGCHEN toward the car. SHEN XINGCHEN is hit onto the hood, then falls to the ground. Her phone drops beside her. She looks very weak.

JIANG YE crosses her arms, looking down at SHEN XINGCHEN on the ground.

JIANG YE (V.O.)
Shen Xingchen, do you know how long I've watched you?

EXT. BUILDING EXTERIOR - DAY - FLASHBACK

ADOPTIVE FATHER crouches down to comfort YOUNG JIANG YE who is crying. YOUNG XINGCHEN sits alone on the steps.

ADOPTIVE FATHER
(to Jiang Ye, beaming)
What a good girl.

ADOPTIVE MOTHER walks over, her gaze fixed on Young Xingchen.

ADOPTIVE MOTHER
(gently)
That little girl... she's so quiet. Maybe she needs us more?

ADOPTIVE MOTHER points to YOUNG XINGCHEN. ADOPTIVE FATHER looks at YOUNG XINGCHEN, then smiles in agreement.

ADOPTIVE FATHER
Child, would you like to come home with us?

ADOPTIVE MOTHER smiles and extends her hand to YOUNG XINGCHEN.

ADOPTIVE MOTHER
Come on.

YOUNG XINGCHEN stands up, takes ADOPTIVE MOTHER's hand, and the three leave together. YOUNG JIANG YE watches their backs, her small hands clenching into fists.
continuation:
INT. RESTAURANT - NIGHT

JIANG YE and SHEN XINGCHEN sit at a dining table. JIANG YE is pouring wine.

SHEN XINGCHEN
Jiang Ye, I don't drink.

JIANG YE
(with a sharp laugh)
You never could handle your liquor. Some things don't change, even when lives do.

SHEN XINGCHEN
What do you mean?

JIANG YE (V.O.)
She doesn't even see it. The life that should have been mine.

JIANG YE
Feng Xiao called me. About the engagement.

SHEN XINGCHEN
We haven't seen each other in over twenty years. I don't even know what he looks like now.

JIANG YE takes a sip of wine, her knuckles white on the glass.

JIANG YE
How convenient. Some people get everything... without even trying.

INT. ALLEY - NIGHT

Back to reality, JIANG YE's face is twisted with jealousy, but her hand trembles slightly.

JIANG YE (V.O.)
They say imitation is flattery. But you... you were always the original.

SHEN XINGCHEN lies weakly on the ground. Her phone rings. She struggles to reach for it.

Just as she's about to touch the phone, JIANG YE steps on it, crushing the screen. Her heel grinds down slowly.

SHEN XINGCHEN looks at her in pain.

ON SCREEN: TO BE CONTINUED

title: The Broken Her
description: A young student is terrorized by a powerful young master, leading to a tragic fall, while her mother witnesses the horror unfold.
context:
Title: The Broken Her
Description: A young student is terrorized by a powerful young master, leading to a tragic fall, while her mother witnesses the horror unfold.

INT. APARTMENT - DAY

HAN SANSHAO wears a black tank top and studded pants, his back to the camera as he cleans his bloodied knuckles. He turns, his smile empty.

ON SCREEN: HAN is HAN SANSHAO, Third Young Master Han

HAN SANSHAO slowly walks forward and crouches down.

HAN SANSHAO
Let's try that again.

YE TANGTANG, wearing a school uniform, scrambles up from the sofa in terror and retreats toward the window.

YE TANGTANG
One more step and I'm gone.

YE TANGTANG opens the window. Her temple is bruised, her lip split, her eyes hold a final decision.

HAN SANSHAO
(chuckling)

YE TANGTANG
(terrified)
Stay back!

HAN SANSHAO
Han Corporation owns this building. I own you.

HAN SANSHAO walks to the window, looking down at YE TANGTANG.

HAN SANSHAO
Then jump. Need instructions? Jump. Jump! Jump!

HAN SANSHAO
(shouting)
JUMP!

YE TANGTANG falls from the window.
continuation:
EXT. BUILDING ENTRANCE - DAY

YE TANGTANG lies on the concrete ground, covered in blood, dead.

Her mother, YE XIANGYUN, stands in the parking lot not far away, having witnessed everything, her expression shocked and anguished.

ON SCREEN: YE TANGTANG, daughter of YE XIANGYUN

YE XIANGYUN
(silent scream)
Tangtang!

ON SCREEN: YE XIANGYUN

Time reversal effects and sound.

ON SCREEN: 2 HOURS EARLIER

EXT. STREET - DAY

HAN SANSHAO wears a studded leather jacket and sunglasses, getting out of a luxury car, followed by a group of men in black suits.

YE TANGTANG, in her school uniform, stands by the roadside holding a few small oranges.

HAN SANSHAO swaggers down the street with his men, walking toward the fruit stand where YE TANGTANG is.

HAN SANSHAO
Give me your number. Now.

He reaches for the oranges in YE TANGTANG's hands. YE XIANGYUN, who runs the fruit stand, immediately rushes over to protect her daughter.

YE XIANGYUN
Young man, what would you like to eat? We have barbecue here, and oranges, all quite sweet. Try one.

YE XIANGYUN picks up an orange and offers it to HAN SANSHAO.

HAN SANSHAO knocks over the basket full of oranges.

HAN SANSHAO
Eat what?! You're not even good enough to look at.

YE XIANGYUN shields her daughter behind her.

YE XIANGYUN
Why are you doing this?

The frame freezes on the terrified faces of YE XIANGYUN and YE TANGTANG.

title: Motherless Love
description: A mother sacrifices everything, including her own kidney and dignity, to save her son's life and send him to university.
context:
Title: Motherless Love
Description: A mother sacrifices everything, including her own kidney and dignity, to save her son's life and send him to university.

INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - DAY

Tense music.

A DOCTOR and NURSES push a hospital bed carrying WANG BIN rapidly forward. WANG CUIFEN follows anxiously beside them.

DOCTOR
Acute renal failure. We need to get him to surgery now.

WANG CUIFEN tries to follow into the operating room but is stopped by the DOCTOR.

DOCTOR
You can't go in. Wait here.

WANG CUIFEN grips the DOCTOR's arm, her voice trembling.

WANG CUIFEN
Doctor, please. Wang Bin just got into university. He's... he's everything.

DOCTOR
(gently removing her hand)
His kidney function is critically compromised. He'll likely need a transplant. But we don't have a donor match available.

WANG CUIFEN
I've already been tested. My kidney is compatible. Please.

DOCTOR
You're not an ideal candidate. The surgery carries significant risk for someone your age.

WANG CUIFEN
(quietly intense)
He's my son. I'll sign whatever you need.

The DOCTOR studies her face, sees her resolve, then nods once.

DOCTOR
Wait here. We'll do everything we can.

WANG CUIFEN watches them disappear through the doors, pressing a trembling hand to her mouth.

TRANSITION: Two months later, Xiushui Village
continuation:
EXT. XIUSHUI VILLAGE - DAY

WANG CUIFEN carries a cloth bundle, moving with determination despite her slower gait.

WANG CUIFEN (V.O.)
Two eggs a day. That's what the doctor said I could spare. Fifty eggs should be worth something.

WANG CUIFEN stops at a modest house and knocks.

WANG CUIFEN
Uncle Wang? It's Cuifen.

WANG CHENG opens the door, sees her, and his expression softens with pity.

WANG CHENG
Cuifen. I heard about the surgery. How's the boy?

WANG CUIFEN
Recovering well. But... the medical bills took everything. And now there's tuition.

She unwraps the bundle to reveal carefully packed eggs.

WANG CUIFEN
(quietly)
I know these won't cover it. But if you could lend me the rest, I'll work it off. I can clean, cook, work the fields...

WANG CHENG
(sighs heavily)
I just had to pay for my wife's treatment. We're stretched thin.

He disappears inside. WANG CUIFEN stands frozen, not breathing. He returns with an envelope.

WANG CHENG
It's not much. Three thousand. But it's yours.

WANG CUIFEN tries to refuse, but he presses it into her hand.

WANG CUIFEN
I'll repay every cent. I swear it.

WANG CHENG
Just get that boy educated. That's repayment enough.

He closes the door gently. WANG CUIFEN stands there, clutching the envelope and eggs, tears streaming down her face.

TRANSITION: A few days later

EXT. VILLAGE ENTRANCE - DAY

WANG BIN, now healthier, adjusts his backpack. WANG CUIFEN touches his sleeve nervously. VILLAGERS watch from a distance.

WANG CUIFEN
Call me when you arrive. And don't skip meals. I sent some dried mushrooms with your things.

WANG BIN
Mom, you need to rest more. The doctor said—

WANG CUIFEN
(interrupting)
—I'm fine. You just focus on your studies.

The VILLAGE CHIEF approaches, holding a small red envelope.

VILLAGE CHIEF
Wang Bin. The village collected this. For your books.

WANG BIN
Chief, I can't—

VILLAGE CHIEF
You can. Your mother carried water for eight families last week while her incision was still healing. We know what this cost.

WANG BIN stares at his mother, understanding finally hitting him fully. He takes the envelope with trembling hands.

WANG BIN
(whispering)
Mom... your kidney. The doctor told me you insisted. You didn't tell me about the pain, the work...

WANG CUIFEN
You needed to focus on getting well.

WANG BIN drops to his knees, not in performance, but as if his legs can no longer hold him.

WANG BIN
I thought I understood. I didn't.

WANG CUIFEN pulls him up, fierce and tender.

WANG CUIFEN
You understand now. That's enough.

The BUS DRIVER leans out.

DRIVER
Last call!

WANG BIN boards, looking back. WANG CUIFEN waves once, strong and steady. As the bus pulls away, she finally lets her hand drop, her shoulders sagging with exhaustion and relief.

title: Lost in Your Gentle Eyes
description: A woman sacrifices everything for her actor husband's career, only to face his coldness and betrayal after he becomes successful.
context:
Title: Lost in Your Gentle Eyes
Description: A woman sacrifices everything for her actor husband's career, only to face his coldness and betrayal after he becomes successful.

INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

GUO ROU sits on the sofa, hand pressed to her abdomen. Her phone shows Gu Yu's Weibo - Best Actor photos, fans asking about his "mystery muse."

GUO ROU (V.O.)
I was Guo Rou, VP of Starlight Entertainment. Gu Yu was a contract actor. I traded my shares for his spotlight. Now I'm trading my life for his freedom.

MONTAGE - FLASHBACK

INT. KTV - NIGHT

GUO ROU in a power suit, the only woman in a smoke-filled VIP room.

GUO ROU
(to MR. WANG)
Mr. Wang, Gu Yu's last series hit 2.3 billion views. This next one? Three-episode guarantee.

She drains her tumbler.

GUO ROU turns to a PRODUCER, pouring steadily.

GUO ROU
Director Chen wants to recast? Gu Yu's data guarantees 200M opening.

ANGLE ON: In the mirror, GUO ROU's reflection doubles over for a heartbeat.

She turns to SISTER ZHANG, BROTHER LI, BROTHER ZHAO:

GUO ROU
Sister Zhang. Brother Li. Brother Zhao. Your investments made him. Let me show you his real worth. I'll drink it all, you drink as you please.

EVERYONE
Good, good, good.

She empties her glass. They cheer.

END MONTAGE
continuation:
INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

Back to reality. GUO ROU still sits on the sofa. She drops the phone.

GUO ROU (V.O.)
He won Best Actor. I became invisible. The jasmine perfume wasn't mine.

INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)

GUO ROU in pajamas, working at the kitchen island. The door opens.

GU YU enters, drops his keys.

GUO ROU
Reshoots?

GU YU
Yeah.

He shrugs off his coat. She catches it. Her fingers brush his collar - stage makeup and jasmine.

GUO ROU
(softly)
This isn't makeup.

GU YU
(looking away)
Don't start.

He leaves. The jasmine lingers.

INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

GUO ROU sits alone, tears silent. Divorce papers beside a medical report: "Metastatic."

GUO ROU (V.O.)
Five years. I was his ladder. Now I'm his inconvenience.

GUO ROU
(to herself)
You want clean? I'll give you clean.

She picks up the pen. Signs. Throws it down. It rolls off.

Pain hits. She grabs the pill bottle: "Refills: 0." Medical report shows "Oncology."

EXT. PARK - DAY (FLASHBACK)

YOUNG GUO ROU (QINGQING), white dress, reads beneath a ginkgo tree. YOUNG GU YU approaches.

YOUNG GU YU
Qingqing!

He holds out her company badge. Behind it, two wrinkled exhibition tickets.

YOUNG GU YU
Waited six hours. I like you.

QINGQING
(taking them)
You remembered.

YOUNG GU YU
I remember everything.

She takes the tickets. Ginkgo leaves fall.

INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

GUO ROU looks at the empty pill bottle, then a ginkgo leaf outside.

GUO ROU (V.O.)
He remembers his lines. I remember everything else.

She pulls out her phone. Opens "Dr. Li - Oncology." Dials.

title: Law Proves Me Innocent, Love Sentences Me to Death
description: A renowned gold-medal lawyer gives up her career to become a housewife for six years, only to discover during her live legal consultation stream that her husband considers their marriage a fraud.
context:
Title: Law Proves Me Innocent, Love Sentences Me to Death
Description: A renowned gold-medal lawyer gives up her career to become a housewife for six years, only to discover during her live legal consultation stream that her husband considers their marriage a fraud.

INT. BEDROOM - DAY

LI YAOXUE lies awake in bed, staring at a wedding photo of her and GU SIPEI on the wall.

ANNOUNCER (V.O.)
I hereby announce that the first place winner of this year's Global Lawyer Gold Award goes to—Lawyer Li Yaoxue!

LI YAOXUE closes her eyes.

LI YAOXUE (V.O.)
Six years ago, I stood on that stage.

FLASHBACK:

LI YAOXUE, wearing a lawyer's robe, holds up a trophy at a grand awards ceremony. The audience applauds thunderously. She smiles brilliantly, full of pride and ambition.

END FLASHBACK.

LI YAOXUE (V.O.)
Now, I answer to a different call.

She touches the wedding photo, her expression bittersweet.

GU SIPEI enters, checking his watch.

GU SIPEI
You're still in bed.

LI YAOXUE
I was about to—

GU SIPEI
No need. Breakfast is ready.

LI YAOXUE
You didn't have to.

GU SIPEI
I married you for a reason, didn't I? Go eat before it gets cold.

He adjusts his tie, a practiced gesture.

GU SIPEI
I have an early meeting. Don't wait up.

He kisses her forehead—a brief, automatic gesture—then exits.

LI YAOXUE watches him leave, her smile fading slightly.

Her phone BUZZES. LI YAOXUE answers.

BEST FRIEND (O.S.)
Stream's at ten, right? You're cutting it close.

LI YAOXUE
I'll be ready.

BEST FRIEND (O.S.)
Six years, Yaoxue. You really think he'll be okay with this?

LI YAOXUE
He doesn't control everything.

BEST FRIEND (O.S.)
Just... be careful.

LI YAOXUE
I know what I'm doing.

She ends the call, a flicker of doubt crossing her face.
continuation:
INT. STUDY - DAY

LI YAOXUE sits at a desk, adjusting a microphone, phone propped before her. The livestream interface shows viewer counts climbing.

COMMENT
The legendary Lawyer Li is back!

COMMENT
Show yourself!

COMMENT
We want to see the famous gold-medal lawyer!

LI YAOXUE turns on the camera, composed.

LI YAOXUE
Good morning. This is Lawyer Li. For those joining the consultation, please follow the protocol. Active participants receive a token of appreciation.

She indicates a sleek jewelry box beside her—containing an elegant necklace.

LI YAOXUE
Let's begin. First caller?

She taps her screen.

LI YAOXUE
You're connected. Please describe your case.

GU SIPEI (O.S.)
Lawyer Li. I need to dissolve a fraudulent marriage. A woman deceived me—trapped me with false pretenses. I want to make her pay.

LI YAOXUE's breath catches. This voice—familiar yet cold, professional.

LI YAOXUE (thinking)
Isn't that... no. It can't be.

GU SIPEI (O.S.)
She pretended to be someone she wasn't. Wasted six years of my life. How do I ensure she leaves with nothing?

LI YAOXUE stares at the screen—audio only, caller ID hidden. Her knuckles whiten on the desk edge.

LI YAOXUE (V.O.)
Six years.

The stream chat EXPLODES with reactions.

FREEZE FRAME on LI YAOXUE's face—shock giving way to something harder. Determined.

TO BE CONTINUED

title: Three Lives in a Floating Dream
description: A woman seeks revenge after being left for dead, assuming a new identity to confront those who wronged her and her family.
context:
Title: Three Lives in a Floating Dream
Description: A woman seeks revenge after being left for dead, assuming a new identity to confront those who wronged her and her family.

EXT. WILDERNESS - NIGHT

JI YUNMENG (20s, trembling) has her hands bound and mouth sealed with tape. She struggles against the ropes, eyes wide with terror.

A MALE KIDNAPPER in a security uniform pins JI YUNMENG's head down with his boot.

JIANG YOURONG (40s, elegant, drunk on power) walks forward, swirling a bottle of expensive alcohol, studying Ji Yunmeng like a specimen.

JIANG YOURONG
(ice beneath silk)
You always wanted to belong. Now you always will.

JIANG YOURONG shoves JI YUNMENG to the ground. The MALE KIDNAPPER covers her with a weighted burlap sack.

The MALE KIDNAPPER drags the sack to the riverbank—it catches, then slides free. SPLASH. The river claims its offering.
continuation:
INT. HOSPITAL WARD - DAY

JI YUNMENG wears a hospital gown, her face a map of cuts. Thick bandages cover her eyes. She unwinds them slowly, revealing one eye clouded white, the other burning with focus.

JI YUNMENG (V.O.)
(ritualistic whisper)
Ji Yunmeng drowned in that river. These scars are her tombstone. Nian Zihe is what the water spat back.

EXT. CEMETERY - DAY

Rain hammers down. NIAN ZIHE, in a black suit, holds an umbrella over two fresh graves—her own clothes soaked through. She places a shattered whiskey bottle and a security badge on the wet earth. Her expression is stone.

NIAN ZIHE (V.O.)
They thought the river would bury their secrets. I'll teach them how secrets are buried.

title: Late Night God of Cooking
description: A powerful woman seeking to unify the city's underworld confronts a notorious gang leader, leading to a fateful night at a mysterious restaurant.
context:
Title: Late Night God of Cooking
Description: A powerful woman seeking to unify the city's underworld confronts a notorious gang leader, leading to a fateful night at a mysterious restaurant.

INT. WAREHOUSE - NIGHT

A row of bound men under a single swinging bulb.

WANG XINRAN (V.O.)
Grandfather's final test: unify Jiangcheng or walk away.

ZHANG HU drops to the concrete, blood at his mouth. WANG XINRAN sits, impassive.

ZHANG HU
(struggling)
My brother will burn this place down.

WANG XINRAN
Bring him my offer: the 20 clubs for your life.

She signals. Her men cut ZHANG HU's ropes. He scrambles for the door.

WANG XINRAN (V.O.)
Mercy is just delayed war.

EXT. STREET - NIGHT

ZHANG BAO in white, his crew in black. They move with disciplined steps.

ZHANG HU limps into view, clutching his ribs.

ZHANG HU
Brother... Wang family.

ZHANG BAO
(stops)
They don't touch our business.

ZHANG HU
She does. Says she'll be waiting.

A SHENG
The men need food before a fight.

ZHANG BAO
(nods)
We'll eat. Then decide.

Ahead, a lone restaurant glows. Sign: "Late Night God of Cooking."

ZHANG HU
Just there.

A SHENG
Pricey block for a diner.

ZHANG BAO
Good. Neutral ground.

They head toward it.
continuation:
INT. LATE NIGHT GOD OF COOKING RESTAURANT - NIGHT

Empty. A chalkboard: "Today's Meditation: Egg Fried Rice 99998"

ZHANG BAO, ZHANG HU, and A SHENG sit.

ZHANG BAO
Menu?

The PROPRIETOR appears, calm and exact. He hands a single-page menu.

ZHANG HU
(pointing at board)
Ninety-nine thousand?

A SHENG
(to Proprietor)
For rice?

PROPRIETOR
For clarity.

SUBORDINATE A
Brother Bao, this is—

ZHANG BAO
(raising hand)
Three.

PROPRIETOR
One per person. House rules.

He walks away. ZHANG BAO watches him go.

ZHANG BAO (V.O.)
Before war, my grandfather fed us. "Taste what you're losing."

Kitchen sounds: wok on flame, oil sizzling.

The PROPRIETOR returns, three bowls. Steam rises.

ZHANG BAO
(scents it)
WANG XINRAN eats here?

PROPRIETOR
She pays full price.

ZHANG BAO
(smiles)
Honest.

He eats. Shoulders drop. The men watch, uncertain.

A SHENG
Brother? The crew's waiting.

ZHANG BAO
Tell them to wait.

He takes another bite, slow.

ZHANG BAO
(to Proprietor)
She's outside.

PROPRIETOR
Always.

ZHANG BAO
(to A SHENG)
The clubs are hers. But not my men.

He stands, leaves cash on the table.

ZHANG BAO
(to Proprietor)
One more. For her.

The PROPRIETOR nods, already reaching for a fourth bowl. ZHANG BAO exits. His men follow, confused.

title: Schemes and Rouge in the Deep Mansion
description: A maid is reborn after suffering a tragic death and vows revenge against the mistress who destroyed her.
context:
Title: Schemes and Rouge in the Deep Mansion
Description: A maid is reborn after suffering a tragic death and vows revenge against the mistress who destroyed her.

INT. LUXURIOUS HALL - DAY

Light streams through the latticed windows into the antique hall. Several maids stand solemnly on both sides. Maid HAI TANG kneels on the carpet in the center.

ZHAO MANHIANG, the mistress, wears luxurious red brocade robes and an exquisite headdress. She sits elegantly, sipping tea.

ZHAO MANHIANG
Hai Tang, the Young Master returns tonight. You will attend him.

HAI TANG keeps her head lowered, her fingers pressing into the carpet weave.

HAI TANG (V.O.)
Didn't I die?

MONTAGE - FLASHBACK

INT. HALL - DAY (MEMORY)

The image turns black and white, filled with oppression. HAI TANG looks at ZHAO MANHIANG with determination.

HAI TANG
I would rather die than become a concubine!

HAI TANG throws her teacup to the floor. It shatters.

ZHAO MANHIANG
(frozen smile)
How... disappointing.

Maid DU JUAN steps forward, her movements precise.

DU JUAN
The Mistress shows you kindness.

DU JUAN's slap is sudden. HAI TANG falls, blood at her lip. DU JUAN grabs her hair, forcing her head back.

DU JUAN
(quietly, for Hai Tang alone)
You should have said yes.

ZHAO MANHIANG
(coldly)
Take her to learn her place.
continuation:
INT. WOODSHED - DAY (MEMORY)

HAI TANG is secured to a wooden post. Her hands are locked with iron chains.

HAI TANG
(struggling)
Let me go!

ZHAO MANHIANG (O.S.)
Since Miss Hai Tang thinks so highly of herself...

ZHAO MANHIANG stands at the woodshed door, speaking to two household guards.

ZHAO MANHIANG
...find someone to teach her humility.

GUARD (O.S.)
It will be done, Young Mistress.

The door closes. Darkness. The sound of a struggle. A muffled scream. Then silence.

INT. DILAPIDATED HUT - DAY (MEMORY)

ON SCREEN: Three Years Later

Heavy snow falls. HAI TANG, gaunt and limping, empties a chamber pot. She stumbles, catches herself against the wall.

HAI TANG
(to herself)
They all watched... the cup breaks...

She falls. The snow begins to cover her form.

HAI TANG (V.O.)
This time, I watch too.

END MONTAGE

INT. LUXURIOUS HALL - DAY

HAI TANG snaps back to reality, her spine straightening a fraction.

DU JUAN
The Young Mistress is asking you a question. Are you deaf?

HAI TANG raises her head, a slight smile on her face.

HAI TANG
This servant has learned her place. Whatever you wish.

ZHAO MANHIANG
(satisfied)
Good. Obedience becomes you.

HAI TANG
(submissively)
Yes.

ZHAO MANHIANG
No time like the present. Tonight.

HAI TANG bows and kowtows.

HAI TANG
This servant obeys.

As HAI TANG slowly raises her head, her eyes meet DU JUAN's for a heartbeat—then turn sharp with focused hatred.

HAI TANG (V.O.)
Zhao Manhiang, I remember everything.

ZHAO MANHIANG picks up her teacup, a satisfied smile on her lips.

ZHAO MANHIANG
See that she is prepared.

ZHAO MANHIANG watches Hai Tang exit, her expression unreadable. She turns the jade hairpin in her fingers.

SCENE END

title: After My Scumbag Husband's Terminal Diagnosis, I Went Crazy
description: A woman discovers her husband has a terminal illness and desperately seeks to save him, unaware of his true nature.
context:
Title: After My Scumbag Husband's Terminal Diagnosis, I Went Crazy
Description: A woman discovers her husband has a terminal illness and desperately seeks to save him, unaware of his true nature.

INT. HOSPITAL CONSULTATION ROOM - DAY

SHU YUAN sits rigidly, watching DR. LIN LEYAN examine a test file.

SHU YUAN
Leyan, please. Just tell me what it says.

LIN LEYAN
His creatinine reading is 731. Normal levels are below 133.

SHU YUAN
(pleading)
I don't understand what that means. Just tell me.

LIN LEYAN
(quietly)
End-stage renal failure. Without immediate intervention...

SHU YUAN
No. There has to be a mistake. Run the tests again.

LIN LEYAN
I already have. Three times.

Lin reaches across the desk, covering Shu Yuan's trembling hands.

LIN LEYAN
(gently)
Yuanyuan, I know you want to fix this. But you have to accept—

SHU YUAN
(cutting in)
A transplant. That's the answer, right?

LIN LEYAN
If we can find a matching donor within a week... there's a chance.

SHU YUAN
Mine. Take mine.

LIN LEYAN
You know it's not that simple. And the procedure costs at least 500,000 yuan.

SHU YUAN
(urgent)
I'll find the money. Just promise me you'll call me the moment a donor matches.

LIN LEYAN
I'll put him on every priority list I can. But Yuanyuan, he deserves to know. It's his life.

SHU YUAN
(firmly)
Not yet. Let me protect him from this until I have a solution.

Shu Yuan stands, already dialing her phone.

LIN LEYAN
Wait. I work with a medical foundation. Let me file an emergency grant application for your family.

SHU YUAN
(grateful)
Thank you.

Shu Yuan heads for the door.

LIN LEYAN
Yuanyuan?

Shu Yuan pauses, hand on the doorknob.

LIN LEYAN
You promised to take care of yourself too. Don't forget that.

SHU YUAN
I won't.

Shu Yuan exits, the door clicking softly shut.
continuation:
INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR - DAY

LIN LEYAN leans against the wall near the elevators, speaking with a YOUNG NURSE.

YOUNG NURSE
Dr. Lin, you just prioritized your friend's husband like that?

LIN LEYAN
He's family. I have to try.

GU ZEYANG watches from behind a pillar, a wilted bouquet in his hands.

LIN LEYAN
(continuing)
Seven days. If we don't find a kidney donor by then, I'm not sure she'll survive the month.

YOUNG NURSE
It's always the devoted ones who ignore their own symptoms. Now it's half a million yuan for a miracle.

LIN LEYAN
(sharp)
We don't discuss patients in public corridors. Even when it's personal.

The nurse nods and hurries away.

GU ZEYANG (V.O.)
Seven days. So that's her secret. Hiding uremia from me.

He crushes the flower stems in his fist.

GU ZEYANG (V.O.)
500,000 yuan to save her? She really thinks I'm that desperate.

SHU YUAN rounds the corner, her phone pressed to her ear, and stops short.

SHU YUAN
(surprised)
Zeyang? What are you doing here?

GU ZEYANG looks at her coldly, the mangled flowers dangling in his grip.

GU ZEYANG
Came to check on my "busy" wife. Heard you haven't been feeling well.

title: Sleepless in Harbor City
description: A woman discovers her mysterious lover from a passionate night may be connected to her own husband, as family intrigue unfolds around a dying patriarch's fortune.
context:
Title: Sleepless in Harbor City
Description: A woman discovers her mysterious lover from a passionate night may be connected to her own husband, as family intrigue unfolds around a dying patriarch's fortune.

INT. BILLIARD ROOM - NIGHT

An ambiguously lit billiard room. Playing cards are scattered on the table.

SONG QINGYUN, a woman wearing a black lace mask and a black slip dress, is leaning over to take a shot. LIANG YOUJIN, a handsome man in a white shirt and suspenders, approaches from behind, teaching her hand-over-hand.

LIANG YOUJIN
(voice low, controlled)
Does your husband know about us?

SONG QINGYUN straightens up, holding the cue, and turns to look at him. Her eyes glitter behind the mask.

SONG QINGYUN
I'm interested in you.

She pauses, her gaze teasing, searching.

SONG QINGYUN (CONT'D)
And him?

SONG QINGYUN turns and walks away, sitting on a nearby sofa. She picks up a rose-shaped candle from the table and blows on it, the flame dancing in her palm.

LIANG YOUJIN walks over and stands in front of her.

SONG QINGYUN
(looking up at him)
Divorce. Maybe he's even better at playing games than I am.

LIANG YOUJIN suddenly reaches out and grabs her chin, his eyes sharp.

LIANG YOUJIN
(low voice, serious)
What did you say?

SONG QINGYUN
(fearless)
I said...

She reaches up and removes her mask, revealing her delicate features.

SONG QINGYUN (CONT'D)
Divorce. You...

Before she can finish, LIANG YOUJIN leans down and kisses her. They kiss passionately as he lifts her up and presses her onto the sofa.

MONTAGE:
- The two kiss passionately on the sofa.
- LIANG YOUJIN lifts SONG QINGYUN into his arms.
- They kiss in front of a mirror, images overlapping.
END MONTAGE.

LIANG YOUJIN (V.O.)
Baby, this is your punishment. You've been very naughty.
continuation:
INT. HOTEL BEDROOM - MORNING

Sunlight streams through the curtains into the room. SONG QINGYUN lies in the disheveled bed, drowsy.

Her phone rings on the nightstand. She reaches out to answer it.

SONG QINGYUN
(hoarse voice)
Who is it?

LIANG YOUJIN (V.O.)
(on phone, voice cold, formal)
Liang Youjin.

SONG QINGYUN
(confused)
Who's Liang Youjin?

LIANG YOUJIN (V.O.)
(on phone)
Your husband. The one you married three years ago. Don't tell me you've forgotten.

SONG QINGYUN snaps awake, sitting up abruptly and wrapping herself in the blanket. Her knuckles whiten around the phone.

SONG QINGYUN (V.O.)
Why does this feel like being caught in bed?

SONG QINGYUN
(into phone, tight)
What do you need?

LIANG YOUJIN (V.O.)
(on phone)
Tomorrow there's a family banquet at the old Harbor City estate. Grandfather is gravely ill. We need to be there together.

Beat.

LIANG YOUJIN (V.O.) (CONT'D)
(on phone)
The succession arrangements will be announced. Try not to embarrass the family.

SONG QINGYUN (V.O.)
The Liang family patriarch's time is running out. It seems the battle for the billion-dollar fortune has already begun beneath the surface.

SONG QINGYUN
I'll return as soon as possible.

LIANG YOUJIN (V.O.)
(on phone)
Wait. Baby, drink more warm water. Your voice sounds so hoarse.

FLASHBACK: Brief flashes of their intimate moments from last night—a whispered "baby" in the dark.

SONG QINGYUN freezes upon hearing "baby" and noticing her hoarse voice, her eyes filling with doubt. She hangs up, staring at the phone.

SONG QINGYUN (V.O.)
That voice... why does it sound so familiar?

She gets up and picks up the black dress from the floor beside the bed. A small card slips from the dress.

She picks up the card. It reads: "Looking forward to seeing you again, my butterfly," with a butterfly drawn beside it.

SONG QINGYUN looks at the card thoughtfully. She walks to the nightstand and holds the card near a lit candle.

SONG QINGYUN (V.O.)
I won't play with the same man twice.

The card slowly burns in the flame. She watches it turn to ash, her hand steady but her eyes conflicted.

INT. MANSION HALLWAY - DAY

SONG QINGYUN, now dressed in an elegant white qipao, walks down the hallway with dignified grace. Her face is a perfect mask of composure.

At the end of the hallway, LIANG YOUJIN waits for her. Seeing her approach, a smile curves his lips as he unconsciously touches the butterfly tattoo behind his ear.

LIANG YOUJIN (V.O.)
It's been seven years, little butterfly. We're finally going to officially meet.

He watches SONG QINGYUN, his gaze complex and profound. She meets his eyes, her expression giving nothing away—but her fingers, hidden in her sleeve, tremble slightly.

title: The Stock Market Tycoon's Comeback
description: A legendary stock trader known as "One Six Middle Road" faces a coordinated attack from rival traders who crash his stocks, forcing him to choose between saving himself or protecting the retail investors who trust him.
context:
Title: The Stock Market Tycoon's Comeback
Description: A legendary stock trader known as "One Six Middle Road" faces a coordinated attack from rival traders who crash his stocks, forcing him to choose between saving himself or protecting the retail investors who trust him.

INT. LU MINGYU'S OFFICE - DAY

LU MINGYU (on-screen text: LU MINGYU) sits surrounded by six monitors casting blue light on his face. Empty coffee cups litter his desk. His fingers dance across the keyboard with mechanical precision, but his jaw is clenched tight.

INT. HAICHENG SECURITIES TRADING HALL - DAY

The hall is packed. RETAIL INVESTORS crane their necks at the giant LED screen, phones clutched like lifelines. The air smells of stale cigarettes and desperation.

OLD INVESTOR (on-screen text: OLD INVESTOR)
(trembling finger pointing at screen)
There! 9898! Brother Six's fingerprints all over it!

FEMALE INVESTOR (on-screen text: FEMALE INVESTOR)
(gripping her child's hand)
Sea Cucumber Pigeon... up eight percent already!

The screen erupts in green. A digital stamp appears: LIMIT UP.

CROWD
(gasping)
He did it... he actually did it...

INT. LU MINGYU'S OFFICE - DAY

LU MINGYU's shoulders drop a fraction. He reaches for a cold teacup, hand trembling slightly before steadying.

HE XIAOQUN (V.O.)
(through headset, breathless)
Big brother... we locked the board.

INT. HE XIAOQUN'S OFFICE - DAY

HE XIAOQUN (on-screen text: HE XIAOQUN) stares at his screen, knuckles white on his mouse.

HE XIAOQUN
(low, urgent)
Thirty million in paper profits. Should we start peeling—

INT. LU MINGYU'S OFFICE - DAY

LU MINGYU's expression hardens. He types a single command.

LU MINGYU
Quiet. Something's wrong.

On his main monitor, the LIMIT UP stamp flickers. Then vanishes.
continuation:
INT. HE XIAOQUN'S OFFICE - DAY

HE XIAOQUN shoots to his feet, his chair slamming backward.

HE XIAOQUN (V.O.)
(desperate)
No, no, no—it's dumping!

HE XIAOQUN
(to himself, cracking)
That's not us. Who's hitting the bid?

INT. LU MINGYU'S OFFICE - DAY

LU MINGYU's face is stone, but his throat bobs in a swallow.

LU MINGYU
(into headset)
Hold your position. Don't chase it down.

INT. DARK ROOM - NIGHT

Four figures in animal masks glow in monitor light. MA JIALOU (on-screen text: MA JIALOU) leans back, fingers steepled.

MA JIALOU
The lemmings are panicking. Perfect.

CHIP MONKEY (on-screen text: CHIP MONKEY)
(voice filtered)
Order flow shows retail stop-losses triggering like dominoes.

LITTLE SQUID (on-screen text: LITTLE SQUID)
(tilting head)
One Six's reputation won't survive this.

TECH HERO (on-screen text: TECH HERO)
(leaning forward)
He thought he could rewrite the rules. Teach the sheep to think.

MA JIALOU
(smiling behind the mask)
Now watch him bleed. Either he cuts Zhiyuan Tech to save himself...

CHIP MONKEY
Or he goes down with them.

LITTLE SQUID
Either way, we feast.

They laugh - a cold, synthesized chorus.

The video call windows blink out, one by one. Alone, CHIP MONKEY removes his monkey mask, revealing GU KAI's face.

GU KAI (on-screen text: GU KAI)
(cold laugh)
When I become the real One Six Middle Road...

INT. LU MINGYU'S OFFICE - DAY

HE XIAOQUN paces behind LU MINGYU, who hasn't moved in twenty minutes. The screen shows cascading red.

HE XIAOQUN
Big brother, Zhiyuan Tech is our lifeblood. If we don't sell now—

LU MINGYU
(quietly)
Seventy thousand retail accounts followed us in.

INT. HE XIAOQUN'S OFFICE - DAY

HE XIAOQUN
(pleading)
They'd do the same to you! Every man for himself!

INT. LU MINGYU'S OFFICE - DAY

LU MINGYU finally turns from the screen. His eyes are hollow but burning.

LU MINGYU
Then why did they open accounts at One Six Middle Road?

HE XIAOQUN's mouth snaps shut. LU MINGYU looks back at the screen.

LU MINGYU
Prep the war chest. We're catching the knife.

INT. HAICHENG SECURITIES TRADING HALL - DAY

The hall is silent except for choked sobs. The OLD INVESTOR sits on the floor, back against the wall.

FEMALE INVESTOR
(voice breaking)
Brother Six wouldn't... not like this.

OLD INVESTOR
(bitter laugh)
They all say that. Until the bill comes due.

The screen blinks. A massive green order appears. Then another. And another.

FEMALE INVESTOR
(whisper)
Look...

The OLD INVESTOR squints. The stock halts its freefall. Begins to climb.

OLD INVESTOR
(softly, almost prayer)
He's... buying?

ON SCREEN: The price ticks up. Slowly. Deliberately.

title: Rebirth of the Scorned Wife
description: A modern woman dies from overwork and wakes up in the 1980s, reborn in the body of an overweight woman who was mocked and driven to suicide.
context:
Title: Rebirth of the Scorned Wife
Description: A modern woman dies from overwork and wakes up in the 1980s, reborn in the body of an overweight woman who was mocked and driven to suicide.

INT. MOURNING HALL - DAY

Several VILLAGERS struggle to carry a black coffin, but quickly lose their grip and drop it on the ground.

VILLAGER A
Christ almighty! My spine's done. She's gotta be three hundred pounds if she's an ounce.

VILLAGER B
(puffing)
Like dragging a sack of cement through mud.

VILLAGER C
Always parading around in city dresses. Made a proper spectacle at Xiao Qin's factory last week, didn't she?

HAN RUOLIN
(walking over with a water cup)
Here, big brother. Wet your throat.

VILLAGER C
(taking the cup)
Aiya, only you think of us.

HAN RUOLIN
It's a shame, though. She's already gone, and Xiao Qin won't even show his face.

VILLAGER C
(slyly)
Good riddance. Now he can remarry proper. You and him, that would be—

VILLAGER B
(stuttering)
A... a... a perfect match!

VILLAGER C
Right, right, right.

HAN RUOLIN
(laughing softly)
Big brother, please.

VILLAGER A
Alright, enough resting. Let's finish this.

VILLAGER A
Come on, heave.

Everyone gathers around the coffin again to lift it.

VILLAGER A
Three, two, one!

Just as they exert force, a light sound comes from inside the coffin. Everyone stops.

VILLAGER B
...I heard something.
continuation:
VILLAGER A
No, you didn't. Again!

They prepare to lift. A louder THUMP echoes. The lid shifts.

VILLAGER A
(terrified)
God... God help us!

HAN RUOLIN
(panicking)
Quick! Nail it down! Before she—

HAN RUOLIN (V.O.)
Du Qiao, you're finally dead. Your husband, your son... they're mine now.

Suddenly, the coffin lid is thrown open by a tremendous force, and a hand reaches out. Everyone screams and backs away.

LI YUE (in Du Qiao's body) sits up from the coffin and stretches.

LI YUE
(raspy, disoriented)
...server migration at three AM... need to file the incident report...

LI YUE looks around, completely bewildered.

LI YUE
Who are you people? Why are you dressed like this? Why am I in a box?

HAN RUOLIN
(trembling, pointing)
Are you... human or ghost?

VILLAGER D
Back! Back!

LI YUE tries to climb out but discovers her body is abnormally large. She stares at her hands.

LI YUE
What is...? This isn't my body. My body was... I was at my desk. The IPO deadline. I just needed...

Memory fragments flash: A floral dress, seams straining. Her husband's face—disgust. Concrete walls echoing with laughter. Ice-cold water filling her lungs.

LI YUE
(clutching her chest)
No. Oh no. I died. I died and I've been... I've been...

She looks at the mourners' 1980s clothing. At the wooden coffin. Her eyes widen with shock.

LI YUE
(whisper)
...transmigrated.

HAN RUOLIN
Du Qiao...?

LI YUE's head snaps up. Her eyes—sharp, modern—fix on Han Ruolin.

LI YUE
Who the hell is Du Qiao?

title: The Whole City Knows
description: A woman who won 10 million in the lottery becomes the subject of a live broadcast reality show, revealing her true character when her impoverished mother begs for help with her father's medical bills.
context:
Title: The Whole City Knows
Description: A woman who won 10 million in the lottery becomes the subject of a live broadcast reality show, revealing her true character when her impoverished mother begs for help with her father's medical bills.

INT. BROADCAST STUDIO - DAY

A large broadcast studio. Several GUESTS sit on stage, and the audience fills the seats below. A FEMALE HOST stands at center stage.

HOST
(facing the camera)
Ten million dollars. That's what Wang Lingzhi won. That's also how many opinions this city suddenly has. Tonight, we watch—and you judge.

HOST
Now, let me introduce our life observers. Professor of Philosophy at Jiangdong University, Professor Li Ren.

CLOSE-UP: Professor Li Ren smiles thinly and waves. Sparse applause.

HOST
Chairman of Fuyao Group, Mr. Cao De.

CLOSE-UP: Mr. Cao De nods, eyes sharp. Polite applause.

HOST
Our nation's famous commentator, Teacher Liu Fang.

CLOSE-UP: Teacher Liu Fang stands and bows. Scattered applause.

HOST
Our observers will score Miss Wang Lingzhi's choices. Next, please watch.
continuation:
INT. LUXURY GOODS STORE - DAY

MONTAGE: WANG LINGZHI, designer suit and sunglasses, walks into a luxury goods store. STORE CLERKS line up and bow.

STORE CLERKS
(in unison)
Miss Wang.

CUSTOMER A and CUSTOMER B notice WANG LINGZHI and whisper.

CUSTOMER A
There she is. The lottery girl. Cameras everywhere.

CUSTOMER B
Poor woman. No such thing as a private sin anymore.

A STORE CLERK walks up carrying a black handbag.

STORE CLERK
Miss Wang, this is your "New Life." Please inspect it.

WANG LINGZHI
(examining the bag)
New Life. This name suits me. How much?

STORE CLERK
One million.
