Severe anaphylaxis. Another ten minutes, we might've lost her.

The DOCTOR shakes his head and leaves.

A XIAO walks to the bedside and holds NUANNUAN's hand.

A XIAO
Nuannuan, I'm so sorry. I forgot—

NUANNUAN opens her eyes and looks at him.

NUANNUAN
(raspy)
You celebrated her birthday... in our home.

A XIAO
Nuannuan, you...

A XIAO's phone rings. He walks aside to answer it.

A XIAO
Hello?

ZHANG YUYAN (V.O.)
(crying)
Brother A Xiao? I'm at the office alone. The security alarm went off. I think someone's breaking in.

A XIAO
Yuyan, get out. Call building security—

ZHANG YUYAN (V.O.)
Please come. I'm scared.

A XIAO hangs up and turns to NUANNUAN.

NUANNUAN (O.S.)
Go save your secretary.

A XIAO
Nuannuan, it's a real emergency. I'll be back.

A XIAO turns and hurries away.

NUANNUAN lies in bed, devastated, tears streaming down her face.

After a while, NUANNUAN struggles to sit up and picks up her phone. She scrolls through it—seeing a photo of A Xiao and Zhang Yuyan, heads together at a restaurant, posted last night.

NUANNUAN dials.

NUANNUAN
Mom. Tell Gu Chuan I accept. And move the wedding up.

She hangs up, staring at the ceiling. The tears stop. Her expression hardens into resolve.

title: Giving Up Hope
description: A woman endures emotional neglect and abuse from her fiancé who cannot let go of his ex-girlfriend, until a broken cup becomes the final straw.
context:
Title: Giving Up Hope
Description: A woman endures emotional neglect and abuse from her fiancé who cannot let go of his ex-girlfriend, until a broken cup becomes the final straw.

INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT

WEN ZHIXIAN, wearing a white shirt, curls up in the corner of the bathroom, letting cold water from the shower pour over her entire body. She covers her face with both hands, crying silently.

WEN ZHIXIAN (V.O.)
Hope doesn't fracture. It erodes.

INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

WEN ZHIXIAN carries a cup of coffee, smiling as she walks toward GU YUCHEN, who sits on the sofa reviewing contracts.

WEN ZHIXIAN
Yuchen, I made that Ethiopian blend you like—

GU YUCHEN doesn't look up, his hand swats impatiently.

GU YUCHEN
I said no distractions.

GU YUCHEN stands up abruptly, knocking into WEN ZHIXIAN. Coffee spills across his sleeve. He curses under his breath, walking away without a glance back. WEN ZHIXIAN watches his back, the smile fading.

WEN ZHIXIAN (V.O.)
His words were sharpening.

INT. LIVING ROOM - DAY

WEN ZHIXIAN sits on the sofa, phone pressed to her ear, her posture eager.

WEN ZHIXIAN
(into the phone)
Yuchen, you won't believe what happened at the gallery today—

The line disconnects. She stares at the screen, the moment stretching.

WEN ZHIXIAN (V.O.)
Every silence grew heavier.
continuation:
EXT. STREET SIDE - NIGHT

GU YUCHEN is drunk, leaning against a utility pole. WEN ZHIXIAN supports him, struggling with his weight.

GU YUCHEN suddenly grips her chin, his eyes focusing with drunken clarity.

GU YUCHEN
(murmuring)
Your jaw... it's all wrong. Not like hers.

He shoves her away. WEN ZHIXIAN stumbles, catching herself against a wall. She watches him sink to the ground, the truth pooling like blood.

WEN ZHIXIAN (V.O.)
Truths spoken like accidents.

INT. BATHROOM - NIGHT

Flashback. WEN ZHIXIAN breaks down crying under the shower.

INT. LIVING ROOM - NIGHT

WEN ZHIXIAN kneels on the floor, cleaning up broken coffee cup pieces. Her injured hand trembles. Her gaze falls on a mug with a cartoon pattern, tucked in the back of the cabinet—different from their usual set.

WEN ZHIXIAN (V.O.)
But I never imagined the last straw would be something so small.

She reaches for it. Her fingers slip. The mug falls, shattering on the tile—not with a crash, but a soft, final sound.

GU YUCHEN hears the sound and rushes over.

GU YUCHEN
(roaring)
Wen Zhixian!

He glares at WEN ZHIXIAN furiously.

GU YUCHEN
Didn't I tell you not to touch this cup? Do you know it's...

WEN ZHIXIAN
(interrupting, voice breaking)
I know. I know it's from her. But I didn't—

GU YUCHEN
Don't touch me! You think I'll believe you? You're just narrow-minded, you can't tolerate anything related to Zhaomu!

WEN ZHIXIAN
(her own fury rising)
Then why is it here? In our home? With our wedding invitations on the table!

She points to the mantle where a photo of GU YUCHEN and another woman sits half-hidden behind a plant.

GU YUCHEN
(quietly venomous)
Don't you dare speak her name.

He raises his hand. The slap cracks across her face. WEN ZHIXIAN's head snaps. She touches her cheek, disbelief giving way to something harder.

Flashback. GU YUCHEN fastens a pearl bracelet on WEN ZHIXIAN's wrist, his voice earnest.

GU YUCHEN (V.O.)
This can be our beginning.

Back to reality. WEN ZHIXIAN looks at the bracelet. She twists it until the string snaps. Pearls scatter, bouncing across the hardwood.

WEN ZHIXIAN
(not a question)
You asked me to build a life on ground you never left.

She places the broken string in GU YUCHEN's hand and turns to leave.

GU YUCHEN stands frozen, the string coiled in his palm like a dead thing.

WEN ZHIXIAN pauses at the threshold. She looks back. Then, methodically, she walks back and slaps him—not in fury, but with the weight of finality. The sound echoes.

ON SCREEN: TO BE CONTINUED...

title: The Debt of Honor
description: A man returns from prison expecting to fulfill his engagement, only to be rejected and humiliated by the family he once sacrificed for—discovering loyalty is a currency they no longer accept.
context:
Title: The Debt of Honor
Description: A man returns from prison expecting to fulfill his engagement, only to be rejected and humiliated by the family he once sacrificed for—discovering loyalty is a currency they no longer accept.

EXT. LIANG FAMILY ESTATE COURTYARD - DAY

Aerial shot of a magnificent Chinese-style mansion with a plaque reading "LIANG RESIDENCE." The courtyard is decorated with lanterns and filled with guests attending a banquet. A lion dance troupe performs in the yard, drums and gongs resounding, creating a festive atmosphere.

MASTER LIANG, the elderly founder of the Liang family, wearing a brown robe, top hat, and holding a cane, walks spiritedly on the red carpet, greeting guests.

GUEST
Master.

GUEST (O.S.)
Master, Master.

A SUBORDINATE rushes up to MASTER LIANG.

SUBORDINATE
He's here.

MASTER LIANG
King Qi Shan?

SUBORDINATE
(shakes head)

MASTER LIANG
Show him in.

MASTER LIANG turns to welcome the honored guest. However, standing at the entrance is a plainly dressed man with prison-hardened eyes - ZHAO GANGZI. The lions fall silent.

MASTER LIANG
(strained)
Zhao Gangzi.

ZHAO GANGZI walks slowly on the red carpet as surrounding guests cast strange looks. MASTER LIANG, the second daughter LIANG RUORU, and her mother LI JINLIAN all look at him with carefully masked panic.
continuation:
LIANG RUORU
(whisper-harsh)
You were told not to come today.

ZHAO GANGZI
The engagement was set three years ago. I'm here to honor it.

LI JINLIAN
(sharp)
You've tracked prison filth into our home. Did you truly believe a promise could survive three years of shame?

LIANG RUORU
(tightly)
You shouldn't have done this.

ZHAO GANGZI
(steady)
Are you breaking your word?

LIANG RUORU
Because you are an ex-convict.

ZHAO GANGZI
(quietly fierce)
I took that record for you. For this family.

LIANG RUORU
(crossing her arms)
The past is ash. Where's your proof?

ZHAO GANGZI
(shaken)
What?

ZHAO GANGZI stares at LIANG RUORU, his hand moving to his coat pocket where something heavy rests.

title: The Divine King Couple
description: Two legendary warriors, the Northern God Lord and the Southern Goddess King, defend their nation's borders before resigning to reunite and live ordinary lives together.
context:
Title: The Divine King Couple
Description: Two legendary warriors, the Northern God Lord and the Southern Goddess King, defend their nation's borders before resigning to reunite and live ordinary lives together.

INT. NORTHERN BORDER OF DAXIA - NIGHT

ON SCREEN: Northern Border of Daxia

Torches sputter in churned mud. The stench of blood hangs thick. Daxia's broken shield wall holds by a thread. Bodies of the fallen litter the scorched earth, armor twisted like parchment.

A BARBARIAN GENERAL, scar crossing his blind eye, wipes gore from his mace and laughs through broken teeth.

BARBARIAN GENERAL
The Northern God Lord is myth! This land is ours!

His horde ROARS, pounding battered shields. They surge forward—

—And freeze. 

From the writhing fog, shadows emerge. Black armor that drinks the firelight. At their head, CHU XIAO. No flourish. No proclamation. Just presence.

The BARBARIAN GENERAL's good eye narrows.

BARBARIAN GENERAL
Impossible. He hasn't been seen in—

CHU XIAO
(softly, but it cuts through the din)
Ten years. I was beginning to think you'd forgotten.

Silence. Then the MASKED PERSON at the General's side draws twin curved blades, moonlight dancing on the steel.

MASKED PERSON
Then let us remind you why you hid.

CHU XIAO moves. Not fast—inevitable. His sword flares to life, flame racing up the blade like a living thing. He meets the Masked Person mid-leap.

The clash rings like a temple bell. CHU XIAO flows between three opponents. Each strike is precise, almost meditative. A Barbarian lunges—CHU XIAO pivots, the blade passing through where he was. The soldier falls, armor glowing red.

The MASKED PERSON presses a desperate attack. CHU XIAO deflects, guiding the energy past his shoulder, then reverses. The pommel strikes the mask—crack—the figure crumples.

The BARBARIAN GENERAL charges. CHU XIAO plants his feet. The dragon tattoo on his neck begins to glow. Behind him, a massive phantom takes shape—flaming scales, ancient eyes. Not a beast. A guardian.

The phantom dragon ROARS. The Barbarian army breaks. They flee into the darkness.

CHU XIAO watches them go. The flame on his sword dies. He looks at the blood on his hands—not all of it enemy—and wipes it on his cloak.

EXT. SOUTHERN DOMAIN OF DAXIA - DAY

ON SCREEN: Southern Domain of Daxia

Morning mist clings to rice paddies turned to mire. The corpses of Daxia's southern legion steam in the cold. MU BINGQING stands atop a burial mound, her silver-white armor unstained amid the carnage.

ON SCREEN: Southern Goddess King - Mu Bingqing

Below, a thousand warriors of Wo kneel in surrender. Only their GENERAL remains standing, his banner trembling in his grip.

WO GENERAL
You can't kill us all. The empire will just send more.

MU BINGQING
Not for ten years.

She descends the mound. Her sword, still sheathed, hums with contained power. The air around her grows brittle.

WO GENERAL
You'd massacre prisoners?

MU BINGQING stops before him. She reaches out—not to strike. She touches the banner. Frost spreads from her fingertip, racing up the silk, turning it to brittle crystal. It shatters.

MU BINGQING
No. I'm taking your will to fight.

She walks past him. The WO WARRIORS flinch as she passes, but she touches none of them. They simply... break. Not bodies. Spirits. They drop their weapons and stare at their hands as if forgetting what they're for.

At the tree line, she turns back. The entire invasion force sits in the mud, weeping.
continuation:
EXT. SOUTHERN DOMAIN MILITARY CAMP - DAY

MU BINGQING unbuckles her pauldrons. They fall with a heavy CLANG. Her lieutenant, MEI, catches them, eyes wide.

MEI
My lady, you can't—

MU BINGQING
I already have.

She's down to quilted gambeson now. From a chest, she takes a simple jade pendant—civilian jewelry. Slips it on.

MEI
The council will demand your return. The peace won't hold without your name.

MU BINGQING
My name is just a word. Teach them to remember the dead instead.

She pulls out the folded marriage certificate. It's worn at the creases, read a thousand times. She touches Chu Xiao's written name, a ghost of a smile on her lips, then tucks it away.

MU BINGQING
The Southern Goddess King died on that field. Tell them Mu Bingqing is going home.

MEI salutes, tears in her eyes. The FEMALE SOLDIERS stand at attention as MU BINGQING walks through their ranks, each gaze a farewell.

INT. NORTHERN FORTRESS - NIGHT

CHU XIAO sits on a war chest, cleaning his blade. The firelight makes him look older than his thirty years. Around him, his veterans stand guard—more family than soldiers.

A grizzled SERGEANT steps forward.

SERGEANT
The capital's already sending couriers. They want a triumph. They want their hero.

CHU XIAO
The only triumph is that we're still breathing.

He slides the sword into its scabbard. The leather is worn smooth—not from battle, but from thumbprints at morning and dusk, when he would take out a hidden thing to look at it.

The SERGEANT follows his gaze.

SERGEANT
She still waiting?

CHU XIAO
She's never waited. She's been fighting her own war.

He pulls out the red marriage certificate. It's creased, the characters fading. He studies it like a map to a place he thought he'd never reach.

CHU XIAO
But the war's over now. For both of us.

He stands and offers the sword to the SERGEANT. Not as a gift—as a burden being set down.

CHU XIAO
Tell them Lin Chu Xiao is dead. All that remains is a man with a promise to keep.

The SERGEANT takes it, understanding. The other SOLDIERS bow—not to a god, but to a brother.

EXT. SOUTHERN DOMAIN MILITARY CAMP - DAY

MU BINGQING stands at the camp's edge. Her simple travel pack is light. She looks back once at the banners, the armor, the life.

Her hand rests on her chest, where the certificate lies against her skin.

A young SOLDIER approaches, offers her a lotus bun wrapped in cloth.

YOUNG SOLDIER
For the journey, my lady.

MU BINGQING
I'm not your lady anymore.

She takes it anyway. Smiles. For the first time in years, it reaches her eyes.

MU BINGQING
Just Mu Bingqing.

She walks toward the rising sun. The certificate flutters in her hand as she adjusts her pack. The names are visible: Lin Chu Xiao, Mu Bingqing.

Close-up on the certificate, but now we see something new—a date. Ten years ago, the day they married in secret before the wars began.

The parchment trembles—not from wind. From the hand holding it, finally steady.

FADE OUT.

title: Universe Cave Heaven
description: An immortal cultivator confronts the embodiment of the Heavenly Dao to break through the barriers of time and return to the mortal realm.
context:
Title: Universe Cave Heaven
Description: An immortal cultivator confronts the embodiment of the Heavenly Dao to break through the barriers of time and return to the mortal realm.

INT. IMMORTAL REALM - DAY

The Immortal Realm, shrouded in clouds and mist. Giant floating stone islands hover in the air, waterfalls cascading from their edges, frozen mid-flow.

CHEN YULONG, magnificent armor bearing a charred scar across the chestplate, sits cross-legged on a floating rock. A golden light formation flickers around him, unstable.

He opens his eyes. The light fades.

CHEN YULONG
(to himself)
Three hundred years, and still the mortal heart beats beneath immortal skin.

He touches his chest. A faint blue lotus tattoo pulses, then disappears.

EXT. ENTRANCE TO THE RIVER OF TIME - DAY

Chen Yulong stands at the edge of a cliff. Below, time flows as liquid silver.

The sky changes color. A massive blue vortex appears.

CHEN YULONG
(alert)
So you knew I was coming.

The HEAVENLY DAO EMBODIMENT emerges from the vortex, an old man in white robes woven with constellations.

HEAVENLY DAO EMBODIMENT
I am the guardian of all cause and effect. You achieved Immortal Venerable to escape grief. Do not throw away eternity for a ghost.

CHEN YULONG
She is not a ghost to me.

HEAVENLY DAO EMBODIMENT
Return to your cultivation. The mortal world has forgotten you.

CHEN YULONG
Then I will remind them.

HEAVENLY DAO EMBODIMENT
I have witnessed ten thousand ascensions. All who return become less than memory.

Chen Yulong draws his sword. The blade bears a single notch near the hilt.

CHEN YULONG
This notch remembers. That is enough.

HEAVENLY DAO EMBODIMENT
Infinite Universe, Ten Directions Earth Treasury!

The sky fractures. Crystal pillars of causality descend.

CHEN YULONG
Azure Lotus Sword Art.
continuation:
His sword meets the first pillar. The impact ripples through his arm.

A lightning bolt tears through his shoulder, leaving a smoking wound.

CHEN YULONG
(strained)
Sword Wind.

He deflects the bolt, but staggers. Blood drips from his gauntlet.

HEAVENLY DAO EMBODIMENT
You bleed eternity. How much can you lose before you are no longer yourself?

CHEN YULONG
(steady)
As much as it takes.

He channels energy. The sword's notch glows blue.

CHEN YULONG
Azure Lotus Sword Art. Sword Force.

The golden net of sword qi strikes. The Heavenly Dao Embodiment flickers, briefly transparent.

HEAVENLY DAO EMBODIMENT
(surprised)
You use your own soul as blade edge?

CHEN YULONG
What else would I use?

Chen Yulong's charred scar spreads across his armor. His hair shows a streak of white.

CHEN YULONG
(shouting)
Azure Lotus Sword Art! Sword Song!

A massive golden sword shadow erupts from his blade, consuming the Heavenly Dao Embodiment. As the embodiment shatters, he laughs.

HEAVENLY DAO EMBODIMENT
You win the battle. But against time... you will lose yourself.

The vortex collapses. Chen Yulong falls to one knee, breathing heavily.

EXT. MOUNTAIN PEAK IN THE MORTAL REALM - DAY

Chen Yulong materializes, sword planted in earth to support himself. He is visibly aged, armor cracked beyond repair.

He gazes at a distant village. A blue lotus blooms in the valley below.

CHEN YULONG
(quietly)
The pond... it still holds water.

He touches the notch in his sword—now cold.

He transforms into a white light and vanishes.

title: The Prince and the Courtesan
description: A woman sold into servitude discovers her fate entwined with a compromised prince, challenging both his supposed infertility and her own survival.
context:
Title: The Prince and the Courtesan
Description: A woman sold into servitude discovers her fate entwined with a compromised prince, challenging both his supposed infertility and her own survival.

INT. BROTHEL - NIGHT

Golden lanterns cast fractured light across a smoke-filled hall. GUESTS shout over dice while DANCERS move through orchestrated desire.

MADAM
(through a painted smile)
Wine for all! Enjoy the pleasures of the night!

The MADAM shoves a YOUNG GIRL toward the main floor.

MADAM
(coldly)
Earn your keep or join the street rats.

The MADAM ascends the stairs, her smile evaporating into calculation.

INT. BROTHEL ROOM - CONTINUOUS

STEWARD HE drags SONG SHIWEI by her bound wrists.

SONG SHIWEI
(struggling, fierce)
I was contracted as a maid! Not this—

STEWARD HE hurls her against the bedpost.

SONG SHIWEI
(breathless)
My father died in your master's service. Have you no honor?

STEWARD HE
Honor? The debt he left buys you tonight.

SONG SHIWEI
I will not—

She bites his forearm, deep. He backhands her across the face.

STEWARD HE
(hissing, clutching his bleeding arm)
Next time, I'll sell you to the docks. Master Wu arrives within the hour.

He locks the door. Her lip bleeds. She spits crimson onto the floor.

SONG SHIWEI
(to herself, trembling but resolute)
Not while I draw breath.
continuation:
INT. BROTHEL ROOM - LATER

The lock clicks. PEI JINGYUAN stumbles through, his pupils blown, sweat soaking his royal silks. He leans on the doorframe, fighting invisible flames.

PEI JINGYUAN
(groggy, desperate)
Which room...?

SONG SHIWEI bolts for the hidden servant's entrance. He catches her arm—not roughly, but with a drugged man's clumsy strength.

PEI JINGYUAN
(squeezing his eyes shut)
Please. They're watching. If I don't... they'll know I'm not compromised.

She sees the corridor shadow—ZHOU QING's silhouette. Understanding cuts through her terror.

SONG SHIWEI
(whispered, calculating)
Sixteen. My first night.

PEI JINGYUAN
(pleading)
I won't remember this. You won't suffer for it. The physicians say I'm barren.

He presses something into her palm—a jade token, still warm.

PEI JINGYUAN
Take this. For your silence. For whatever comes after.

Their eyes meet. Two cornered animals. She makes a choice, subtle and terrible, and nods once.

He pushes her toward the bed. The candle gutters. Rose petals drift from a rafter, already dying.

INT. BROTHEL ROOM - MORNING

SONG SHIWEI wakes alone, the token clutched in her fist. PEI JINGYUAN stands at the window, his back to her, shoulders rigid with shame.

PEI JINGYUAN
(without turning)
I remember enough to hate myself. The token bears my seal. Use it if you must. Or bury it. I deserve no agency in your choice.

He leaves. She examines the token: the character PEI, carved into blood-jade.

SONG SHIWEI (V.O.)
The Prince Regent. The one they call the Shadow King.

INT. BROTHEL CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS

ZHOU QING blocks her exit, his hand on his sword hilt.

ZHOU QING
(formal, lethal)
His Highness was never here. You were never this. For your sake, for ours—let it die.

He melts into the morning crowd. She stands very still, then tucks the token into her breast.

SONG SHIWEI (V.O.)
Let it die. Let me live.

INT. MEDICAL CLINIC - DAY

ON SCREEN: ONE MONTH LATER

The PHYSICIAN withdraws his fingers from her wrist, his face a careful blank.

PHYSICIAN
(quietly)
The pulse is strong. Too strong. How many times did he—

SONG SHIWEI
Once. Enough.

PHYSICIAN
Then fortune mocks medicine. You carry not one child, but two. And they will be... noticeable. Soon.

SONG SHIWEI stares at her hands—calloused from scrubbing floors, now holding twin destinies.

SONG SHIWEI
(to herself)
Barren. They said barren.

The physician begins mixing herbs. She doesn't stop him.

title: The Richest Man Became My Suitor After Divorce
description: After her grandfather's death, Tang Yao enters a marriage of convenience with the cold CEO Ji Hancheng to fulfill a promise between their families.
context:
Title: The Richest Man Became My Suitor After Divorce
Description: After her grandfather's death, Tang Yao enters a marriage of convenience with the cold CEO Ji Hancheng to fulfill a promise between their families.

EXT. COMPANY BUILDING - DAY

A black luxury car with license plate A99999 slowly approaches. At the same time, a young woman in a yellow modified cheongsam, TANG YAO, arrives on a beige electric scooter. The luxury car stops at the company entrance, and TANG YAO parks her scooter nearby.

A group of BODYGUARDS in black suits rush out of another car and line up beside the luxury car. TANG YAO parks, removes her helmet, revealing her delicate features.

ON SCREEN: TANG YAO - DISGRACED HEIRESS

The BODYGUARDS open the car door. An elderly man in white traditional Chinese clothing, holding a cane, JI YEYE, exits the car surrounded by bodyguards.

ON SCREEN: JI SHOUCUN - JI FAMILY PATRIARCH

JI YEYE
Miss Tang.

TANG YAO
Grandpa Ji. Thank you for meeting me.

JI YEYE
Old Tang saved my life in '98. I promised him I'd protect his girl. This is my grandson, Ji Hancheng.

The BODYGUARDS step aside, revealing a young man in a blue striped suit with a stern expression, JI HANCHENG, who walks forward.

ON SCREEN: JI HANCHENG - ACTING CEO, JI GROUP

TANG YAO extends her hand.

TANG YAO
Mr. Ji.

JI HANCHENG glances at her hand, then walks past without accepting it. TANG YAO smoothly lowers her hand, typing a quick text.

TANG YAO (V.O.)
At least he looked at my hand. For a second.

JI HANCHENG (V.O.)
Grandpa skipped his medication this morning. This is blackmail.

JI YEYE
(smiles)

TANG YAO
Let's not keep the office waiting.

JI HANCHENG (V.O.)
She's already planning her exit strategy.

JI YEYE
Go on.

JI HANCHENG walks toward the marriage registration office. TANG YAO follows, sending her text. JI YEYE and his men watch with satisfied expressions.

JI YEYE
She has his eyes. Steady.

BODYGUARD
Sir?

JI YEYE
Nothing. Just an old man's fancy.
continuation:
EXT. MARRIAGE REGISTRATION OFFICE - DAY

TANG YAO and JI HANCHENG exit, both expressionless, holding red marriage certificates.

TANG YAO (V.O.)
Two signatures. Twenty minutes. One lifetime.

They approach JI YEYE.

TANG YAO
Grandpa Ji, I have hospital rounds. I'll call tomorrow.

JI YEYE
Take care, child.

TANG YAO turns and walks to her scooter, securing the certificate in her medical bag. JI YEYE and JI HANCHENG watch her merge into traffic.

JI HANCHENG (V.O.)
She rides like she's escaping a sinking ship.

JI YEYE
Bring her home tonight. No arguments.

JI HANCHENG
I understand.

JI YEYE (V.O.)
She's drowning, boy. Don't just watch.

JI HANCHENG (V.O.)
If this marriage secures Grandpa's treatment and my board vote... fine. For now.

INT. MEMORIAL HALL - DAY

ON SCREEN: THREE DAYS LATER - TANG FAMILY MEMORIAL

TANG YAO, dressed in black, kneels before her grandfather's portrait.

TANG YAO'S GRANDFATHER (V.O.)
Yaoyao, happiness is a choice. Not a debt.

A man in a worn suit, TANG XIN, stumbles in, flanked by two COLLECTORS.

ON SCREEN: TANG XIN - YAOYAO'S FATHER

TANG XIN
Yaoyao—please. They followed me here.

DEBT COLLECTOR
Two hundred thousand. Today.

TANG XIN
(crawling toward TANG YAO)
Your grandfather's insurance! It's family money!

TANG XIN lunges for TANG YAO's bag. She sidesteps, letting him crash. The marriage certificate slides out.

TANG YAO
(quietly)
I liquidated it. For Grandpa's surgical bills. Every yuan.

TANG XIN stares at the certificate, then at her.

TANG XIN
You married him? The Ji family CEO?

The DEBT COLLECTOR picks up the certificate, photographing it.

DEBT COLLECTOR
Mrs. Ji. We'll be in touch about your father's obligations.

TANG YAO watches the collector dial his phone, her knuckles white.

FADE OUT.

title: After Divorce, CEO Lu Becomes a Simp and Helps Me with My Shoes
description: A woman diagnosed with terminal liver cancer discovers her husband's mistress is pregnant and decides to end her three-year marriage to live for herself.
context:
Title: After Divorce, CEO Lu Becomes a Simp and Helps Me with My Shoes
Description: A woman diagnosed with terminal liver cancer discovers her husband's mistress is pregnant and decides to end her three-year marriage to live for herself.

INT. HOSPITAL - DOCTOR'S OFFICE - DAY

GU XINGMAN sits across from the DOCTOR.

DOCTOR
Miss Gu, you have late-stage liver cancer. I'm afraid you only have a year left.

GU XINGMAN's breath catches. She reaches for the report, fingers trembling. Stares at the words until they blur. A tear lands on the paper. She doesn't wipe it away.

INT. HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR - DAY

GU XINGMAN moves through the hospital corridor, the report clutched to her chest. The world blurs around her.

She stops by a window, fishing out her phone. The lock screen shows a wedding photo: her and a man, smiling.

A notification appears: BAI JINROU. A message.

She opens it. An ultrasound. Then a photo of her husband's watch—on a nightstand she recognizes—next to a pregnancy test. Two lines. A third photo: the same watch, on Bai Jinrou's wrist in a mirror selfie. No words. None needed.

Gu's hand goes slack. The diagnosis report slips, pages scattering. She stares at the images, then at her reflection in the window. A woman crumbling from the inside out.
continuation:
EXT. LU FAMILY VILLA - DAY

An aerial shot of the manicured estate. Text on screen: LU ESTATE, 3 YEARS LATER.

INT. LU FAMILY - LIVING ROOM - DAY

Fresh lilies overflow from crystal vases. The HOUSEKEEPER adjusts their stems.

GU XINGMAN enters, her keys clicking on marble. She inhales—and a ragged cough tears through her. She covers her mouth, body convulsing.

HOUSEKEEPER
Madam? The master requested these. Said the roses were... predictable.

Gu straightens, wiping her watering eyes. She approaches the vase, running a finger along a petal. Her throat itches. Her skin flushes where pollen touched.

Without a word, she plucks a lily. Crushes it in her fist. The petals bruise, then tear. She opens her hand. The mangled pieces fall like confetti.

She climbs the stairs without looking back.

INT. LU CORPORATION - CONFERENCE ROOM - DAY

LU SIYE sits at the head of the table, checking his watch. ASSISTANT HE hovers nearby.

ASSISTANT HE
The quarterly review begins in two minutes. Secretary Gu isn't responding.

FEMALE COLLEAGUE
She's never missed a board meeting. Not once.

LU SIYE dials. Voicemail. His knuckles whiten.

LU SIYE
(to Assistant)
Find her.

ASSISTANT HE
Yes, sir.

The door swings open.

GU XINGMAN (O.S.)
Looking for me?

GU XINGMAN stands in the doorway, red silk dress cutting through the beige corporate landscape. Sunglasses hide her eyes. She holds a manila envelope—not her usual leather portfolio.

She walks to the head of the table, each step deliberate, and sets the envelope before Lu SiYe.

The room holds its breath.

title: Crossing Ice to Embrace You
description: A mysterious warrior living in hiding works as a pool cleaner, but his past catches up with him when an elite assassin is sent to kill him.
context:
Title: Crossing Ice to Embrace You
Description: A mysterious warrior living in hiding works as a pool cleaner, but his past catches up with him when an elite assassin is sent to kill him.

INT. INDOOR POOL - NIGHT

LING TIAN, late 20s, wears a white tank top and shorts with a dark blindfold—a self-imposed handicap. He crouches by the pool, methodically wiping tiles with a towel despite not seeing them. His movements are precise, economical. A faded scar traces his collarbone.

CHENG CHENG, elegant in an orange patterned bikini with a matching cover-up, emerges from the pool and sits at the edge. Water droplets fall from her wet hair. She watches his hands.

CHENG CHENG
Come here and give me a massage.

LING TIAN continues wiping the floor, his head tilted to track her by sound.

LING TIAN
Sorry, I only know how to scrub backs, not massage.

CHENG CHENG takes out a matte black card from her cover-up and places it on the pool edge. No numbers. Just a single embossed character: Han.

CHENG CHENG
This card is worth a year of your wages here. Walk away. Tonight.

LING TIAN stops his work, stands up, and walks behind CHENG CHENG. He begins massaging her shoulders. His fingers find pressure points with surgical precision.

CHENG CHENG
(enjoying it)
Harder.

LING TIAN
That wasn't the deal.

CHENG CHENG
Not bad technique. Why don't you stay with me tonight?

CHENG CHENG reaches back to touch LING TIAN's face. He grabs her wrist—firm but not cruel.

LING TIAN
I sold my sword. Not my soul.

LING TIAN releases her hand. CHENG CHENG smiles, stands up from the pool, and tucks the black card into the collar of LING TIAN's tank top.

CHENG CHENG
Is that so? Come find me tomorrow.

CHENG CHENG smiles and turns to leave.
continuation:
EXT. BUILDING EXTERIOR - NIGHT

A black Maybach sedan drives through the night and stops at the building entrance.

INT. MAYBACH CAR - NIGHT

A MYSTERIOUS MAN sits in the back seat, watching surveillance footage on a phone—Ling Tian's downcast face, his scar circled.

MYSTERIOUS MAN (V.O.)
Three years ago, the War Ancestor of the Great Han vanished. I lost hundreds tracing him to this hole. If he lives, the Han cannot fall. You have one chance.

YI XUAN, mid-20s, gets out of the car. She wears a black swimsuit beneath a white robe.

YI XUAN
Don't worry. I never miss.

YI XUAN walks confidently toward the building.

INT. INDOOR POOL - NIGHT

LING TIAN crouches down to continue wiping the floor. He slips the black card into his pocket. The door opens.

LING TIAN
(without looking up)
Sorry, we're closed.

YI XUAN walks over, wearing a black swimsuit with a white robe.

YI XUAN
That's okay, I'll be quick.

YI XUAN removes her robe and throws it on the ground. It lands with a soft thud—weighted.

YI XUAN
Master, my chest hurts. Can you massage it for me?

YI XUAN approaches LING TIAN from behind, wrapping her arms around him. A very thin metal wire appears in her hands, ready to strangle his neck.

LING TIAN
(coldly laughing)
Silver silk golden wire. Third on the assassin rankings. I expected better.

LING TIAN breaks free by simply standing—his movement cracks the tile beneath his feet. He twists, and YI XUAN is airborne, splashing into the pool.

LING TIAN
(coldly)
You dirtied my floor. Get out now. If you ever step through that door again, I'll kill you.

YI XUAN looks at LING TIAN in shock from the water. LING TIAN turns around, wipes the blood from the corner of his mouth. His eyes are ice cold—and utterly exhausted.

title: The Transmigrated Princess's Counterattack Strategy
description: A modern forensic pathologist is transported into the body of a betrayed princess consort who lost everything to a scheming concubine and her cruel husband.
context:
Title: The Transmigrated Princess's Counterattack Strategy
Description: A modern forensic pathologist is transported into the body of a betrayed princess consort who lost everything to a scheming concubine and her cruel husband.

INT. PRINCE'S MANOR BEDROOM - DAY

LING SIWAN sits on the bed, leisurely stirring a bowl of medicinal tonic with a silver spoon.

THE PRINCESS CONSORT kneels on the ground, clothes disheveled, blood at the corner of her mouth, her expression a blend of grief and banked fury.

PRINCESS CONSORT
(coldly)
If it's revenge you want, take it from me. But my child—he's innocent. Give him back.

LING SIWAN
(sinisterly sweet)
Sister, don't be dramatic. You caused my fall—accidents happen. Though I must admit, your child's... gift... has done wonders for my recovery.

The PRINCESS CONSORT's eyes widen with comprehension. She lunges at LING SIWAN, grabbing her throat with trembling hands.

PRINCESS CONSORT
(hoarsely)
You monster.

The PRINCE rushes in and shoves the PRINCESS CONSORT away. She collapses.

PRINCE
(furious, weary)
Still at it? Even now?

PRINCESS CONSORT
(coughing blood, laughing bitterly)
She used our child for her medicine, and you ask if I'm still at it?

MAID
(rushing to support her)
Your Highness, the physician said she mustn't be distressed—her vitals are already—

PRINCE
I don't care! She had an affair and birthed that bastard. She deserves every ounce of this.

PRINCESS CONSORT
(quietly, dangerously)
It was that night. The fire. You were delirious. I carried you out.

LING SIWAN
(feigning shock)
Sister... the stable boy with the lazy eye? Even for a lonely noblewoman, that's desperation. The whole manor knows. Just admit it.

PRINCE
(explosive, embarrassed)
Stop lying! I defended you to the court, to my father. And this is how you repay me?

The PRINCE turns to leave. LING SIWAN walks past, her heel "accidentally" grinding into the PRINCESS CONSORT's hand.

The PRINCESS CONSORT lies motionless, blood pooling beneath her.

PRINCESS CONSORT (V.O.)
Chu Chen'an, when you learn it was my blood that saved you, my body that warmed you that night—regret won't be a sharp enough word.
continuation:
EXT. ANTIQUE ARCHITECTURE PLAZA - DAY

A SCIENTIST sits on a rideable suitcase, watching a short drama, shaking her head.

SCIENTIST
Five episodes of "Chasing Wife to Crematorium" and she hasn't even planted a single piece of forged evidence. Amateur hour.

She accelerates the suitcase, swerving to avoid a CHILD crossing her path while shouting.

SCIENTIST
Watch it! Garbage sorting is a civic duty!

The suitcase clips a stone pillar. The SCIENTIST is launched into the air, strikes her head, and crashes down, unconscious.

PASSERSBY gather.

PASSERBY A
Someone call emergency services!

PASSERBY B
Is she breathing?

The scene flashes between the SCIENTIST's bleeding temple and the PRINCESS CONSORT's lifeless form.

INT. PRINCE'S MANOR BEDROOM - DAY

THE PRINCESS CONSORT lies on the bed, eyes snapping open—cold, clinical, assessing her surroundings like a crime scene.

ON SCREEN: TO BE CONTINUED

title: Ultimate Teacher
description: A man named Su Ming accepts a challenge to reform the worst school in the province within a month to win a marriage contract with a beautiful CEO, aided by a mysterious system.
context:
Title: Ultimate Teacher
Description: A man named Su Ming accepts a challenge to reform the worst school in the province within a month to win a marriage contract with a beautiful CEO, aided by a mysterious system.

EXT. ROOFTOP - DAY

On a rainy day, a man in a leather jacket, SU MING, stands on the rooftop holding a black umbrella.

SU MING (V.O.)
Longhua Academy. Not a school anymore—just a warehouse for problems the system gave up on. Ranked dead last province-wide.

FLASHBACK:

Students in uniform loiter in chaotic clusters. One group huddles around a phone, another shoves a classmate against a locker.

BACK TO PRESENT:

On the exterior wall behind SU MING, a weathered poster reads: "REFORM ACADEMY, WIN CONTRACT" beside a photo of CEO QIAO XUE.

SU MING (V.O.)
If I can pull them back from the brink in thirty days... I win a marriage contract with CEO Qiao Xue. The terms are clear.

SU MING's knuckles tighten on the umbrella handle, rainwater streaming from the spokes.

SU MING (V.O.)
They were my classmates once. I know exactly how they think.

A STATIC HISS fills the air. Geometric equations materialize, glowing faint blue around SU MING's silhouette.

SYSTEM (V.O.)
Umbrella Protocol activated. Experience-based behavioral correction will trigger reward distribution.

SU MING flinches, fingers brushing his earpiece.

SU MING
System?

SU MING (to himself)
Two years in the wilderness. About time the universe cashed its debt.

SU MING's jaw sets. He snaps the umbrella shut.

SU MING
Let's see what dead last really looks like.

He strides past the poster. His fingers tear it from the wet brick as he passes.

SYSTEM (V.O.)
Commencing operation.
continuation:
INT. PRINCIPAL'S OFFICE - DAY

Modern campus buildings visible through floor-to-ceiling windows.

SU MING waits by the window, arms crossed. His dry shirt suggests he's changed since the rooftop.

Sharp FOOTSTEPS. QIAO XUE (late 20s, sharp suit, glasses) enters, tablet in hand.

Her presence catches SU MING's attention. He subtly straightens his posture.

SU MING (quietly)
Impressive.

QIAO XUE settles behind the desk, scrolling through the tablet.

QIAO XUE
The certificate photo didn't do you justice, Mr. Su. You're the ninety-ninth candidate this year. The others broke contracts, not students.

SU MING
I don't break easily. Though I'm curious—is the contract clause still binding?

QIAO XUE
One month. Measurable improvement in behavior metrics. Today's monthly exam is your baseline assessment. Ready to observe?

SU MING
I'll need a uniform.

QIAO XUE (puzzled)
You're administrative staff.

SU MING (leaning forward, precisely)
Tigers don't change their stripes from the observation deck.

title: I Became a Wealthy Heir on My Wedding Day
description: On his wedding day, Chu He discovers his bride in bed with another man, only to learn he is the long-lost heir to a powerful conglomerate.
context:
Title: I Became a Wealthy Heir on My Wedding Day
Description: On his wedding day, Chu He discovers his bride in bed with another man, only to learn he is the long-lost heir to a powerful conglomerate.

INT. TEMPLE - DAY

CHU GE, mid-40s in a simple dark suit, kneels in a temple alcove. Two BODYGUARDS wait respectfully at a distance. He holds worn wooden divination blocks.

CHU GE clasps his hands together, eyes closed.

CHU GE
Please, gods and Buddha, help me find my brother. Before it's too late.

CHU GE tosses the blocks. They clatter on the stone floor - both land face up.

SECRETARY LIN, professional in a black dress, rushes up the temple steps, phone in hand.

SECRETARY LIN
President Chu. The investigator just sent confirmation.

CHU GE
(standing slowly)
And?

SECRETARY LIN
Your brother is in Hidden Dragon Village. Getting married today.

CHU GE
(surprised, then pained)
Married? Without me there to see it?

SECRETARY LIN
The car is ready. We can make the reception if we leave now.

CHU GE
No. We'll make the ceremony. And Secretary Lin - cancel my meetings. Find me a gift. Something worthy of a Chutian heir.

SECRETARY LIN
Yes, sir.

Chu Ge looks at a faded photo on his phone - two boys, one helping the other with a basketball, a tender smile on his face.
continuation:
EXT. RESIDENTIAL BUILDING - DAY

CHU HE, late 20s, adjusts his slightly loose suit. He holds a modest bouquet of carnations. A PHOTOGRAPHER gestures from the street below.

PHOTOGRAPHER
The groom looks great! One more smile! Look up!

Chu He forces a smile, but his eyes scan the building's windows. The BEST MAN, his cousin, approaches with a phone.

BEST MAN
Your father's calling. Third time.

CHU HE takes the phone.

CHU HE
(answering)
Dad. I told you, it's handled.

CHU HE'S FATHER (V.O.)
Xiao He, listen. Ruyan's father just called. She's been asking about annulment procedures. Last week.

CHU HE
(frozen, hand tightening on the bouquet)
What?

CHU HE'S FATHER (V.O.)
She's carrying your child. Whatever happens today, just... be patient with her.

CHU HE
(quietly)
I'll handle it.

He ends the call, his knuckles white on the bouquet stems.

INT. LIU RUYAN'S BEDROOM - DAY

LIU RUYAN, 26, sits on the edge of the wedding bed in her dress, head in her hands. JIANG KUN, 30s and predatory, leans against the wall by the window.

JIANG KUN
Your phone's been buzzing since breakfast. He knows, doesn't he?

LIU RUYAN
(quietly, not looking up)
His father suspects. I told you this was insane, doing this here—

JIANG KUN
The best place to hide is in plain sight. And he's downstairs taking photos.

JIANG KUN crosses to her, stands too close.

JIANG KUN
One month. You marry him, get access to his family's accounts, then file for divorce. The paternity test stays our secret. Everyone wins.

LIU RUYAN
Except Chu He.

JIANG KUN
(smiling)
Small price for a fortune.

INT. HALLWAY - DAY

CHU HE walks upstairs, bouquet in hand, his face set, jaw tight. The BEST MAN follows, checking his phone.

They reach the door of Room 402. It's ajar. A man's low laugh floats out from inside.

INT. LIU RUYAN'S BEDROOM - DAY

JIANG KUN is close to LIU RUYAN, his hand on her bare shoulder strap, whispering in her ear. Her face is turned away, eyes closed.

The door opens. CHU HE stands in the doorway, frozen.

CHU HE sees them. The bouquet slides from his hands. Carnations scatter on the floor.

JIANG KUN looks up slowly, not startled. Pleased.

LIU RUYAN turns. Her eyes meet Chu's. For a moment, just guilt.

No one speaks.

title: Dark Forest Encounter
description: A young woman saves a poisoned heir in a dark forest, leading to a fateful meeting that intertwines their destinies as she becomes entangled in a murder conspiracy.
context:
Title: Dark Forest Encounter
Description: A young woman saves a poisoned heir in a dark forest, leading to a fateful meeting that intertwines their destinies as she becomes entangled in a murder conspiracy.

INT. DARK FOREST - NIGHT

A rainy night, lightning and thunder flash through the trees. Rain pours through the canopy in sheets.

LING YING, wearing simple clothes and a bamboo hat, runs frantically through the muddy path, constantly looking back at pursuing flashlights.

She slips and falls toward a ravine—

A hand shoots out. QIN YANSHI, white shirt and black leather jacket soaked through, face pale and weak, catches her wrist and pulls her to safety.

In the distance, flashlight beams and pursuing voices can be heard.

PURSUER (V.O.)
There's movement over there, spread out! Catch him!

LING YING
(urgent whisper)
Follow me.

LING YING pulls QIN YANSHI to hide behind a rocky overhang. The pursuers pass by with flashlights and clubs.

QIN YANSHI trembles, poison making his muscles spasm. He notices the purple veins visible on his hands.

PURSUER LEADER (V.O.)
Remember: Qin Yanshi's been injected. He can't have gone far. Check every crevice.

The pursuers move past. QIN YANSHI releases his grip on the rock, his body swaying. He focuses on LING YING's silver ring with a distinctive lotus design.

QIN YANSHI
(strained)
That ring... proof you helped me...

He twists it—the ring separates into two halves. He pockets one half.

LING YING
What are you—

QIN YANSHI
Promise I'll find you...

QIN YANSHI collapses against the rock, unconscious. LING YING looks at him, then at the half-ring, conflicted.

FADE TO:

EXT. HILLSIDE - DAY

Golden sunrise. QIN YANSHI has changed into a clean white shirt. A DOCTOR is taking a blood sample. SECRETARY XU stands nearby.

SECRETARY XU
Young Master, we've searched the entire mountain. No trace of the woman. Do you recall any identifying features?

QIN YANSHI
(recalling)
Only that ring. Silver. Splits in two. Find it, and you find her.

He holds up the half-ring, letting light catch the lotus etching.

QIN YANSHI
She saved my life. Use every resource.
continuation:
INT. JIANG FAMILY LIVING ROOM - DAY

JIANG MOTHER and JIANG FATHER sit on the sofa. Their daughter LING YAN anxiously paces.

JIANG MOTHER
The Qin matriarch is dead. They found the car—a rental under our family name. If they trace it—

LING YAN
(voice breaking)
I didn't mean to—to hit her that hard. She recognized me, she was going to expose the deal—

JIANG FATHER
(interrupting)
Enough. The rental agreement uses Ling Ying's identity. It's clean.

At the doorway, LING YING, now wearing a white dress, walks in and overhears. Shock and betrayal cross her face.

LING YING
You sent me away because you couldn't afford three children. I worked three jobs to get into Beijing University, thinking we could finally—

JIANG MOTHER
(desperate)
Ying'er, there's been a terrible accident. Your sister—she was driving. A woman was hit. But the car... it was rented using your ID months ago.

LING YING
My ID? You took my documents when I left—

LING YAN
(approaching)
It was an accident. Please. I'll spend my life making it up to you.

LING YAN notices the silver lotus ring on LING YING's hand.

LING YAN
That ring...

She grabs Ling Ying's hand, examining it.

LING YAN
Let me hold onto it for now. For safekeeping.

LING YING
No! It's from Grandma—

LING YAN
The grandmother who gave you away? I'll keep it safe.

Ling Yan pulls the ring off, trying it on. It fits. Ling Ying lunges, but JIANG FATHER restrains her.

EXT. JIANG FAMILY ENTRANCE - DAY

LING YING is escorted out, hands bound with zip ties. A black sedan pulls up.

SECRETARY XU and QIN YANSHI exit the car. JIANG MOTHER approaches.

JIANG MOTHER
May I help you?

SECRETARY XU
I represent Qin Corporation security. We're investigating the matriarch's death.

LING YAN
(internal monologue)
Qin Corporation? They found the car already?

LING YAN immediately points at the restrained LING YING.

LING YAN
She's the one you want! She confessed to everything!

LING YING sees QIN YANSHI. Recognition and hope flare in her eyes.

LING YING
(internal monologue)
It's him. If he remembers the forest, he knows I wasn't driving. I was saving him.

QIN YANSHI's gaze falls on LING YING, then on the ring gleaming on LING YAN's finger.

QIN YANSHI
(internal monologue)
The ring... but the woman in the forest was different. I felt her urgency, her kindness.

He pulls out his half. The lotus patterns match.

QIN YANSHI
(to Ling Yan)
Miss, where did you acquire that ring?

LING YAN
(nervously)
Family heirloom. Passed down.

QIN YANSHI
(to Secretary Xu)
Run facial analysis on last night's thermal footage. Cross-reference with these women.

He looks between the sisters, suspicion growing.

QIN YANSHI
One of you was in that forest last night. The truth will show which.

ON SCREEN: TO BE CONTINUED

title: Sin and Sugar
description: A desperate young woman encounters a mysterious injured man while trying to save her sick mother, leading to a fateful night that changes everything.
context:
Title: Sin and Sugar
Description: A desperate young woman encounters a mysterious injured man while trying to save her sick mother, leading to a fateful night that changes everything.

INT. CAMPING CANOPY - NIGHT

GU NIAN, LIU RUQING, and two other classmates (one male, one female) sit around a table.

GU NIAN
Excuse me, I need to take a call.

GU NIAN gets up and leaves.

FEMALE CLASSMATE
Isn't she the eldest daughter of the Gu family? Why is she borrowing money from us at a camping party?

LIU RUQING
(pouring tea)
Her mother is dying. Some debts you can't outrun.

EXT. WISHING TREE - NIGHT

GU NIAN stands under a tree covered with red ribbons, anxiously making a phone call.

GU NIAN
(into phone, voice choking)
Dad, I'm only short a hundred thousand yuan. Mom's illness can't be delayed any longer.

FATHER (V.O.)
Then stop prolonging it. Your sister got into a car accident and hit someone.

GU NIAN
(agitated)
She's not my sister, she's your illegitimate daughter!

The call is disconnected. Busy tone. GU NIAN stares at her phone in despair, tears streaming down her face.

GU NIAN
(looking up, praying)
God, why don't you punish the bad people instead of tormenting my mother? Who can help me?

A man (LU CHENZHOU) suddenly runs past her from behind, knocking GU NIAN down. They both fall on the grass.

GU NIAN
Ah!

GU NIAN helps LU CHENZHOU sit up.

GU NIAN
Sir, are you alright?

LU CHENZHOU
(weakly)
Help me. No hospitals.

In the distance, two PURSUERS in suits are searching.

PURSUER A
Split up. You go that way.

LU CHENZHOU
(grabbing GU NIAN's arm)
Help me, help me.

GU NIAN
Come!

GU NIAN helps LU CHENZHOU up, and they stumble into a nearby tent.
continuation:
EXT. TENT - NIGHT

The two PURSUERS approach the tent. A silhouette is projected on the tent showing GU NIAN kneeling over LU CHENZHOU, performing CPR motions—rhythmic, medical, urgent.

PURSUER A
(contemptuously)
Hmph, a camper practicing first aid. Let's go.

The PURSUERS turn and leave.

INT. TENT - NIGHT

GU NIAN
(relieved)
They're gone. It's okay now.

GU NIAN notices LU CHENZHOU's black turtleneck is torn, with blood on his collarbone.

GU NIAN
(panicked)
Sir, you're hurt!

LU CHENZHOU's eyes are unfocused. He suddenly pulls GU NIAN down as his knees buckle, collapsing behind crates.

LU CHENZHOU
(breathing heavily)
I'm sorry. I was drugged. Can't stay awake.

GU NIAN
(struggling to support him)
Then let me help.

The light in the tent flickers, implying they remain through the night—her monitoring his breathing, not relations.

EXT. TENT - NIGHT

GU NIAN runs out of the tent disheveled and falls to the ground. LIU RUQING walks over from a distance and sees this.

LIU RUQING
(puzzled, to herself)
Why is she in such a panic?

EXT. WISHING TREE - NIGHT

The PURSUERS return to the tree.

LU CHENZHOU (V.O.)
(voice weaker)
Find that woman for me. She has what I need.

EXT. ROADSIDE - NIGHT

GU NIAN sits alone by the roadside, hugging herself and shivering, wearing glasses, crying helplessly. Her phone vibrates.

She picks up the phone and sees a photo: a woman in a hospital coat standing by her mother's bedside, holding a document: "TREATMENT AUTHORIZATION: PENDING PAYMENT."

GU NIAN
(terrified)
Mom...

GU NIAN immediately makes a call.

GU NIAN
(crying into phone)
What do you want?

THREATENER (V.O.)
Come to the hospital immediately. Otherwise, your mother's treatment authorization will be revoked.

GU NIAN's face is filled with fear and despair.

title: Revenge of a Grieving Mother
description: A woman plots to destroy her husband and his family after they caused the death of her newborn son through their negligence and cruelty.
context:
Title: Revenge of a Grieving Mother
Description: A woman plots to destroy her husband and his family after they caused the death of her newborn son through their negligence and cruelty.

INT. DIMLY LIT ROOM - NIGHT

QINQIN, a woman in a black business suit, walks determinedly into a dimly lit room. BOSS XU sits behind a desk.

QINQIN (V.O.)
The loan defaults in seven days. By then, every asset we own will be worthless to him.

QINQIN sits down across from BOSS XU.

BOSS XU examines the marriage certificate and property deed, then studies QINQIN.

BOSS XU
You're destroying your own credit to ruin him.

QINQIN
(coldly precise)
He deserves to lose everything.

BOSS XU
What justifies this?

QINQIN
(quiet rage)
His family killed my son. He chose to let it happen.

EXT. PARK - DAY (FLASHBACK)

FIVE MONTHS AGO

QINQIN, eight months pregnant in a blue maternity dress, sits on a park bench speaking softly on her phone.

QINQIN
(on the phone)
Checkup went perfectly. Four weeks to go.

Nearby, SISTER-IN-LAW guides her son JIAHUI on a bicycle.

SISTER-IN-LAW
Jiahui, ease up on the pedals. Gentle turn—yes, like that!

She releases the bike. JIAHUI wobbles, loses control. The bicycle veers toward QINQIN.

JIAHUI
(panicked)
Can't stop!

QINQIN turns. The front wheel strikes her side. QINQIN falls from the bench. JIAHUI topples over.

SISTER-IN-LAW rushes forward, her face flickering between alarm and calculation.

SISTER-IN-LAW
Jiahui!

QINQIN lies curled on the ground, breathing sharply, hands cradling her belly.

QINQIN
The baby...

JIAHUI cries, a shallow scrape on his elbow. SISTER-IN-LAW kneels by him first.

SISTER-IN-LAW
Let me see—just a scratch. You'll be fine.

QINQIN's husband AYUE runs over, his phone still in hand.

AYUE
What happened—Jesus, Qinqin!

He crouches beside QINQIN.
continuation:
QINQIN
(clenched teeth)
Contractions. Something's wrong.

SISTER-IN-LAW
(over her shoulder)
Ayue, Jiahui's arm needs cleaning. The handlebar caught him.

JIAHUI
(whimpering)
Mom, it stings...

QINQIN
We need the hospital. Now.

AYUE
Right—I'll get the car, we can—

SISTER-IN-LAW
(interrupting)
Qinqin's not due for weeks. It's probably false labor from the shock.

QINQIN
(gasping)
This isn't false. I'm bleeding.

SISTER-IN-LAW
(pleading)
Ayue, your brother died when Jiahui was a baby. He's all I have left. Please—just get him checked first. Ten minutes.

AYUE looks between them.

QINQIN
Ayue. Don't leave me here.

SISTER-IN-LAW
(to QINQIN)
You're an adult. You can wait. He's a child.

(to AYUE)
She's fine. Women carry babies through worse. But Jiahui—what if it's worse than it looks?

JIAHUI
(pale)
Mom, I feel dizzy...

SISTER-IN-LAW
See? Ayue, I'm begging you.

She grips AYUE's arm, tears forming.

AYUE
(agonized)
Qinqin... it's just ten minutes. I'll be right back.

QINQIN
(pleading)
Please. I can't stand up.

AYUE
Wait here. I'll send an ambulance from the car.

SISTER-IN-LAW
She doesn't need an ambulance, she needs to breathe. Come on.

SISTER-IN-LAW pulls AYUE up. He looks back once, then guides JIAHUI away, moving faster with each step.

QINQIN watches them leave, clutching her stomach. A dark stain spreads on her dress. Her breathing shallows.

QINQIN
(voice breaking)
It hurts...

She's alone. The phone lies broken beside her.

title: After My Husband Cheated, I Became a Matchmaker
description: A woman discovers her husband and best friend's betrayal while battling terminal illness on a hospital rooftop.
context:
Title: After My Husband Cheated, I Became a Matchmaker
Description: A woman discovers her husband and best friend's betrayal while battling terminal illness on a hospital rooftop.

INT. HOSPITAL ROOFTOP - NIGHT

XUE NING supports SU LI, who is wearing a patient gown and hat, as they walk on the rooftop.

SU LI
Xue Ning, thank you for these nights. I know Yan Li's been working late. You shouldn't have to--

XUE NING
(smiling gently)
What are best friends for? Yan Li wanted tonight to be perfect. He'll be here soon.

XUE NING and SU LI stop at a bench. XUE NING holds SU LI's hand.

XUE NING
We prepared something. Wait by the trellis? He wants to see your face when you see it.

XUE NING leads SU LI to a corner with a decorative trellis, partially hidden by hanging vines. XUE NING makes a "shh" gesture, then turns and leaves.

SU LI leans against the wall, her face showing an expectant smile.

XUE NING walks to the other side. JI YAN LI approaches, and they embrace.

SU LI overhears from her spot. The smile fades.

JI YAN LI
(quietly, to XUE NING)
The lawyer called. Papers are ready.

XUE NING
(softly)
We should wait. She's weaker tonight.

JI YAN LI
We've waited six months. Every day she holds on, we're--
continuation:
XUE NING
(lowered voice)
--I know. But she trusts us.

A beat of silence. Su Li's tears begin. She shifts, and a small decorative stone falls from the ledge.

JI YAN LI and XUE NING turn abruptly.

SU LI staggers out from behind the trellis, clutching her hat.

SU LI
(trembling, but controlled)
Six months.

JI YAN LI
(hesitating, not angry)
Li, you don't understand--

SU LI
(pointing, voice breaking)
I understand perfectly. The late nights. The "gala planning." The way you stopped touching my head when the hair fell out.

JI YAN LI steps forward, not in fury, but panic.

JI YAN LI
Stop. You're making yourself sick--

He reaches for her arm. She jerks back. He grabs harder, instinctively. The hat falls.

SU LI
(different voice)
Let. Go.

She yanks free. Her legs buckle from chemo-weakness, not his force. She goes down, catching herself on the trellis. The hat lies on the ground, bald head exposed.

JI YAN LI stares at his hands. XUE NING moves toward Su Li, then freezes mid-step.

SU LI is on her knees, not crying hysterically, but making a sound like she's dying all over again—quiet, breathless, final.

title: Honey, I Want to Go Public
description: A beloved actor hides his marriage to protect his wife's family from media attention, but the strain of secrecy pushes him toward a public reckoning that could destroy the careful life they've built.
context:
Title: Honey, I Want to Go Public
Description: A beloved actor hides his marriage to protect his wife's family from media attention, but the strain of secrecy pushes him toward a public reckoning that could destroy the careful life they've built.

INT. BEDROOM - MORNING

Morning light filters through sheer curtains. AN ZICHU traces patterns on ZUO LAN's shoulder as she sleeps.

AN ZICHU
Xiaoyin's casting is confirmed. They announce today.

ZUO LAN
(stirring)
We promised to wait.

AN ZICHU
I've waited three years. I've hidden. I've lied.

ZUO LAN
The press killed Mom, Zichu. I won't let that happen to her.

AN ZICHU
So she finds a husband on television while I stay invisible?

The phone RINGS. Caller ID shows "Little Sister Xiaoyin." ZUO LAN frantically grabs it.

ZUO LAN
(whispering to AN ZICHU)
Not a sound.

ZUO LAN answers the call.

ZUO LAN
(into the phone)
Hey.
continuation:
EXT. CITY STREET - CONTINUOUS

ZUO XIAOYIN wears a pristine coat, talking on the phone.

ZUO XIAOYIN
(on the phone, anxiously)
My interview's in forty minutes. You promised you'd help me prep.

INT. BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS

ZUO LAN
(frantic)
I'm leaving now. I just—

ZUO XIAOYIN
—She's all we have left, Lan. Don't forget.

The call drops. ZUO LAN stares at the phone.

She grabs a silk scarf from the nightstand, ties it around her neck—covering a mark. Her hands tremble.

ZUO LAN
(quietly)
Please. Just a little longer.

She kisses AN ZICHU's cheek, brief.

ZUO LAN
Come to the taping early.

ZUO LAN rushes out. AN ZICHU sits in the empty room. He picks up his phone, finds the show's producer contact.

His thumb hovers. Then presses call.

title: My Teacher (Episode 46)
description: A devoted rural teacher helps his three female students escape their families' attempts to stop them from attending prestigious universities abroad, sacrificing himself to ensure their departure.
context:
Title: My Teacher (Episode 46)
Description: A devoted rural teacher helps his three female students escape their families' attempts to stop them from attending prestigious universities abroad, sacrificing himself to ensure their departure.

EXT. RURAL DIRT ROAD - DAY

A pockmarked dirt road bakes under midday sun. Dust motes hang in the air. TEACHER LIN ZHIYUAN (50s, threadbare shirt, deeply weathered hands) stands with three young women: SU HONGYU (clutching a patched duffel bag), JIANG QING (gripping a worn suitcase), and LIN QINGQING (holding a canvas satchel with paint stains). Their luggage sits at their feet, dust-covered.

Lin Zhiyuan reaches into his pocket and pulls out three envelopes, his fingers trembling slightly.

LIN ZHIYUAN
Hongyu. Jiang Qing. Qingqing.

He places an envelope in each student's hand, lingering on the touch.

LIN ZHIYUAN
Keep them safe. Right next to your passports.

SU HONGYU
(quietly)
Teacher, your hands...

She notices his knuckles are raw, nails cracked.

JIANG QING
You sold the last of the rice paddies.

LIN ZHIYUAN
The soil was tired anyway.

LIN QINGQING
(whispering)
Uncle...

Lin Zhiyuan meets her eyes. A shared understanding passes between them.

LIN ZHIYUAN
Your scholarships cover tuition. This... this just covers distance.

A distant engine. They all turn. A minibus appears, kicking up red dust.

Lin Zhiyuan quickly loads their luggage into the bus hold, his movements urgent but gentle.

LIN ZHIYUAN
Go now. Before your parents finish market.

SU HONGYU
(turning back)
Teacher, they'll know you helped.

LIN ZHIYUAN
(smiling)
I'm just an old teacher. What can they do, make me teach more?

But his smile doesn't reach his eyes.

He herds them toward the open bus door. As they climb in, the sound of ANGRY SHOUTS cuts through the air.

Down the road, LIN ZHIJIANG leads a group of VILLAGERS at a dead run. LIN QINGQING's father carries a broken broom handle.

LIN ZHIJIANG
(shouting)
Lin Zhiyuan! Stop that bus!

SU HONGYU'S MOTHER sprints ahead, her market basket abandoned.

SU HONGYU'S MOTHER
continuation:
Hongyu! Get down from there!

Lin Zhiyuan SLAMS the bus door shut and bangs on the side.

LIN ZHIYUAN
Driver! Go!

The minibus lurches forward. Through the window, we see the girls' faces pressed against the glass.

Lin Zhiyuan stands in the road, arms spread wide, blocking the pursuing villagers.

LIN ZHIJIANG
Qingqing, if you leave, you're dead to this family!

LIN QINGQING
(from the bus)
Dad, please!

Lin Zhiyuan grabs Lin Zhijiang's arm, pulling him away from the moving bus. The other villagers swarm.

A fist catches Lin Zhiyuan's jaw. He staggers. The broken broom handle swings at his ribs. He goes down in the dust.

Inside the bus, the girls witness this.

JIANG QING
(screaming)
No!

SU HONGYU
Teacher!

Lin Zhiyuan curls on the ground, shielding his head, but his eyes find the bus. He mouths one word: "Go."

The bus accelerates. The villagers' figures shrink in the dust.

LIN QINGQING
(whispering)
He's still down...

INT. MINIBUS - DAY

The bus rattles over potholes. The three young women stand in the aisle, gripping seatbacks for balance. They exchange looks—guilt, terror, determination.

Lin Qingqing fumbles with her envelope. Inside, besides the ticket, there's a folded sketch: a crude drawing of four figures under a tree, childish but recognizable. Their teacher and them, years ago.

Her hands shake.

LIN QINGQING
We never asked what it cost him.

SU HONGYU
We never had to.

Jiang Qing pulls out her own ticket envelope. Inside, a small, pressed wildflower falls out. She catches it.

JIANG QING
We know.

Lin Qingqing looks at the sketch, then toward the rear window where dust still hangs in the air.

LIN QINGQING
Then we know what we have to do.

They don't kneel. They simply stand there, three young women watching their village disappear, their teacher's blood still drying on the road behind them.

FADE OUT.

title: The Emperor's Fall
description: An empress is betrayed and chased to a cliff by rebel forces. She jumps to escape and is rescued by a leisurely fisherman who pulls her from the river.
context:
Title: The Emperor's Fall
Description: An empress is betrayed and chased to a cliff by rebel forces. She jumps to escape and is rescued by a leisurely fisherman who pulls her from the river.

EXT. CLIFF EDGE - DAY

The EMPRESS (30s, imperial grace in torn silk) and two HANDMAIDENS—XIAO CUI (20s, fierce loyalty) and XIAO DIE (20s, steady resolve)—are cornered by FIVE ARMED REBELS at a mist-shrouded cliff edge. Nowhere to run.

REBEL LEADER
No more paths, Your Majesty.

REBEL #2
Never touched imperial silk before.

REBEL LEADER
Take them!

XIAO CUI and XIAO DIE draw hidden daggers, forming a protective wall. Steel clashes. XIAO CUI takes a blade to the shoulder—staggers but holds.

EMPRESS
(urgent whisper)
Stand down. That's an order.

XIAO DIE
We serve beyond death, my lady.

XIAO CUI
Live for us.

Both maidens charge. They fall within heartbeats, bodies crumpling at the cliff edge.

The EMPRESS drops to her knees, fingers trembling over XIAO DIE's still face. Grief hardens to stone.

She rises, backing to the precipice. Her hand clutches an IMPERIAL SEAL necklace.

EMPRESS
(low, lethal)
I remember every face. Every name.

She steps backward into empty air.
continuation:
EXT. RIVERSIDE - DAY

GU FEIBAI (35, scholar's hands in peasant garb) lounges by the river, incongruous sunglasses perched on his face. Two maidens—DA YA (capable) and ER YA (sharp)—stand guard.

DA YA
The council meeting begins at dusk.

GU FEIBAI
Zhang Biao can wait. I'm teaching him patience.

DA YA
You've caught nothing for hours.

GU FEIBAI
Then leaving now would be defeat.

ER YA
We could lie. Say you released them.

GU FEIBAI
Er Ya, your mouth is why you never see court.

DA YA
Master—

The fishing float VANISHES underwater—YANKED violently downstream.

DA YA
Master!

GU FEIBAI heaves back. The line strains, moving WITH the current.

ER YA
That's drifting. Not fighting.

A white-clad figure BREACHES the surface, hook snagged on her robes.

GU FEIBAI
Not a fish. A woman.

He drops the rod, sprints along the bank. The maids follow.

Together they drag the unconscious EMPRESS ashore. Her imperial seal necklace gleams in the sun.

GU FEIBAI's smile vanishes. He goes still, recognition flickering across his face.

DA YA checks her pulse.

DA YA
Alive. Barely.

GU FEIBAI
(quietly)
Of course she is.

He crouches, fingers hovering over the seal. His expression shifts—calculation, then decision.

GU FEIBAI
Da Ya, prepare the carriage. Er Ya, fetch my medicine chest. Silence.

He rolls up his sleeves, posture transforming from lazy fisherman to something deliberate and dangerous.

GU FEIBAI
(to himself)
So it begins again.

His hands move to begin resuscitation, practiced and precise.

title: A Good Match Is Never Too Late
description: A mother is given a second chance when she's reborn to the moment before her son's destructive choices tear their family apart.
context:
Title: A Good Match Is Never Too Late
Description: A mother is given a second chance when she's reborn to the moment before her son's destructive choices tear their family apart.

INT. APARTMENT - NIGHT

XIAO HUI sits on the sofa, clutching a worn bankbook.

XIAO HUI (V.O.)
Please. Let this be enough.

FLASHBACK: JIANG NAN slumped over the kitchen table, empty bottles around him.

JIANG NAN stumbles through the front door, reeking of alcohol.

JIANG NAN
(pointing at Xiao Hui)
That wedding should've been mine.

XIAO HUI sees him and quickly stands up.

XIAO HUI
I'll get you some water.

JIANG NAN rushes forward and grabs Xiao Hui's arm, his face twisted with rage.

JIANG NAN
You made sure it wasn't.

XIAO HUI
(panicked)
Jiang Nan, you're drunk--

JIANG NAN
Xu Wanru. Remember her? Or don't you read the gossip pages?

FLASHBACK: XU WANRU in a wedding gown, laughing with her GROOM.

JIANG NAN
(agitated)
She would've had my kid. The Xu empire would've been mine.

JIANG NAN collapses onto the sofa, exhausted. A bottle falls to the floor.

JIANG NAN
(crying out)
Twenty years... you owe me twenty years!

XIAO HUI sits down beside him and gently touches his hand.

XIAO HUI
You think Miss Xu loved you? Or just the idea of a husband her father couldn't control?

JIANG NAN
(lying on the sofa, drunk)
What's wrong with being a live-in son-in-law? Being a Xu dog beats being a Jiang son.

JIANG NAN suddenly pushes XIAO HUI away. She falls to the floor.

XIAO HUI
(heartbroken)
I sold my wedding ring for your first debt. Canceled my surgery for your phone. And you think I kept you from paradise?

JIANG NAN
Mom... you're my mother. You chose to have me. That means you owe me everything.

JIANG NAN's gaze drops to the bankbook in her hand. His eyes light up and he reaches to grab it.

JIANG NAN
What's that?

XIAO HUI
Let go!

They struggle over the bankbook. JIANG NAN pushes XIAO HUI down.

JIANG NAN opens the bankbook and sees the amount.

JIANG NAN
(delighted)
One hundred thousand... hoarding it from your own son?

XIAO HUI gets up and grabs his arm.

XIAO HUI
It was for your debts! To save you!

JIANG NAN's gaze falls on the jade bracelet on XIAO HUI's wrist.

JIANG NAN
And that bracelet. The apartment. Everything.

XIAO HUI
(crying out)
Your father gave me this!

JIANG NAN
(sneering)
So that's it. You're collecting for your new family.

XIAO HUI
(in disbelief)
Jiang Shan raised you like his own--

JIANG NAN
Give it to me!

XIAO HUI
Let go!

JIANG NAN yanks hard. XIAO HUI is violently pushed away, her head hitting the corner of the sofa. She falls to the ground, motionless. JIANG NAN grabs the items and runs out without looking back.
continuation:
INT. APARTMENT HALLWAY - NIGHT

JIANG NAN rushes out and collides with a well-dressed man, JIANG SHAN.

JIANG SHAN
Jiang Nan?

JIANG NAN
(roaring)
Get out of my way!

JIANG NAN pushes JIANG SHAN aside and runs off. JIANG SHAN frowns, sensing something is wrong, and hurries into the apartment.

INT. APARTMENT - NIGHT

JIANG SHAN rushes inside and sees XIAO HUI lying in a pool of blood.

JIANG SHAN
(horrified)
Xiao Hui!

JIANG SHAN kneels down and holds XIAO HUI in his arms.

JIANG SHAN
Xiao Hui!

XIAO HUI
(barely breathing)
The bracelet... it's yours.

JIANG SHAN
(choking up)
Stay with me. We'll fix everything.

XIAO HUI
(using her last bit of strength)
Next time... I won't choose wrong.

XIAO HUI's hand falls limp, losing all signs of life.

JIANG SHAN
(devastated)
Xiao Hui! Xiao Hui, don't leave me! Xiao Hui!

INT. BEDROOM - DAY

XIAO HUI's eyes snap open. She finds herself lying in bed, completely unharmed. She sits up in shock, looking around.

JIANG NAN (O.S.)
Mom! This is the last time I'm calling you that! If you don't agree to let me be with Wan Ru, I'm cutting ties with you!

XIAO HUI hears her son's voice, her eyes filled with shock and disbelief.

XIAO HUI
(internal monologue)
I... I've been reborn.

title: Youth Must Not Be Wasted
description: A mother struggles to support her son's education after he is accepted into a prestigious university, only to face unexpected challenges from her community.
context:
Title: Youth Must Not Be Wasted
Description: A mother struggles to support her son's education after he is accepted into a prestigious university, only to face unexpected challenges from her community.

INT. COURTYARD - DAY

ZHANG YUMEI, a woman with graying hair, sits weaving bamboo baskets. She coughs into her sleeve, trying to muffle the sound.

XIAO SHENG (O.S.)
Mom! Mom!

XIAO SHENG runs into the courtyard, breathless, clutching an envelope.

XIAO SHENG
Tsinghua! The physics department!

ZHANG YUMEI takes the letter, tears streaming down her face.

ZHANG YUMEI
Your father would be so proud.

She tries to stand, but wobbles. XIAO SHENG steadies her.

XIAO SHENG
You're pale.

ZHANG YUMEI
(waving her hand)
Just tired. I must finish these. For your tuition.

She sees his worry and softens.

ZHANG YUMEI
How much is it?

XIAO SHENG watches her face as she unfolds the fee schedule.

CLOSE-UP: The letter reads "Tuition & Housing: 8,500 yuan/semester."
continuation:
ZHANG YUMEI
(shocked)
Eight thousand...?

ZHANG YUMEI's face changes, showing great anxiety.

ZHANG YUMEI
(talking to herself, anxiously)
Only two thousand in the box... Short by so much...

She furrows her brow, then remembers.

ZHANG YUMEI
That's right. The committee owes me. For the festival baskets. I'll collect today.

She turns toward the house just as VILLAGE CHIEF and his WIFE walk in.

VILLAGE CHIEF
(smiling)
Yumei! Good news travels fast.

VILLAGE CHIEF
We heard about Tsinghua. My nephew also got into Jiangbei University. We're holding a joint graduation banquet. How much will your family donate to celebrate?

ZHANG YUMEI freezes, the letter feeling heavy in her hands.

ZHANG YUMEI
(stunned)
Donate?

VILLAGE CHIEF'S WIFE
Everyone contributes what they can. But we will announce all the families who support our village scholars.

ZHANG YUMEI looks at her son, then at the chief. Her face shows a mixture of pride and panic.

ZHANG YUMEI
For the community. Of course.

title: Parents' Long-Awaited Happiness
description: A wealthy man returns after twenty years to fulfill his promise to his wife, but their son opposes the reunion, fearing the loss of a bigger fortune.
context:
Title: Parents' Long-Awaited Happiness
Description: A wealthy man returns after twenty years to fulfill his promise to his wife, but their son opposes the reunion, fearing the loss of a bigger fortune.

INT. COUNTRY ROAD - NIGHT

WANG QIUMING, dressed in a white suit, walks slowly down a country road surrounded by a group of BODYGUARDS in black. His expression is solemn and thoughtful.

WANG QIUMING (V.O.)
Life's greatest regret is when you can finally afford the diamond ring, the person who was willing to marry you in the rain is already gone.

INT. COUNTRY ROAD - NIGHT (FLASHBACK)

Heavy rain pours down. Young WANG QIUMING, wearing a soaked suit, pushes a tricycle. Young LIN QIUXIA, wearing a white dress, happily helps him push the cart. They laugh together in the rain.

LIN QIUXIA
(laughing)
A groom in a suit pushing a tricycle—you're the only one in the world I recognize!

WANG QIUMING
(shouting loudly)
When I buy the warehouse at the east end of the village, I'll sew you a wedding dress made of gold and silver thread!

LIN QIUXIA
Okay!

END FLASHBACK.

WANG QIUMING stands in place, looking at the tricycle before him, lost in memories.

WANG QIUMING
Back then I vowed to make something of myself, to give you a grand wedding. But when I returned to this homeland, a flood had submerged my hometown, tearing my family apart.

WANG QIUMING's eyes redden with emotion.

WANG QIUMING
(choking up)
Qiuxia, where are you?

Assistant WANG HUI rushes down from the car.

WANG HUI
Chairman! We found her! Chairman, I found your wife! Number 37 Wutong Alley.

WANG QIUMING
(urgently)
Are you certain it's her?

WANG HUI
Your wife has been going to the dock every day waiting for your return. She's been waiting for twenty years.

WANG QIUMING
(excitedly)
Prepare ninety-nine dragon and phoenix chests, plus a betrothal gift of one billion yuan, and deliver them to her tonight!

WANG HUI
Sir, the board—should we confirm the identity first?

WANG QIUMING
(firmly)
I know my Qiuxia. Tonight, we make this right.
continuation:
INT. OFFICE - DAY

ZHOU LING runs excitedly into the office. XIAO CHEN is working at his desk, rubbing his temples.

ZHOU LING
Honey! The chairman's assistant called—they found your mother a husband!

XIAO CHEN
(looking up, wary)
What are you talking about?

ZHOU LING
(slaps a red envelope on the desk)
Longteng Group. One billion yuan betrothal. They're sending the chests tomorrow.

XIAO CHEN
(stunned, opening the envelope)
This can't be real. Mom never said—

ZHOU LING
She wanted to surprise you! And think—no children, that whole empire... 

XIAO CHEN
(quietly)
Mom waited twenty years. This isn't about money.

ZHOU LING
(leaning close)
Isn't it? You've been paying her rent. Now she can finally help you. This is your inheritance, Xiao Chen. Yours.

XIAO CHEN stares at the document, conflicted.

XIAO CHEN
I need to hear her say it.

He picks up his phone.

INT. LIN QIUXIA'S HOUSE - DAY

LIN QIUXIA is cleaning the house. Her phone on the table rings. She walks over and answers it.

LIN QIUXIA
(gently)
Hello?

XIAO CHEN (O.S.)
Mom. The lawyer's papers... is it true?

LIN QIUXIA
(a smile in her voice)
Yes. Your father came back. We're having the ceremony Saturday. Will you come?

INT. OFFICE - DAY

XIAO CHEN's face darkens. He looks at the betrothal amount, then at his wife's expectant face.

XIAO CHEN
(strained)
Mom, you don't know this man anymore. Twenty years...

LIN QIUXIA (O.S.)
He's the man who promised me a wedding dress of gold thread.

XIAO CHEN
(clenching the phone)
Mom, please—just delay the ceremony. A week. Let me check—protect you.

INT. LIN QIUXIA'S HOUSE - DAY

LIN QIUXIA's smile fades. She understands now.

LIN QIUXIA
(quietly)
You're afraid he'll take what you think is yours.

She hangs up the phone, standing still, the weight of twenty years settling on her shoulders.

title: The Bitter Stepmother Awakens and Goes Wild
description: A renowned actress known for tragic roles collapses at an awards ceremony and awakens in an ancient world, inhabiting the body of a mistreated daughter who was just murdered.
context:
Title: The Bitter Stepmother Awakens and Goes Wild
Description: A renowned actress known for tragic roles collapses at an awards ceremony and awakens in an ancient world, inhabiting the body of a mistreated daughter who was just murdered.

INT. AWARDS CEREMONY RED CARPET - NIGHT

The SOUND of camera shutters click like machine gun fire. Fans SCREAM from behind velvet ropes.

RUAN QINGXUAN, mid-30s, glides down the red carpet in a sapphire gown that catches every flash. She clutches two golden trophies, her face a mask of practiced serenity. BODYGUARDS flank her, creating a bubble of space.

HOST (V.O.)
This just in—Ruan Qingxuan sweeps Best Actress and the prestigious Tearjerker Award for her devastating performance in "The Abandoned Wife."

Reporters surge forward, microphones stabbing the air like spears.

YOUNG REPORTER
Miss Ruan! Your thoughts on the double win?

Ruan stops. Turns. The cameras love her, but her eyes hold something else—exhaustion flickering beneath the perfection.

RUAN QINGXUAN
I'm grateful. Truly. But after twelve years of playing victims, I have to ask—why does everyone see tragedy when they look at me? I can be more. I want to be more.

She holds up the trophies.

RUAN QINGXUAN (CONT'D)
So this is my last time holding these. No more tears. No more suffering. I'm done.

The reporters explode with questions. Ruan takes one step, then—

Her face drains of color. She grips her head, a look of pure terror crossing her features.

RUAN QINGXUAN
No—not again—

She collapses. The trophies CLATTER on marble. Her head smacks the red carpet.

CHAOS erupts. Bodyguards rush in. Reporters press closer.

YOUNG REPORTER
Someone call a doctor! Miss Ruan! Miss Ruan!
continuation:
INT. MOURNING HALL - DAY

Silence. Thick incense chokes the air.

Ruan Qingxuan lies on a wooden funeral bed, drapin white burial silks. Her eyes snap open.

MEMORIES slam into her—fragmented, violent, not her own:
- A newborn's cry, a father's disappointed scowl
- Small hands scrubbing clothes in freezing water
- A shove from behind, the shock of icy water filling lungs
- A scroll being read: "By imperial decree..."
- Ruan Yuyao's sneering face: "You'll never be more than a concubine's daughter..."

Ruan's chest heaves. She focuses on a figure across the room.

NINGSHUANG, a teenage maid, kneels before RUAN YUYAO, early 20s, who wears silk robes that scream privilege.

RUAN YUYAO
You dare accuse me? Your precious Second Miss slipped and fell. Her own clumsiness killed her.

NINGSHUANG
I saw you push her! For that marriage decree—you wanted—

Ruan Yuyao's fan snaps shut.

RUAN YUYAO
Guards.

Two burly MAIDS seize Ningshuang, forcing her to her knees.

NINGSHUANG
(struggling)
What are you—

RUAN YUYAO
Since you're so devoted, go serve her in hell.

Ningshuang's eyes lock onto Ruan's body, tears streaming.

NINGSHUANG
Second Miss! I'll find you in the next life!

Ruan Yuyao raises a heavy wooden club, her face a mask of genteel cruelty.

RUAN YUYAO
Such touching loyalty. Let's make it quick.

Ruan Yuyao swings—

Ruan Qingxuan sits bolt upright, the silk shroud sliding off.

RUAN QINGXUAN
Seriously? Can't a corpse get some fucking sleep around here?

The club freezes mid-air. Ningshuang's mouth falls open. A tiny vase drops from someone's hand, SHATTERING.

RUAN YUYAO
